title: MCP semantic search tool (mempalace_search equivalent)
description:
Goal: implement the search tool -- the highest-value read path, used constantly throughout this session for bug-family-search, prior-art lookup, and cold-start context recovery. Signature mirrors the existing mempalace_search tool: query text, optional wing/room filters, optional limit. Implementation: embed the query text (all-MiniLM-L6-v2), run SELECT id, wing, room, title, text, VEC_DISTANCE(embedding, string_to_vector(:q)) AS dist FROM drawers [WHERE wing=? [AND room=?]] ORDER BY dist ASC LIMIT :limit -- the WHERE-scoped form should hit the (wing,room) index added in loom-40ec.7's schema mitigation, keeping per-project search fast regardless of aggregate corpus size (per drawer_loom_decisions_521e654693797b4f169b4cbd's D8 sub-second bar + the loom-40ec.7 finding). Return drawer_id + title + a relevance-ranked snippet, matching mempalace_search's existing result shape closely enough that downstream skill prompts (session-startup, bug-family-researcher, etc.) don't need rewriting.

RED: seed >=20 drawers across 2 different wings; mcp_search('some real phrase from one seeded drawer', wing=<that wing>) returns that drawer in the top results; mcp_search(same query, wing=<other wing>) does NOT return it (wing scoping actually filters, doesn't just re-rank). Also assert an unscoped mcp_search(query) still returns results (cross-project search still works, just scans everything per the ADEQUATE-at-sub-second verdict).

Files: memory-server/mcp_server/tools/search.py, memory-server/tests/test_mcp_search.py
Depends on: loom-40ec.4.1 (needs the drawers table + add_drawer's embedding pipeline in place)
close_reason: memsrv_search implemented: wing/room-scoped VEC_DISTANCE search using the (wing,room) index (loom-40ec.7 mitigation). 6 new tests confirm scoping actually filters (not just re-ranks) and unscoped cross-project search still works. Full suite 18/18, independently re-verified, ruff clean. Merged to main at commit 5bd1738 (merge of db3c231).

title: MCP server scaffold + core drawer CRUD (get/add/update/list_drawer)
description:
Goal: stand up the actual MCP server process for loom's new memory server, backed by the production schema in memory-server/schema.sql (already built, loom-40ec.3) and the running dolt sql-server (memory-server/scripts/start-server.sh). Implement the FOUNDATIONAL tool surface first:
- mcp_get_drawer(drawer_id) -> full content + metadata
- mcp_add_drawer(wing, room, title, content, source_file?) -> embeds content (all-MiniLM-L6-v2, matching the schema's VECTOR(384) column and SPIKE-1/loom-40ec.3/.7's embedder choice), inserts into drawers via string_to_vector(), returns drawer_id
- mcp_update_drawer(drawer_id, content) -> re-embeds + replaces the row (full-body-replace per D6's locked concurrency model -- wrap in the optimistic-retry-on-OCC-abort pattern D6 specifies: on a Dolt OCC conflict, refetch + reapply once, else surface the conflict to the caller)
- mcp_list_drawers(wing?, room?, limit=20, offset=0) -> paginated listing with content previews (mirrors the existing MemPalace tool's shape so downstream skills/recipes that already call mempalace_list_drawers need minimal adaptation)

Use the official MCP Python SDK (the 'mcp' package, FastMCP-style server) for the protocol layer, exposed over stdio (matching how Claude Code's MCP client connects to local servers). Naming: prefix tools distinctly from the existing 'mempalace_*' tools during development/testing (e.g. 'memsrv_get_drawer') to avoid collision while both servers may be configured simultaneously during the loom-40ec.6 cutover; the cutover bead handles final naming/config-swap.

RED: mcp_add_drawer('loom', 'decisions', 'test title', 'test body') returns a drawer_id; mcp_get_drawer(that_id) returns the same title/body; mcp_update_drawer(that_id, 'new body') then mcp_get_drawer(that_id) reflects the new body; mcp_list_drawers(wing='loom') includes that drawer_id in its results.

Files: memory-server/mcp_server/ (new package -- __init__.py, server.py, tools/drawers.py), memory-server/tests/test_mcp_drawers.py, memory-server/requirements.txt (add the mcp SDK dependency)
close_reason: FastMCP server (mcp==1.28.1, stdio) exposing memsrv_get_drawer/add_drawer/update_drawer/list_drawers over the production Dolt schema. Implements D6's optimistic-retry concurrency (drawer_loom_decisions_521e654693797b4f169b4cbd) -- discovered and correctly handled Dolt's REPEATABLE-READ silently allowing same-row concurrent writes; explicit SERIALIZABLE upgrade surfaces the conflict for retry. 12/12 tests pass (8 new, 4 existing), independently re-verified + ruff clean. Merged to main.

title: Investigate Dolt vector-index scaling — ORDER BY VEC_DISTANCE does full table scan, not index-assisted ANN
description:
Discovered 2026-07-09 during loom-40ec.3 (production schema + dolt sql-server bring-up): dolt 2.1.10's CREATE VECTOR INDEX does NOT get used by the query pattern SELECT ... ORDER BY VEC_DISTANCE(embedding, :q) LIMIT k — verified via EXPLAIN FORMAT=TREE, which showed a TopN over a full table scan computing VEC_DISTANCE for every row, not an index-assisted nearest-neighbor lookup. Consequence: latency scales with corpus size rather than staying flat. Measured: 38 rows -> p95=3.45-4.05ms (matches SPIKE-1's regression floor, comfortably under the 10ms bar); 200 rows -> p95=11.9ms (BREACHES the 10ms bar). This calls into question how well D5 (Dolt sql-server locked as substrate, drawer_loom_decisions_521e654693797b4f169b4cbd) holds at loom's REAL eventual corpus scale (188 decisions drawers today, likely growing to thousands across all wings/rooms/projects once the shared server aggregates multiple projects) -- SPIKE-1 itself only benchmarked at a 38-row MVP sample (see loom-uk6m, the already-filed optional full-corpus re-run bead) and never surfaced this table-scan behavior since it never tested past ~38 rows.

Investigate before/during loom-40ec.4 (MCP server build): (1) does dolt 2.1.10 support any index hint / query rewrite to force ANN-pruned lookup via the vector index rather than a full scan (check dolt docs/changelog, dolt#579 and related vector-index issues); (2) does a newer dolt release fix this (vector indexing was noted in the original design research as 'beta/perf-disclaimed through mid-2026'); (3) if no fix is available, model realistic latency at loom's actual multi-project corpus scale (thousands of rows) and decide whether sub-second (rather than sub-10ms) retrieval latency is acceptable for loom's actual usage pattern (interactive MCP tool calls during a Claude Code session) -- it very likely is fine even at higher latency, but this should be an explicit judgment call, not a silent assumption inherited from SPIKE-1's small-sample numbers. (4) consider a partitioning strategy (e.g. per-wing/per-room tables, or an ANN library alternative) only if (1)-(3) show a real problem at expected scale.

Files: memory-server/schema.sql, memory-server/tests/test_memory_server.py (extend to test at a larger corpus size), possibly a new memory-server/docs/vector-index-scaling.md note
RED: at a corpus size matching loom's REALISTIC aggregate multi-project scale (start with an order-of-magnitude estimate, e.g. 2000-5000 rows), p95 query latency is measured and an explicit ADEQUATE/NOT-ADEQUATE-at-this-latency judgment is recorded (not silently assumed) — this is a decision-recording task as much as a technical investigation.
close_reason: Re-measured at real scale using 5,772 real MemPalace drawers: p95=309-318ms, extrapolated p95=1.2s at loom's full eventual ~24,401-row non-sessions corpus. EXPLAIN confirms full table scan at both scales (known open Dolt Beta limitation, dolthub/dolt#8659). Verdict: ADEQUATE under new D8 (sub-second bar, not the 10ms bar SPIKE-1's tiny sample implied). Added (wing,room) index to schema.sql as a low-risk mitigation. Full findings folded into drawer_loom_decisions_521e654693797b4f169b4cbd. Merged to main.

title: Cutover: retire MemPalace, resolve loom-p01j
description:
Goal: point loom's (and any other loom-managed project's) MCP config at the new Dolt-backed memory server, decommission the old MemPalace MCP + its auto-mine hooks entirely (this resolves loom-p01j's open DECIDE: keep-disabled/fix-upstream/re-enable question — cutover makes it moot, the hooks get removed rather than re-enabled), verify all mempalace_* tool calls round-trip correctly against the new server, and update install.sh + docs that reference MemPalace. Close loom-p01j as part of this task's D-phase capture.

Files: TBD (install.sh, docs/ references to MemPalace, .claude/settings.json MCP config)

title: Migration script: MemPalace to Dolt
description:
Goal: export the existing MemPalace corpus (~791k drawers across all wings) into the new Dolt schema. Note: the sessions wing ballooned to ~750k-790k garbage drawers due to the runaway auto-mine bug documented in loom-p01j (a stop-hook respawned 'mempalace mine --wing sessions' on every session-save, each grabbing the fcntl single-writer flock). Must decide + implement prune-vs-carry for that garbage — recommend pruning drawers created by the runaway auto-mine and keeping pre-bug session data; cross-reference loom-p01j's timeline for the bug's onset to draw the cutoff. Re-embed with the SAME embedder used in the schema/SPIKE-1 tasks (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) for consistency with the locked D5 benchmark.

Files: TBD (depends on where the MCP server task lands its code)

title: Production schema + dolt sql-server bring-up for loom memory server
description:
Goal: harden the SPIKE-1 scratch schema (~/loom-spike1-benchmark/doltdb) into a production-ready Dolt database. Extend drawers(id, wing, room, title, text, embedding VECTOR(384) NOT NULL) with metadata columns mirroring MemPalace's existing drawer shape (filed_at, source_file, chunk_index, parent_drawer_id, added_by) + CREATE VECTOR INDEX. Add wings/rooms reference structure. Configure dolt sql-server for production use (tuned max_connections, auth, persistent data dir — NOT the scratch ~/loom-spike1-benchmark path).

RED: A dolt sql-server instance boots against the production schema; INSERT INTO drawers (...) VALUES (...) with a 384-dim embedding succeeds; SELECT ... ORDER BY VEC_DISTANCE(embedding, :q) LIMIT 5 returns results with p95 latency <10ms (SPIKE-1 measured p95=3.94ms as the regression floor — see run_benchmark_server.py in ~/loom-spike1-benchmark/ for the harness to adapt).

Files: TBD once implementation location is chosen (likely a new top-level directory, e.g. loom-memory-server/ or similar)
close_reason: memory-server/ subdirectory built with production drawers+kg_triples schema, self-contained dolt install, start-server.sh bring-up, and RED-spec pytest suite (4/4 GREEN, p95=3.45ms independently re-verified against SPIKE-1's 3.94ms floor at matching 38-row corpus scale). Nested project-constitution.md carve-out confirmed working (Python/pip shadows loom's bash-only top-level constitution). Merged to main.

title: Build MCP server exposing mempalace_* tool surface on Dolt
description: Goal: implement an MCP server exposing the same tool surface as the existing MemPalace MCP (mempalace_search, mempalace_get_drawer, mempalace_update_drawer, mempalace_kg_query, mempalace_kg_add, mempalace_list_drawers, mempalace_diary_write/read, etc.) backed by the Dolt schema from the schema-bring-up task, using the isolation model locked by the C3 task and the KG disposition locked by the C5 task. This is the largest task in the epic — expect to sub-break-down into per-tool implementation + embedding pipeline + auth once started; do not treat this bead as atomic, split it further at claim time.
notes: Split 2026-07-09 into loom-40ec.4.1-.4.5 (see children). Scoping principle: build an MVP covering the mempalace_* tools actually exercised this session (search, get/add/update/list_drawer, kg_query/kg_add, diary_write/read, status/kg_stats) before the long tail (tunnels, hallways, tags, deletion, sync/checkpoint, aaak_spec, taxonomy, mine). This bead itself becomes a lightweight tracking parent; the real work happens in its children.
close_reason: MVP tool surface complete: .4.1 (scaffold+drawer CRUD), .4.2 (search), .4.3 (KG tools), .4.4 (diary+status) all closed and merged, full suite 36/36 passing. .4.5 (long-tail: tunnels/hallways/tags/deletion/admin) remains open as deliberately deferred follow-up work -- not blocking loom-40ec.5 (migration) or loom-40ec.6 (cutover), which only need the core CRUD/search/KG/diary surface this MVP delivers. See drawer_loom_decisions_521e654693797b4f169b4cbd for the full epic context.

title: Resolve C3 — per-client-branch isolation model for shared Dolt
description:
Design-shaped task, no code. Goal: decide how concurrent loom sessions avoid stomping on a shared Dolt sql-server when writing decision drawers/KG facts — the default single 'main' branch lets sessions write directly and collide. Options to evaluate: (a) per-session/per-agent Dolt branch + merge-at-commit (git-native, mirrors D2's concurrency-safe requirement, matches loom's own bd-state merge-driver precedent from loom-4um); (b) optimistic row-level locking via CAS (not yet tested by SPIKE-1); (c) hybrid — per-repo branch + periodic merge into main. Starting evidence: SPIKE-1 (loom-zu91) already proved the SINGLE shared-branch/single-writer model has no reader lockout under concurrent writes (baseline 2.88ms mean read latency, 7.2ms under a concurrent bulk-writer, p95 latency 3.94ms) — so the open question is really whether per-session branching is even necessary for WRITE-write conflicts, not read-write. Output: locked decision + rationale written into drawer_loom_decisions_521e654693797b4f169b4cbd (flip [C3]).

Files: none (MemPalace drawer update only)
AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE: needs-decision (design judgment on isolation model)
notes: RESOLVED 2026-07-09: D6 — no branch-per-session, no custom CAS. Rely on Dolt's native SQL transaction semantics on a single shared branch (dolt branch/merge is a git-CLI batch-workflow concept, orthogonal to live sql-server access). Two clients writing different rows never conflict; same-row concurrent writes are caught by Dolt's OCC at commit time (aborts the loser). loom's write pattern (session updates drawers it owns) doesn't justify branch-per-session complexity. Practical mechanism for loom-40ec.4: wrap update_drawer in a thin optimistic-retry on OCC-abort. Full rationale folded into drawer_loom_decisions_521e654693797b4f169b4cbd (D6).
close_reason: C3 resolved as D6 — no branch-per-session; Dolt native transaction semantics + optimistic-retry wrapper suffices. See drawer_loom_decisions_521e654693797b4f169b4cbd.

title: Shared Dolt memory server for loom (replace MemPalace)
description: Implementation epic for the design cycle locked in drawer_loom_decisions_521e654693797b4f169b4cbd (soundness green as of loom-zu91/SPIKE-1, 2026-07-09). MemPalace is untenable for loom (corruption + single-writer lockout from a runaway auto-mine hook, ~750k-790k garbage drawers in the sessions wing — see loom-p01j). Locked decisions: D1 memory must be shared across all repos (networked, not embedded/per-repo); D2 must be concurrent-safe (no reader lockout, proven via SPIKE-1: p95=3.94ms latency, 2.5x slowdown under concurrent writes, zero lockout); D3 architecture = shared server-backed store; D4 per-capture LLM cost is not a decision axis; D5 substrate = Dolt sql-server (locked over Hindsight per SPIKE-1: Recall@10=0.81, MRR=0.52 on loom's own decision-drawer corpus). Open implementation questions carried forward as child tasks: C3 per-client isolation model, C4 migration plan, C5 KG disposition. Sibling record: loom-p01j (MemPalace-untenability + exploration/design-doc promotion, currently unblocked).

title: SPIKE-1: benchmark Dolt-vector (+ Hindsight if marginal) on loom's OWN decision-drawer corpus — resolve C1/C2, lock or flip D5
description:
Gating experiment for the design cycle 'shared networked memory server for loom' (design-doc drawer_loom_decisions_521e654693797b4f169b4cbd). Resolves open markers [C1] Dolt-vector adequacy + [C2] retrieval quality unmeasured. Run in a FRESH session on the PC (needs software installs + clean context).

STAGED-FIRST (MVP resolves the DECISIVE blocker C1 alone; only escalate to the full Hindsight head-to-head if Dolt looks marginal):

CORPUS: loom's own decision drawers. Export loom/decisions room from MemPalace (mempalace_list_drawers wing=loom room=decisions, ~190 drawers) to JSONL {drawer_id, title/source_file, text, lineage_links}.

GROUND TRUTH (the crux — no manual labeling needed): use loom's OWN link structure as relevance labels. For each drawer, query = its title/topic; relevant-set = the drawers named in its '## Lineage' section + KG edges (grounded_in / sibling_of / follows / informs_design_of / supersedes / fixes_sibling_of) from mempalace_kg_query. Metric = Recall@k (k=5,10) + MRR that the vector search surfaces the lineage-linked siblings. Also add a handful of real bug-family-search queries (a new bead's title+symptom → which prior drawers should surface).

DOLT SIDE (MVP): install dolt >=1.47.0; dolt sql-server (max_connections>1); table drawers(id, wing, room, text, embedding VECTOR); CREATE VECTOR INDEX; embed corpus with all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (MATCH MemPalace's embedder for apples-to-apples — sentence-transformers) OR whatever MemPalace 3.5.0 uses; query via ORDER BY VEC_DISTANCE(embedding, :q) LIMIT k. MEASURE: Recall@k, MRR, p50/p95 query latency at loom's doc count. ALSO test the concurrency claim: run a background writer (bulk insert) while timing interactive reads — confirm no reader lockout (the whole point vs MemPalace). ALSO note ops footprint (install/run/backup) hands-on [C4].

HINDSIGHT SIDE (only if Dolt marginal): stand up a CLEAN Hindsight instance (NOT liza's — separate bank/db), ingest the same corpus (POST /background, document_id=drawer_id), run the same queries via recall, map recalled facts back to source document_id, score the same Recall@k/MRR. Compare.

EXIT: (a) Dolt-vector surfaces lineage siblings at good Recall@k + acceptable latency + no reader lockout -> C1/C2 resolved, LOCK D5=Dolt, advance design-doc soundness toward green. (b) Dolt-vector marginal/too-slow -> run the Hindsight arm, compare, decide D5 on the numbers (+ Dolt FTS-only-until-GA fallback). Either way: write results into the design-doc drawer, flip the relevant [CLARIFICATION] markers, update soundness.

Files: ~/loom-spike1-benchmark/ (scratch harness + results; NOT loom source)
AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE: needs-attended fresh-session + software installs
notes: RUNBOOK (self-contained, for a fresh PC session): ~/loom-mempalace-promotion/SPIKE-1-START-HERE.md. Design-doc=drawer_loom_decisions_521e654693797b4f169b4cbd; exploration(promoted)=drawer_loom_decisions_479c330ce7da70f55d4b0a2b; research reports in ~/loom-mempalace-promotion/.
close_reason: SPIKE-1 benchmark complete: Dolt-vector Recall@10=0.81, p95 latency=3.94ms, no reader lockout under concurrent write load (38/188-drawer MVP sample, condensed text). D5=Dolt LOCKED per user decision; drawer_loom_decisions_521e654693797b4f169b4cbd updated to soundness=green, C1/C2 resolved. Follow-up loom-uk6m filed (optional full-corpus re-run).

title: File the shared-memory-server exploration→design-cycle promotion into MemPalace (blocked by MemPalace auto-mine)
description:
PROMOTION STAGED ON DISK, could not file to MemPalace live — the palace is unwritable because MemPalace's own plugin save-hook (~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/mempalace/hooks/mempal_save_hook.sh) respawns 'mempalace mine --wing sessions' on frequent session-save events; each mine grabs the fcntl single-writer flock and re-latches every MCP client read-only faster than a write can land. Also: SIGTERM'd mines leave a STALE mine_palace_*.lock that keeps blocking until removed, and mempalace_reconnect does NOT re-acquire the writer role (needs a fresh server started while the flock is free). sessions wing ballooned ~445k→~750k drawers from the auto-mine.

STAGED FILES (file verbatim when palace is writable):
- Exploration drawer: /tmp/claude-1000/-home-frank-repos-loom/60493e0f-7e49-4b77-b01f-36e7bec82fa3/scratchpad/exploration-hindsight-drawer.md  → file to loom/decisions, tag exploration, status=promoted.
- Design-doc drawer: /tmp/claude-1000/-home-frank-repos-loom/60493e0f-7e49-4b77-b01f-36e7bec82fa3/scratchpad/design-doc-shared-memory-server.md → file to loom/decisions (L2 design doc).
- Two deep-research reports: task outputs wf_ff946c15 (substrate survey) + wf_f443bbe1 (Hindsight-vs-Dolt head-to-head).
NOTE: /tmp scratchpad is session-scoped — copy these into a durable path (e.g. ~/repos/loom/.notes/) before the session ends.

DECISION (converged, design-ready): loom memory must be a SHARED + CONCURRENT + server-backed store (networked cross-project knowledge is a hard requirement; embedded/collapse-onto-Dolt is OUT). Hindsight ruled out as a total swap; shared Dolt sql-server LEADS on verbatim+trust+git-native, gated on a corpus-benchmark spike (SPIKE-1). Full head-to-head verdict in the design-doc.

TO UNBLOCK: disable the MemPalace save-hook auto-mine (mempal_save_hook.sh / session_end / precompact), kill any live 'mempalace mine', remove stale mine_palace_*.lock, then start a fresh MCP server (flock free) and file the two drawers + KG lineage.

Files: /tmp/claude-1000/-home-frank-repos-loom/60493e0f-7e49-4b77-b01f-36e7bec82fa3/scratchpad/exploration-hindsight-drawer.md, /tmp/claude-1000/-home-frank-repos-loom/60493e0f-7e49-4b77-b01f-36e7bec82fa3/scratchpad/design-doc-shared-memory-server.md
AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE: needs-attended MemPalace + plugin-hook change
notes: DECIDED 2026-07-09 (Frank): keep the MemPalace auto-mine hooks disabled permanently — no upstream fix needed. Loom is retiring MemPalace entirely (epic loom-40ec, Dolt-backed memory server), so the auto-mine bug is moot rather than something to fix. The 8 disabled hook copies (marked LOOM-DISABLED-AUTOMINE, .loom-bak backups) stay as-is until loom-40ec.6 (cutover) removes MemPalace + its hooks entirely — at that point this is a deletion, not a re-enable decision. Residual non-blocking item: (1) tag_drawer bug (drawer-not-found on fresh add) — non-critical, no action needed given the retirement plan.
close_reason: Promotion filed 2026-07-08 (design-doc + exploration drawers, 5 KG triples, liza tunnel). Interim DECIDE resolved 2026-07-09: keep auto-mine hooks disabled permanently, no upstream fix — MemPalace is being retired entirely via epic loom-40ec. Physical hook removal deferred to loom-40ec.6 (cutover), not a re-enable decision. Non-critical residual: tag_drawer bug on the exploration-status tag, no action needed given retirement.

title: mempalace hardening: single-instance lock + repair --from-sqlite (concurrent-session corruption class)
description:
[Moved from liza_base-zo2h on 2026-06-20 — mempalace tooling is stewarded in loom, so the hardening work lives here. Originating incident bead: liza_base-4mnn. Full diagnosis in the liza_base conversation 2026-06-20 + MemPalace liza_base wing (findings/decisions).]

Surfaced by liza_base-4mnn (2026-06-12 palace corruption). chromadb 1.5.9 corrupts the shared palace when two Claude sessions open it concurrently; Frank runs many concurrent sessions so this WILL recur. Asks (mempalace-project, may file upstream): (1) single-instance advisory/file lock on the palace dir so a 2nd PersistentClient refuses rather than corrupts; (2) 'mempalace repair --from-sqlite' that rebuilds by reading documents/metadata from chroma.sqlite3 + re-embedding, instead of the current cmd_repair which col.count()s the (crashing) collection first (cli.py:201); (3) promote /tmp/palace_recover.py into the package. Until shipped: keep exactly one mempalace.mcp_server alive (pgrep -af mempalace.mcp_server; kill extras).
notes: RESHAPED 2026-07-06: NOT a build bead — upstream (MemPalace/mempalace) already shipped all 3 asks (single-instance write-flock #1264 v3.3.4, repair --from-sqlite #1308 v3.5.0, quarantine_stale_hnsw #1000 v3.3.2). BUT Frank's pipx install is pinned to fork/feature/dnp-search-tag-filter @ 3.3.0, carrying 2 local-only commits (mempalace_tag_drawer e129f61 + tag_filter 69c612f) that are 1044 commits behind v3.5.0 and NOT upstream. So delivery = rebase those 2 commits onto v3.5.0 (worktree ~/repos/mempalace/.worktrees/iqwr-rebase, branch feature/tag-features-v3.5.0), reinstall pipx from reconciled branch (single-instance), verify corruption mechanisms. Divergence-heal follow-on = epic loom-yn38 (upstream both features). Full triage: drawer_loom_decisions_8c8e2b2b9d2c184117e6efaa. Worker add093c9 running rebase in bg.
close_reason: Delivered as verify+reconcile, NOT build. Upstream MemPalace/mempalace already shipped all 3 asks (write-flock #1264 v3.3.4, repair --mode from-sqlite #1308 v3.5.0, quarantine_stale_hnsw #1000 v3.3.2). Frank's install was pinned to fork branch @3.3.0 (predated all). Reconciled the 2 local-only features (tag_drawer, tag_filter) onto v3.5.0 in ~/repos/mempalace branch feature/tag-features-v3.5.0 (worker add093c9: fresh-additive re-apply, tests/test_mcp_server.py 250/250, full suite 3122 passed/0 failed). pipx reinstalled from that branch -> mempalace 3.5.0 live; verified repair --mode from-sqlite + mine_palace_lock + quarantine_stale_hnsw + both features present. Single-instance so no restart race; hardening active on next server spawn. Divergence-heal follow-on: epic loom-yn38.

title: Downstream-routed skills invoke loom helpers by repo-relative scripts/loom-* path → silently dark in downstream projects (class fix + gate)
description:
ROOT CAUSE (surfaced live in liza_base 2026-06-11, Frank-witnessed). loom helper scripts ARE installed globally (~/.claude/scripts/loom-X → symlink into the loom repo) and are project-agnostic. But several DOWNSTREAM-ROUTED skills/commands (symlinked into ~/.claude/{skills,commands} and run against the CURRENT project, cwd = downstream repo) invoke them by BARE REPO-RELATIVE path `scripts/loom-X`, which only resolves when cwd IS the loom repo. In any downstream loom-managed project the relative path misses → 'no such file or directory'. Because the call sites are nudge-not-block with 'if absent, emit nothing and continue' tolerance, the failure is SILENT: e.g. session-startup step 6a's fan-out detector never proposes a wave downstream — a whole loom capability goes dark, invisibly. liza_base only fanned out because the agent reconstructed the wave by hand.

The tell it is an oversight, not a choice: the SAME session-startup skill's mode-check at the top already uses the global path (`~/.claude/scripts/workflow-state mode`). Step 6a just didn't follow the established convention.

This is the concrete, reproducible instance of the downstream convention-drift structural problem (12:03 diary diagnosis) AND a gate-don't-advise/silent-degrade hazard (loom-wj26.1).

FIX (Frank chose 'class fix + gate', 2026-06-11). (1) Convert every downstream-routed invocation of a loom helper script from bare `scripts/loom-X` to the installed global path `~/.claude/scripts/loom-X` (matching the existing workflow-state/statusline convention + loom-fxad/loom-0ahj.2 $HOME/.claude primary). (2) Add a suite GATE (lib/tests/) that statically lints the downstream-routed skill/command set and FAILS listing any bare-path offender — parallel to loom-7f3 (--check-invocable) + loom-fxad (test exercises the installed path).

KNOWN OFFENDERS (gate's RED baseline): skills/session-startup/SKILL.md step 6a + commands/working-a-bead.md (loom-fanout-detect invocations). PROSE-REF siblings to reconcile: dispatch-middle, bead-lifecycle-shell (loom-stage-spend), and the loom-guest/loom-mine-history/loom-adopt command invocations. AMBIGUOUS (triage during gate run; may split to follow-up): audit-project's references mix 'loom's global script' vs 'the project's OWN scripts/ dir' semantics — needs per-line judgment + an allowlist mechanism for legit repo-relative cases.

Files: lib/tests/downstream-script-invocation.test.sh, skills/session-startup/SKILL.md, commands/working-a-bead.md, commands/loom-guest.md, skills/dispatch-middle/SKILL.md, skills/bead-lifecycle-shell/SKILL.md, skills/loom-mine-history/SKILL.md, commands/loom-mine-history.md, skills/loom-adopt/SKILL.md, skills/audit-project/SKILL.md, commands/audit-project.md

RED: INVARIANT: no downstream-routed skill/command (symlinked into ~/.claude/{skills,commands}, run against the current project) invokes a loom helper script by bare repo-relative scripts/loom-*; all such invocations use the installed ~/.claude/scripts/loom-* path. A new lib/tests gate greps the downstream-routed set and FAILS listing any bare-path offender (RED now: session-startup step 6a + working-a-bead); GREEN once all are converted and any legit repo-local exception is explicitly allowlisted.
close_reason: Converted 24 bare repo-relative scripts/loom-* invocations across 9 downstream-routed skills/commands to the installed ~/.claude/scripts/loom-* global path + added lib/tests/downstream-script-invocation.test.sh static-lint gate (4/4). Root cause empirically reproduced (relative path exit 127 vs global exit 0 in loom-managed projects tla-puzzles/mforth). Allowlists loom-docs-catalogue/loom-docs-gen (deliberately repo-scoped). Worker db89deb, merged 34bb095 --no-ff. Full suite 103/103 GREEN; /tmp smoke exit 0. mirrors_pattern_from loom-fxad; sibling_of loom-7f3.

title: loom-mine-history salience pass: claude -p steals survivors-loop stdin (missing </dev/null) — derails every large-repo mine
description:
ROOT CAUSE (rnxp investigation, 2026-06-09 night): the salience loop runs 'reply=$(claude -p "$prompt" ... 2>err)' INSIDE 'while IFS=$'\t' read ... done < "$survivors"' (lib/loom-mine-history.sh:253 in-loop, :681 redirect). Every command in the loop inherits the survivors fd as stdin; claude -p in print mode READS it. With a large survivors stream it slurps the remaining units into its reply (PROVEN: a reply explicitly discussed 'filler0002-filler0400') AND corrupts the loop's own read position. Per-unit verdicts are derailed -> the engine's salient-extraction fails -> 4/4 abort. THIS is the deeper blocker behind rnxp's 0/787, 0/353, and tonight's 4/4 — it survived loom-b10 (body truncation), loom-bzl (fence strip), loom-ug4p (abort-loud, which correctly caught it as a real failure). MASKED by capped runs: a 10-unit survivors file leaves only ~9 tiny remaining lines that don't derail the reply (capped-10 = 10/10, both fg+bg).

FIX: '</dev/null' on the salience claude -p call (line 253) + defensively on the --synthesize arc call (line 765). A shelled-out claude -p in this engine must never read inherited stdin.

VERIFIED: new RED->GREEN regression test lib/tests/loom-mine-history-stdin-isolation.test.sh (witness stub records non-empty stdin = leak; + structural </dev/null pin). RED before: LEAK 1464 bytes + loop truncated to 1/6 processed. GREEN after: empty stdin + 6/6 processed. Full suite 93/93. Empirical A/B (large survivors): engine-style reply discussed the whole filler stream; </dev/null reply = clean per-unit.

Lineage: blocks loom-rnxp (the re-mine could never succeed without this). Siblings: loom-b10, loom-bzl, loom-ug4p, loom-wzcn (transient-throttle hardening — orthogonal). Files: lib/loom-mine-history.sh, lib/tests/loom-mine-history-stdin-isolation.test.sh
RED: INVARIANT: the salience claude -p call receives EMPTY stdin (no survivors-stream leak); with N>=2 survivors the loop processes ALL N, not 1.
close_reason: Fixed </dev/null stdin-steal in salience loop. Merge 4837ab6 (impl 869a74e). RED->GREEN test loom-mine-history-stdin-isolation 4/4, suite 93/93. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_d01bb1882745179a408d39c0. Unblocks loom-rnxp.

title: Harden hook helper-sourcing to be symlink-robust (readlink -f); installed .git/hooks symlinks break BASH_SOURCE-relative ../lib fallback
description:
REGRESSION surfaced post-merge of loom-0ahj.2 (2026-06-08): the installed git hooks (.git/hooks/post-rewrite, .git/hooks/pre-push) are SYMLINKS into the repo hooks/*.sh. When git runs them, BASH_SOURCE[0] is the .git/hooks/ path, so the fallback '. $(dirname BASH_SOURCE)/../lib/loom-hook-helpers.sh' resolves to .git/lib/ (missing). The hooks only work because the PRIMARY source '. $HOME/.claude/lib/loom-hook-helpers.sh' is present — but that symlink only exists after install.sh runs. So merging a new lib helper + a hook that depends on it BREAKS the installed hooks ('loom_env_enabled: command not found' on post-rewrite line 53 + pre-push line 45) until install.sh is re-run. Immediate breakage was fixed THIS session by re-running install.sh; this bead makes it robust.

ROOT CAUSE: BASH_SOURCE-relative ../lib does not resolve through the install symlink. FIX: use readlink -f on BASH_SOURCE so dirname resolves to the repo's real hooks/ dir, making ../lib correct even when invoked via the .git/hooks symlink. Apply to the helper-source fallback in every hook that sources loom-hook-helpers.sh (the 13 refactored in loom-0ahj.2) AND to loom-0ahj.1's CANON_EXPORT line in hooks/post-rewrite.sh (line ~74: $(cd $(dirname BASH_SOURCE) && pwd)/../lib/bd-canonical-export.sh — same fragility, NO $HOME fallback at all, so it points at a missing .git/lib/bd-canonical-export.sh and silently degrades).

TEST GAP (why behavior-preservation missed it): loom-0ahj.2's tests ran hooks from the repo path (hooks/*.sh, where ../lib resolves). The INSTALLED symlink path (.git/hooks/) was never exercised. Add a RED test that invokes a representative hook THROUGH a .git/hooks-style symlink and asserts (a) no 'command not found' and (b) loom_env_enabled/json_get resolve.

Files: hooks/post-rewrite.sh, hooks/pre-push-mkdocs-strict.sh, the other ~11 hooks sourcing loom-hook-helpers.sh, lib/tests/hook-symlink-sourcing.test.sh
RED: INVARIANT: a hook invoked through a .git/hooks symlink (BASH_SOURCE in .git/hooks/) sources lib/loom-hook-helpers.sh successfully and loom_env_enabled is defined — WITHOUT requiring ~/.claude/lib to be populated.
Grounding: discovered-during loom-0ahj.2 post-merge push; closing drawer drawer_loom_decisions_34b92766cd9edfd06992ac54.
close_reason: Symlink-robust hook sourcing: readlink-f on BASH_SOURCE in 13 hooks + loom-0ahj.1 CANON_EXPORT. New hook-symlink-sourcing.test.sh (9/0, RED-first 2/7) exercises the installed .git/hooks symlink path with HOME empty. Full suite 71/71 green; installed post-rewrite+pre-push verified clean.

title: Move-1/D8: upgrade bd v1.0.2→v1.0.4 (export determinism) + memory-line canonicalizer backstop
description:
Goal: Kill the loom-n1sk export churn at its root. The upstream determinism fix (sort memory keys; beads #3474 + byte-stability regression test #4086) already shipped in bd v1.0.4; loom runs stale v1.0.2. Upgrade to v1.0.4 (PIN the tag, do NOT float @latest — some OPEN v1.0.4 server-mode regressions), smoke-test against loom's hooks (bd-merge-driver/post-rewrite/bd-worktree-preseed all shell out to bd). Add a thin loom-side canonicalizer (sort ONLY the 2 _type:memory lines, ~3 lines, verified byte-stable) at the bd-export call sites as a TEMPORARY remove-on-validation backstop + for downstream projects on uncontrolled bd. Subsumes loom-n1sk (its dirty-tree-aborts-merge + close-without-merge hazard both resolve once export is byte-stable).
Files: install.sh, scripts/bd-merge-driver.sh, hooks/post-rewrite.sh, lib/bd-canonical-export.sh, lib/tests/bd-canonical-export.test.sh
RED: INVARIANT: bd export is byte-stable across repeated runs on unchanged state (diff of two consecutive exports is empty).
Steps: 1. RED test asserting byte-stable export. 2. Upgrade bd (pinned) + canonicalizer at call sites. 3. Smoke-test hooks. Verify.
Acceptance: export byte-stable; full suite green; loom-n1sk churn gone. Grounding: design doc 14f08e6d D8; C1 research.
close_reason: 4-way parallel wave: byte-stable bd export (canonicalizer), measure-first stage-spend reader, model-tier policy nudge, history-mine arc filing. All merged --no-ff, 67/67 suite green.

title: Dirty .beads/issues.jsonl in main working tree blocks merges + enables close-without-merge during multi-worker sessions
description:
SYMPTOM: During parallel-worker dispatch + central integration, .beads/issues.jsonl in MAIN's working tree repeatedly goes dirty WITHOUT central having run a bd write. A dirty issues.jsonl makes 'git merge --no-ff <worker>' abort with 'Please commit your changes or stash them before you merge. Aborting' (working-tree-dirty pre-merge check, not a content conflict).

REAL FAILURE (overnight 2026-06-07, dispatch-v2 wave 2): the loom-k0s8 merge aborted on a dirty tree, but a chained command sequence meant the LATER close step STILL RAN. Result: loom-k0s8 was marked closed in dolt + committed while its /design-a-cycle skill/command/test were NOT on main. Caught + recovered manually (re-merged the code into the already-closed bead), but this is a silent correctness hazard: closed bead, unmerged code.

TWO PROBLEMS TO FIX:
(1) STOP THE SPURIOUS DIRTYING. issues.jsonl gets re-exported even when dolt state is unchanged (or only formatting/ordering differs), and parallel-worker bd activity + the worktree-preseed/post-rewrite hooks churn the shared file. Make export idempotent (no rewrite when content-equal), and/or apply the worktree export.git-add=false + info/exclude treatment to MAIN too, and/or only export-on-actual-dolt-change.
(2) MAKE INTEGRATION ROBUST. (a) a pre-merge guard that cleans/normalizes issues.jsonl (git checkout the file when the only diff is bd churn) so merges never abort on it; (b) DECOUPLE the close step from the merge so an aborted merge cannot be followed by a close — bead-lifecycle-shell / integration guidance must verify the merge actually landed (files present, merge commit exists) BEFORE closing, and must NOT chain merge and the close step in a single && sequence.

LINEAGE (same cluster): loom-4um (merge=bd-export driver), loom-x4m (bd-worktree-preseed), loom-yjo (post-rewrite re-export), loom-22h (worktree export-to-root). This is the main-side analogue of the worktree protections those added. RELATED: the bd-close-capture hook also false-positives on the literal two-word phrase in a --description (sibling of loom-9ng substring false-positive) — fold in or cross-ref.

Files: hooks/bd-worktree-preseed.sh, hooks/post-rewrite.sh, scripts/bd-merge-driver.sh, skills/bead-lifecycle-shell/SKILL.md, lib/tests/bd-merge-driver.test.sh

Surfaced during the dispatch-v2 overnight build; the more workers run in parallel, the more often it bites.

title: history-mine LLM salience pass non-functional live: missing prompt instruction + envelope/fence parse (0/787 drafts on e2e dogfood)
description:
P1 — the engine's flagship LLM salience+draft step (stage 4) does not work against real claude. Surfaced by the full-history e2e-api-tests dogfood (2026-06-03): 787 survivors -> 787 claude calls -> 0 drafts emitted ("emitted 0 draft(s)."). This is the "live claude reply shape assumed, not verified" risk the bn7.1/bn7.4 close drawers explicitly deferred to first dogfood; the stub tests passed because the stub emits bare {salient:...} with no envelope and needs no instruction (the costume-integration-test risk bn7.1 named).

TWO bugs in lib/loom-mine-history.sh stage 4:

1. MISSING PROMPT INSTRUCTION. source_text is just "Source type/Identifier/Title/Body/Files touched" — it never instructs the model to (a) judge salience per the trust gate (skip if the source states no rationale) or (b) emit the exact JSON schema {salient,verbatim,synthesis,decision}. Real claude returns prose/markdown, not the bare schema. Fix: prepend a real instruction stating the salience criteria + "respond with ONLY raw JSON, no markdown fence, schema {...}".

2. ENVELOPE + FENCE + ESCAPING PARSE. "claude -p --output-format json" wraps the reply as {"type":"result",...,"result":"<the model text>",...}. The model text itself is a json-fenced block with ESCAPED quotes and literal backslash-n newlines inside the .result string (e.g. result contains: json-fence + newline + {  \"salient\": true, ... } + json-fence). The engine's sed 's/.*"salient":[[:space:]]*\(true\|false\)/' cannot match through the backslash-escaped quotes (\"salient\") -> extracts empty -> treated not-salient -> no draft. Verified: the engine's exact sed on a real reply prints nothing.
   Fix options: (a) use --output-format text so claude prints just the result text (no envelope), then strip a leading/trailing json fence, then parse; (b) unwrap .result via python3 json.loads (lib is jq-free but python3 is available and robust), then strip the fence, then parse the inner JSON; (c) both. MUST handle the json fence the model adds even when asked for raw JSON.

REPRO: scripts/loom-mine-history --root <any-repo> --out /tmp/x ; observe "emitted 0 draft(s)" despite N gated. Single-call probe confirmed: a real claude reply puts {salient:true,...} inside .result as a fenced, escaped string the sed misses.

Files: lib/loom-mine-history.sh (prompt + parse), lib/tests/loom-mine-history.test.sh. The stub MUST be upgraded to emit a REALISTIC enveloped + fenced + escaped reply (not bare JSON) so the test actually exercises the parser — the bare-JSON stub is precisely why this shipped green. Consider a recorded real-reply fixture.

Model id claude-haiku-4-5 is VALID (rc=0; proper JSON returned once an instruction was added). Harvest + gate + wing (verbatim, post loom-kx2) + cost-preview all work; ONLY the LLM salience pass is broken. Blocks any real /loom-mine-history or /audit-project --mine-history use until fixed. bugfix-a-bead / systematic-debugging. Lineage: bn7.1 engine, bn7.4 entry (both deferred live-claude verification here); found by the same e2e dogfood that produced loom-kx2 + loom-mec.
close_reason: Fixed the history-mine LLM salience pass (0/787 dogfood → works live). 3 faults: prompt had no instruction/schema; --output-format json envelope escaped inner JSON defeating sed; field patterns missed space-after-colon. Fix: instructed prompt + --output-format text + fence-strip + space-tolerant patterns; stub upgraded to realistic enveloped/fenced reply. Engine 93/93, suite 54/0, LIVE-validated on e2e-api-tests (5 gated→1 salient draft). Merged 2d149a8. Unblocks real mining.

title: Fix broken docs link blocking Deploy docs CI (upstream-loom-label.md → check-loom-upstream)
description:
Deploy docs CI has been red for 3 days (since 2026-05-23, commit 72b425d). Root cause: docs/reference/upstream-loom-label.md lines 22 and 76 link to slash-commands/check-loom-upstream.md, but loom's slash-commands docs convention is two files only (index.md + all-commands.md via wildcard include-markdown). No per-command pages exist; the author wrote against a convention that isn't there. mkdocs --strict promotes the two broken-link WARNINGs to errors and aborts build.

Fix: strip markdown link wrapper on both occurrences, keep /check-loom-upstream as plain inline code. Matches sibling pattern — grep across docs/ shows /audit-project referenced inline 10+ times with no markdown link wrapper. Minimum-scope change, unblocks CI.

Precedent: loom-59w (2026-05-15) — same bug class, same fix shape (1-line link target correction on main, no worktree, no branch).
notes: Acceptance: mkdocs build --strict exits 0 locally; Deploy docs CI succeeds on next push. Sibling gap: loom-cya hook fires on bead-closure / preflight only, not on merge or push — captured in diary, motivates loom-kbo (pre-push gate, P2 OPEN).
close_reason: Shipped in commit d9a24a1. Deploy docs CI run 26483031966 succeeded. mkdocs --strict rc=0 locally and on CI. Two markdown link wrappers stripped from docs/reference/upstream-loom-label.md (lines 22, 76); /check-loom-upstream now rendered as plain inline code matching the sibling pattern used everywhere else in docs/.

title: feature: extend kg-relationship-extractor with Serena symbol-triple enrichment at D3
description:
GATED BY loom-gn5. If research greenlights, extend the kg-relationship-extractor agent definition (~/.claude/agents/kg-relationship-extractor.md, symlinked from loom/agents/) to dispatch Serena over the bead's touched files and propose symbol-level triples alongside its current workflow-narrative triples.

Integration shape: subagent at D3 enrichment phase. Touched-files scope comes from the commit diff. Serena memory subsystem stays disabled (MemPalace canonical). Subagent proposes triples for main agent to review and file via mempalace_kg_add (existing review-then-file pattern).

If research (loom-gn5) returns NO: supersede this bead at file-time review rather than implementing.

Why P1: top-priority because backlog acceleration depends on this landing once research validates.
design:
Integration point: ~/.claude/agents/kg-relationship-extractor.md (symlinked from loom/agents/kg-relationship-extractor.md).

The subagent today proposes 3-5 workflow-narrative triples at D3 based on the closing drawer's prose. Extension adds a symbol-triple phase BEFORE composing the proposal:

1. Read touched files from the bead's commit diff (git diff --name-only on the bead's merge commit)
2. Dispatch Serena tools per file: find_symbol for all top-level symbols, find_referencing_symbols for symbols mentioned in the drawer prose
3. Apply the selection rule from loom-gn5's findings (likely: only symbols mentioned in drawer prose, not the full symbol graph)
4. Compose proposed triples in MemPalace schema
5. Return both workflow-narrative AND symbol-level triples to the main agent for review

Composition with existing flow: main agent already reviews + files via mempalace_kg_add. No change to that interface.

Non-goals: do NOT enable Serena's .serena/memories/ subsystem; do NOT auto-import full symbol graph; do NOT fire outside D3 (defer pattern-pivot moments and bug-family-researcher enrichment to follow-up beads if value warrants).
close_reason: Superseded by loom-gn5 research verdict (drawer drawer_loom_decisions_c9de5e283972378e084a9602). Original framing (Serena symbol-triple enrichment at D3 via kg-relationship-extractor) was wrong: symbol triples duplicate drawer prose and violate the 5-triple cap. Cross-recipe analysis surfaced NO Serena adoption in loom anywhere. ast-grep evaluation tracked separately as loom-2pg (P3 deferred).

title: research: would Serena symbol triples substantively enrich MemPalace KG? — retro test on liza_base-98an
description:
Assess whether feeding Serena's symbol-level relationships into the kg-relationship-extractor at D3 would yield triples that subsequent sibling beads find useful. Retroactive run against liza_base-98an (mirrored 9uzm pattern). Touched files: liza_base/mcp_servers/world_server.py, liza_base/world/apartment_interface.py, tests/mcp_servers/test_world_server.py, tests/mcp_servers/test_world_server_descriptions.py.

Deliverable: findings drawer in loom/decisions that either greenlights or kills the D3-integration follow-up bead.

Why P1: this evaluation gates whether to absorb Serena into loom's workflow. Outcome shapes how 30-40%+ of currently-queued Python/Go/TS work gets handled across the loom-managed project set. Backlog acceleration depends on resolving this.
design:
Method:
(a) setup — 'uv tool install -p 3.13 serena-agent@latest --prerelease=allow', then 'serena init' against ~/repos/liza_base. Verify Serena's .serena/memories/ subsystem is DISABLED or namespaced before init (non-obvious risk: would fragment MemPalace's canonical tribal-knowledge boundary — exactly the failure mode MemPalace was built to prevent).
(b) extraction — for each of the four touched files, run find_symbol + find_referencing_symbols + symbol overview via Serena MCP.
(c) composition — render Serena's output as KG triples in MemPalace schema (subject --predicate--> object). Use existing relationship vocabulary where possible (defines, references, comprises, members, calls_into, imports). DRAFT ONLY — do not file via mempalace_kg_add yet.
(d) judgment — three criteria:
    1. Do the symbol triples encode information NOT already captured in the prose drawer (drawer_loom_decisions_88aacc394282d2afc139ce22 + 98an's D3 drawer)?
    2. Would these triples have helped liza_base-w84v (the 6-rituals follow-up Frank filed)?
    3. What's the right selection rule? (e.g., only symbols mentioned in prose, only symbols touched by the diff, etc.)
(e) deliverable — file findings drawer in loom/decisions with explicit YES/NO + selection rule.

Non-obvious risk: Serena ships its own .serena/memories/ subsystem. Loom canonicalized MemPalace as the single tribal-knowledge store. Adopting Serena without disabling its memory subsystem would fragment that boundary. Mitigation must be verified at setup, not assumed.

Reference drawers: drawer_loom_decisions_88aacc394282d2afc139ce22 (this brainstorm session).
acceptance_criteria: (1) Serena installed via 'uv tool install -p 3.13 serena-agent@latest --prerelease=allow' + 'serena init' against ~/repos/liza_base; (2) symbol triples drafted for the four 98an touched files; (3) triples written into findings drawer alongside the prose narrative they would compose with; (4) explicit judgment: would these triples have helped liza_base-w84v (6-rituals follow-up)?; (5) explicit YES/NO recommendation for the feature bead with selection-rule guidance
close_reason: Research complete. Verdict: NO Serena adoption in loom anywhere. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_c9de5e283972378e084a9602 (loom/decisions) carries the full analysis including the addendum honest-revised landing after three conversation cycles. ast-grep evaluation deferred as P3 to loom-2pg.

title: Parallel-dispatch ritualization at bead-pickup (statusline + skill auto-invoke)
description:
Root cause: agent defaults to sequential bead-pickup; user has had to prompt "can these run in parallel?" / "you should be using subagents" 4+ times across distinct liza-base sessions, plus surfaced as a structural gap by loom-z3m.1 f3. The dispatching-parallel-agents skill exists but isn't ritualized at the claim phase, so dispatch overhead "feels expensive" and parallelizable work goes sequential.

Surfaced by:
- loom-z3m.1 f3 (loom): "no systematic 'can this fan out?' prompt at bead-creation or middle-time"
- loom-z3m.1 f1 (liza-base): 4+ recurrences "Pick up X, but you really should be using sub agents"

Proposed fix: Two-layer nudge — claim-phase gate in bead-lifecycle-shell + statusline cue + auto-skill-invoke when condition met.
design:
1. bead-lifecycle-shell phase A2 extension: after bd update <id> --claim resolves, mandatorily run a "seam scan":
   bd ready --json | jq -c | analyze for sibling beads with disjoint file sets / no shared deps. Emit one-line "Parallelizable: N candidates (loom-foo, loom-bar)" or "Parallelizable: none."

2. Statusline cue: workflow-state stores "parallel_candidates: N". scripts/statusline.sh surfaces "PAR:N" when N>0.

3. If N>0: auto-invoke superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents skill before the variable-middle begins. Recipe-shell phase A2 returns to recipe with the parallel context pre-loaded.

4. beadpowers:create-beads also gains a "splitting-heuristic check" prompt at file time (from CLAUDE.md): "are these N+ items independent? file as siblings under umbrella for parallel dispatch."

Negative-cases: single ready bead → no cue. Beads share files → not parallelizable → no auto-invoke. Beads cross types (one task, one feature) → parallelizable if independent.
notes:
Worker evidence (verbatim):

[loom-z3m.1 f1 / liza-base — 4 recurrences across distinct sessions]
sid=496a16b9 ts=2026-05-05T20:19:11Z: "Pick up hcn, but you really should be using sub agents to do the work shouldn't you?"
sid=586b9332 ts=2026-05-06T00:31:27Z: "Look through what we have, are there beads that can be run parralel to pl1?"
sid=5b18956e ts=2026-05-08T02:14:30Z: "Go ahead and grab the ones that you can now in parallel."
sid=7faf32b4 ts=2026-05-15T19:53:28Z: "Grab liza_base-3l3n, and run any in parallel that you can with it."

[loom-z3m.1 f3 / loom] sid=d776b23b ts=2026-05-05T20:21:47Z:
"Ok, a question, there doesnt seem to be a particularly strong tendency to either: * Design for paralel subagents working at bead creation time * Do paralel subagents when the option is available"
close_reason: Closed

title: Verification reflexes: tool-inventory + TDD-mid-recipe + alt-hypothesis gates
description:
Root cause: agent stops at the first unavailable verification path or first plausible hypothesis, instead of enumerating alternatives. Three sub-symptoms (verification, debugging, hypothesizing) share the same shape: "first dead-end terminates the search."

Surfaced by:
- HAW f1: agent declared "can't confirm deploy" without trying gh tools, despite having repo-scoped token
- liza f3: TDD discipline slips under multi-stepper pressure — fix written before failing test
- liza f7: shallow investigation — agent stops at first hypothesis that fits

Proposed fix: Three small additions to existing skills + one new PreToolUse guard.
design:
1. superpowers:verification-before-completion gains a "tool-inventory pre-check" step: before declaring inability, run gh auth status / command -v <tool> / claude mcp list and enumerate available verification paths. Add a lookup table (deploy-target → GitHub-side verification command).

2. superpowers:systematic-debugging gains an "alt-hypotheses considered" gate: explicit "≥2 alternatives" diary entry required before fix proposal. bugfix-a-bead's middle references this as mandatory between debugging and RED test.

3. bead-lifecycle-shell gains "bug-mid-recipe" branchpoint: if a bash exit-code!=0 surfaces a NEW failure mode during variable middle, lifecycle-shell mandatorily invokes test-driven-development before edits resume. Implement as PreToolUse guard intercepting Edit/Write to source files when recent bash output contains test-failure markers AND no test file touched since.

4. New hook hooks/edit-after-failure-guard.sh wired into PreToolUse for Edit/Write/MultiEdit. Refuses with reminder + LOOM_EDIT_AFTER_FAILURE_GUARD_SKIP=1 bypass.

Negative-cases: no failure markers → no block. Test file touched after failure → no block. Skip env-var → no block.
notes:
Worker evidence (verbatim):

[HAW f1] sid=34707042 ts=2026-05-16T01:26:17Z
assistant: "I can't confirm a successful deploy from here. ... doctl is installed locally but not authed (returned 401). I don't have the DigitalOcean MCP server attached this session."
user ts=2026-05-16T01:26:55: "So, you should have that data, and you have github access, shouldn't you be able to query?"
(Agent immediately ran gh auth status and confirmed repo scope was available.)

[liza f3] sid=a17aa8ae ts=2026-05-07T17:24:42Z
user: "Ok, your discipline is slipping. First you need to write a test that will fail until you get this right. _then_ do the fix."

[liza f7] sid=5cd97709 ts=2026-05-12T23:51:58Z
user: "Start at the beginning, look through all of it, don't assume just because you find something that _might_ fit the theory you've found it. Be complete."
close_reason: Closed

title: Session-startup: in-progress resume + since-last-session digest + failing-workflow inline
description:
Root cause: 3 distinct manifestations of the same gap surfaced independently across all 3 retrospective-dig projects — session-startup is forward-looking (ready beads) but under-invests in REORIENTATION cues.

Surfaced by:
- loom-z3m.1 f1: docs CI silently red for 2+ days (already addressed by loom-97q; this is reinforcement)
- loom-z3m.1 f2 (HAW): user away >1 week asked "get me up to speed" — no since-last-session digest
- loom-z3m.1 f4 (liza-base): in_progress beads not foregrounded on resume; user surprised work was incomplete

Proposed fix: Extend skills/session-startup/SKILL.md with three new steps inserted between current "Prime beads" and "Pick a bead":
1. Surface in_progress beads FIRST with last diary entry + last-touched file (drives off bd list --status=in_progress --json + transcript-dir mtime).
2. On cold-start with >3-day gap since last user prompt: emit a since-last-session digest (closed-since beads, recent diaries, last N main commits).
3. Inline-print the most recent failing workflow name + 1-line summary (extends loom-97q's count-only output).

See drawer_loom_decisions_a0972be4fbfa533b6e64f010 (T2 capture) for raw evidence.
design:
Skill edits to skills/session-startup/SKILL.md:

Step 1a-new (after "Prime beads"): bd list --status=in_progress --json | jq -c '...' — print "RESUME?" header with each in_progress bead's id, title, last_touched, last diary entry summary.

Step 1b-new: detect last user-prompt timestamp from latest transcript .jsonl mtime (or message timestamps); if >3 days ago, emit:
  - bd list --status=closed --since="<gap_start>"
  - mempalace_diary_read for last 3 entries in project wing
  - git log --oneline --since="<gap_start>"
Output as a single "Since you were last here:" block.

Step 1c-extend: in existing CI-health step, if any failure detected, also run `gh run view <id> --json conclusion,jobs` for the latest failure and print the first non-success job's name + failure-line summary inline.

Negative-cases fixtures (lib/tests/):
- in_progress=empty → step 1a emits nothing, no header
- gap<=3d → step 1b emits nothing
- CI green → step 1c emits nothing
- All three on a real session → assertions check the structure
notes:
Worker evidence (verbatim):

[loom-z3m.1 f1] sid=3e09b900 ts=2026-05-15T22:33:21Z
User: "Ok, we have an ongoing issue, the build keeps failing in GHA. Building docs I believe."

[loom-z3m.1 f2 / HAW] sid=1e971b39 ts=2026-05-13T22:39:30Z
User: "Ok, the password is taken care of. I've been away from this for over a week, get me up to speed on where we're at."
(Agent then probed each bead one-by-one — wasteful tokens, slow ramp.)

[loom-z3m.1 f4 / liza] sid=849c0e63 ts=2026-05-13T01:54:54Z
User: "Ah, I didn't know we hadn't finished liza_base-n7pb, let's get that done."
close_reason: Closed

title: worktree Python-import-resolution leak: pip install -e MAIN shadows worktree modules
description:
Mode 5 from Frank's 2026-05-13 PM writeup. The MOST insidious failure mode because the worker's verification mechanism is the failure surface: an agent that hit Mode 5 ships tests-pass-but-don't-actually-test-the-change.

## Cause

pip install -e <main> was run at some point against MAIN's path. This installs the project (e.g. liza_base) as an editable site-package pointing at MAIN's source. When a worker invokes pytest or any Python script from the worktree, Python's sys.path resolution prefers the site-package over the local worktree copy.

## Symptom

- Worker runs pytest from worktree → tests load MAIN's modules, not worktree's modifications
- Tests pass because MAIN's behavior happens to match desired outcome (or the test doesn't actually exercise the changed code)
- Worker reports 'tests pass' with confidence
- The leak is INVISIBLE during the session
- Surfaces only post-merge when the worktree changes become live and behavior diverges

First documented: e87k agent report 2026-05-14. Self-report: 'Worktree Python-import-resolution leak — pip install -e <main> shadows worktree copies of liza_base modules. PYTHONPATH=$(pwd) works around it.'

## Fix shape (three options, increasing weight)

### Option A — Worker-brief convention + pre-flight smoke test

Each worker brief includes:
  python -c 'import <project_name>; print(<project_name>.__file__)'
Verify the path resolves to a worktree path. If it resolves to MAIN, set PYTHONPATH=$(pwd) before any python invocation.

Cheapest. Pure rule-discipline. Brittle (agents overlook 500-line briefs).

### Option B — PYTHONPATH-prepending wrapper

Ship scripts/loom-worktree-python that:
  - Refuses outside a linked worktree
  - Sets PYTHONPATH=$(pwd):$PYTHONPATH
  - Execs python with passed args

Worker briefs call loom-worktree-python instead of python directly. Catches more shapes than Option A; still requires the worker to know to use it.

### Option C — Worktree-local venv

Each worker dispatch creates (or uses cached) a worktree-local venv at .claude/worktrees/agent-<id>/.venv/. Venv is pip install -e $(pwd) so the worktree's source becomes the editable install. Adds ~30s per dispatch (could be cached).

Heaviest; cleanest semantics. Mode 5 becomes structurally impossible.

## Recommendation

Start with Option A (brief convention + smoke test) as part of fix (f) from the writeup. If smoke tests miss the issue or agents skip the test, escalate to Option B or C.

## Catches

- Mode 5: Python import resolution

## Doesn't catch

- Modes 1, 2, 4: covered by loom-ymc (edit-write-pwd-guard)
- Mode 3: covered by loom-x4m + 8vc (bd-worktree-preseed)
- Subprocess invocations that import the project — same shadowing happens. Option A's smoke test catches this if it runs early.

## Acceptance

- Worker-brief template (or .claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md update) includes the Python smoke test
- If Option B/C land, document in docs/reference/
- Test fixture covers Option B/C if shipped
notes: From Frank's 2026-05-13 PM writeup; surfaced by e87k agent 2026-05-14. Most insidious of the 5 failure modes because the verification mechanism is the failure surface — silent.
close_reason: Closed

title: PreToolUse Edit/Write/MultiEdit guard against worktree path leaks
description:
The recurring loom-path-leak: dispatched workers can Edit/Write/MultiEdit paths that resolve OUTSIDE their isolated worktree, landing changes in the parent repo (MAIN) instead. 5 re-surfaces 2026-05-13 PM session alone; failure modes: (1) absolute-path-in-brief leak, (2) intentional main-side work via cwd drift, (4) relative-path resolution surprise. Mode 3 (bd-state regression) is separate (covered by loom-x4m + 8vc). Rule-discipline (relative paths, pwd verify) alone doesn't catch all four modes; the path-resolution itself can surprise.

## Fix shape (option a from Frank's writeup)

New PreToolUse hook hooks/edit-write-pwd-guard.sh that:

1. Matches Edit / Write / MultiEdit tool calls.
2. Reads cwd (PWD that the hook fires in = worker's actual cwd).
3. If cwd is NOT in a linked worktree (via lib/worktree-detect.sh from loom-x4m), exit 0 (no guard needed in main).
4. Resolve tool_input.file_path: absolute paths checked as-is, relative paths resolved against cwd.
5. Canonicalize via os.path.realpath to defeat symlinks/.. tricks.
6. If resolved path is OUTSIDE the current worktree root, exit 2 with explicit error message naming the resolved target + the worktree root.
7. Otherwise exit 0.

## Catches

- Mode 1 (absolute-path-in-brief leak): explicit /home/frank/repos/<project>/file rejected when cwd is a worktree.
- Mode 2 (cwd-drift intentional main-side work): if cwd drifts to MAIN, the hook is a no-op (allow); if cwd stays in worktree but absolute path targets MAIN, blocked.
- Mode 4 (relative-path resolves wrong): the hook resolves relative paths against cwd; if resolved target falls outside worktree, blocked.

## Doesn't catch

- Mode 3 (bd-state regression). Separate cluster (loom-x4m + 8vc shipped 2026-05-13).
- Read tool calls. Read-only leaks aren't dangerous (just informative).

## Bypass

No env-var bypass. If a worker legitimately needs to edit MAIN, they cd to MAIN first (hook becomes no-op).

## Tests

Shell-fixture tests under lib/tests/edit-write-pwd-guard.test.sh:
- Worktree cwd + Edit on path in worktree -> allow
- Worktree cwd + Edit on absolute path in MAIN -> reject (exit 2)
- Worktree cwd + Edit on relative path that resolves to MAIN (e.g. ../../something) -> reject
- Main cwd + Edit on anything -> allow (hook is a no-op outside worktrees)
- Non-Edit tools (Bash, Read) -> no-op
- Symlink-based escape -> reject (realpath canonicalizes)

## Acceptance

- Hook ships in hooks/.
- Tests added to lib/tests/.
- Hook registered in settings.snippet.json's PreToolUse matchers.
- Doc page docs/reference/edit-write-pwd-guard.md.
- Full loom shell-fixture suite stays green.
notes: Source: Frank's writeup 2026-05-13 from a parallel session that hit 5 path-leak re-surfaces (a2qx Mode 1, mcxw Mode 2, ogot.6 Mode 3, x5m5 Mode 4, plus one more). Dispatcher hit Mode 3 twice merging. Tooling pays for itself fast — rule-discipline alone isn't sufficient.
close_reason: Shipped via commit 1211351 on main. New PreToolUse hook hooks/edit-write-pwd-guard.sh matches Edit/Write/MultiEdit. Source lib/worktree-detect.sh; if cwd is in a linked worktree, resolves tool_input.file_path against PWD (or absolute), canonicalizes via python3 os.path.realpath of existing parent chain, compares against git rev-parse --show-toplevel. Target under worktree root → exit 0; otherwise → exit 2 with explanatory message naming both paths + bypass env var. Catches Modes 1+2+4 from Frank's writeup; Mode 3 separate (covered by loom-x4m + 8vc). Bypass LOOM_EDIT_WRITE_GUARD_SKIP=1 for rare intentional cross-tree ops. 14 fixture tests cover all worktree+target combinations + tool filtering + bypass. Full loom shell-fixture suite: 19/19 files, 264 tests (was 250, +14), 0 failures. Wired via settings.snippet.json (new Edit|Write|MultiEdit matcher). Closing drawer drawer_loom_decisions_<filed-this-call> captures full design + cluster review. Worktree-isolation cluster now FULLY COVERS all known mechanical failure modes. Open: review whether loom-0hi (cwd drift) is subsumed by ymc — likely. KG triples: loom-ymc -> closed_by_commit -> 1211351; loom-ymc -> closes_lifecycle_gap_in -> worktree-isolation-cluster; edit-write-pwd-guard.sh -> uses -> lib/worktree-detect.sh.

title: Fresh worktree's empty .beads/issues.jsonl wipes main's on bd update --claim
description:
Symptom (reproduced 2026-05-11 in two parallel dispatched agents on liza_base e6i + da0):

A loom-managed isolated worktree is created at `<repo>/.claude/worktrees/agent-<id>/`. The worktree gets a normal copy of the repo tree including `.beads/` directory. However the bd dolt DB underneath `.beads/` is EMPTY in the fresh worktree (bd state stored in `.beads/embeddeddolt/` — not propagated by worktree creation, since dolt dbs are local-not-checked-in).

Agent inside the worktree runs:
  bd update <bead-id> --claim

bd writes the claim to the empty dolt DB AND auto-exports the resulting (one-issue) state to `.beads/issues.jsonl`, overwriting the worktree's working-tree copy. When the agent stages `.beads/issues.jsonl` for commit (because bd's pre-commit hook adds it OR the agent's normal git-add sweep grabs it), the worktree commit now contains an issues.jsonl with ONE issue. On merge into main, ALL OTHER ISSUES IN ISSUES.JSONL ARE LOST in the merged tree.

Reproduces every dispatched-agent run; both agents in our 2026-05-11 session had to apply the same workaround ("loom-417 pattern"): manually copy `.beads/issues.jsonl` from main repo HEAD into the worktree before commit so bd ops use the canonical main DB.

Workaround = brittle. Real fix needs to be at the loom worktree-creation level:

OPTION A: When loom creates an agent worktree, also copy/share the parent repo's `.beads/embeddeddolt/` directory (or symlink it), so bd inside the worktree sees the canonical DB.

OPTION B: Loom adds a `.beads/` to the worktree's gitignore-via-info/exclude so bd writes never get staged in the worktree, AND establishes a 'use parent's bd db' convention where bd ops are run via `bd --db <parent>/.beads/embeddeddolt` or with `BEADS_DIR=<parent>/.beads`.

OPTION C: bd itself learns about worktrees and auto-finds the canonical DB up the parent chain (likely an upstream-bd ask).

Cost of inaction: every dispatched-agent run risks data loss in `.beads/issues.jsonl` if the workaround is forgotten. Today's session avoided it but only because both agents knew the pattern.

Source: `liza_base-e6i` + `liza_base-da0` close drawers (2026-05-11). Pattern previously named in liza_base 'loom-417 pattern' / 'session-8 close drawer' references.
close_reason: Shipped via merge commit 467c9e2 on main (worker commit 55dbb72 on frank/loom-x4m). Three-layer fix in one PreToolUse hook hooks/bd-worktree-preseed.sh, memoized via .beads/.loom-preseeded sentinel. L1: bd import .beads/issues.jsonl pre-seeds worktree dolt. L2: bd config set export.git-add false stops auto-staging. L3: .beads/issues.jsonl added to .git/info/exclude as belt-and-suspenders. Skips read-only bd commands and non-Bash tools. Bypass: LOOM_BD_WORKTREE_PRESEED_SKIP=1. New shared helper lib/worktree-detect.sh detects linked worktrees via git rev-parse --git-common-dir comparison. New ref doc docs/reference/bd-worktree-preseed.md + inventory row in docs/reference/hooks/index.md. CLAUDE.md worktree-commit caveat updated. Closing drawer drawer_loom_decisions_d09f9f243008f5a6731542e3 captures full design rationale including why PreToolUse (not SessionStart per loom-w58 finding) and why pre-seed (not BEADS_DIR redirect). 10/10 new tests PASS. Full loom shell-fixture suite: 17/17 files 234 tests 0 failures. Absorbed superseded sibling loom-14w.

title: slim SessionStart hook output for subagents (isSidechain=true / parentUuid != null)
description:
**Top-leverage fix from loom-nsb dynamic-phase research (drawer_loom_decisions_3eec30046461f0766ac92eec).**

Every subagent spawn pays the full Claude Code SessionStart prefix tax (~21 KB per invocation) — using-superpowers preamble (~5.9 KB), using-beadpowers, skills enumeration, bd prime. For application-helper subagents (e.g., liza_base's per-tool-call voice translator) and even legitimate parallel workers, none of this is structurally usable: the brief carries the intent.

The using-superpowers skill already has a `<SUBAGENT-STOP>` directive telling the model to skip itself, but the **hook still emits the full text** — the directive only tells the model what to do after receiving it. The fix is to detect subagent context in the hook itself and skip emission.

## Detection signals

Claude Code passes hook context via env / stdin JSON. Subagent invocations are identifiable via:
- `isSidechain=true` (or equivalent in the hook input)
- `parentUuid` is non-null
- Possibly `source` field or session-id hierarchy

Verify the exact field name(s) by reading recent worker transcript JSON or testing.

## Scope (all loom SessionStart hooks)

- workflow-mode-onboarding.sh (loom)
- mempal-stop-hook.sh trigger (mempalace, but loom-installed)
- bd prime registration (beads, but worth a wrapper)
- using-superpowers / using-beadpowers preambles (superpowers/beadpowers plugins)

For each: detect subagent context → skip emission OR emit a tiny "subagent: skipping standard preamble" stub.

## Estimated savings

~20% of every subagent invocation transcript. Across the observed liza_base history (~2,747 worker transcripts, 305 MB on disk), this is roughly **60 MB of pure preamble waste**, with input-token costs scaling proportionally. Single highest-leverage change in the entire token-burn analysis.

## Acceptance

- All four hook surfaces detect subagent context and skip preamble emission.
- Test fixture: simulate isSidechain=true input, verify hook emits empty (or stub) additionalContext.
- Test fixture: simulate orchestrator-session input, verify full preamble still emits.
- Sample a real liza_base subagent transcript before/after, measure the byte delta.
notes: Surfaced 2026-05-09 via loom-nsb research. See drawer_loom_decisions_3eec30046461f0766ac92eec for full findings. Highest-leverage loom-side change identified.
close_reason: Round-1 parallel dispatch SHIPPED. Closing drawer drawer_loom_decisions_a4a6babeb0557cfd6a949692 (loom/decisions, 2026-05-12) covers all three. Merge commits: dabf03c (no3), 5a16ede (w58), 123d72b (nc2). Worker commits: dbb068b, cb36b5b, 81e4034. Full loom test suite green: 16/16 files, 224 tests. Notable findings: w58 worker discovered CC 2.1.112 may not fire SessionStart hooks for sidechains (fix is still defensive); nc2 verified 10.5x reduction on liza_base (153KB to 14.6KB). Followups: investigate actual SessionStart-prefix injection point + file upstream bd --no-memories flag PR.

title: MCP diary + status tools (diary_write, diary_read, status, kg_stats)
description:
Goal: implement the remaining tools actually exercised this session:
- mcp_diary_write(agent_name, entry, topic?, wing?) -> stores a diary entry (reuse the drawers table with a convention wing=wing_<agent_name> unless overridden, room='diary', matching MemPalace's existing per-agent-wing diary pattern)
- mcp_diary_read(agent_name, n=3) -> returns the N most recent diary entries for that agent, most-recent-first
- mcp_status() -> total drawer count, per-wing counts, per-room counts (mirrors mempalace_status's existing shape, minus the chroma-specific sqlite_integrity block which doesn't apply to a Dolt backend -- replace with a dolt-appropriate health check, e.g. a cheap SELECT 1 + dolt status)
- mcp_kg_stats() -> entity count, triple count (thin wrapper reusing graph_stats from loom-40ec.4.3 if that's landed first, else a standalone query)

RED: mcp_diary_write('test-agent', 'test entry', topic='smoke-test') succeeds; mcp_diary_read('test-agent', 3) includes that entry as the most recent; mcp_status() reflects the seeded drawer count from other tests' fixtures (or a clean count in isolation).

Files: memory-server/mcp_server/tools/diary.py, memory-server/mcp_server/tools/status.py, memory-server/tests/test_mcp_diary_status.py
Depends on: loom-40ec.4.1 (server scaffold)
close_reason: memsrv_diary_write/read, memsrv_status, memsrv_kg_stats implemented. Diary entries stored as drawers (wing_<agent> convention). Status degrades gracefully on Dolt-unreachable. kg_stats delegates to tools/kg.py at call time. Resolved a server.py merge conflict (both .4.3/.4.4 added registration lines) and fixed one integration wrinkle (a test asserted the exact standalone kg_stats shape, broke once delegation activated -- fixed to assert the shared subset). 12 new tests, full suite 36/36, independently re-verified, ruff clean. Merged to main at commit f60a150.

title: MCP knowledge-graph tools (kg_query, kg_add, kg_invalidate, graph_stats)
description:
Goal: implement the KG tool surface backed by the kg_triples table (already built per D7, loom-40ec.3). Tools:
- mcp_kg_add(subject, predicate, object, confidence=1.0, source_closet?) -> inserts a triple (predicate is free-text per D7, NOT an enum -- no validation against a fixed list)
- mcp_kg_query(entity, direction='both', as_of?) -> queries by subject (direction=outgoing), object (direction=incoming), or both, using the (subject)/(object)/(subject,predicate) indices; as_of filters by valid_from/valid_to when given
- mcp_kg_invalidate(triple_id or subject+predicate+object) -> sets current=FALSE and valid_to=NOW() rather than deleting (temporal-validity semantics, matching mempalace_kg_query's existing as_of/current fields)
- mcp_graph_stats() -> entity count (distinct subjects+objects), triple count, current vs expired breakdown

RED: mcp_kg_add('A', 'grounded_in', 'B'); mcp_kg_query('A', direction='outgoing') returns the A->grounded_in->B fact with current=true; mcp_kg_invalidate that triple; a subsequent mcp_kg_query('A') either omits it or marks current=false depending on whether the caller asked for historical facts.

Files: memory-server/mcp_server/tools/kg.py, memory-server/tests/test_mcp_kg.py
Depends on: loom-40ec.4.1 (server scaffold)
close_reason: memsrv_kg_add/kg_query/kg_invalidate/graph_stats implemented over kg_triples (D7 schema). Free-text predicate (no enum), genuine direction=both union, temporal as_of filtering, entity_count via subject UNION object. 6 new tests, full suite 24/24, independently re-verified, ruff clean. Merged to main at commit e8f4c71 (merge of 4222d96).

title: constitution-enforce.sh silently no-ops — yq missing from PATH
description:
Discovered 2026-07-09 while dispatching loom-40ec.3: yq is not installed anywhere on this machine (checked common paths, apt/dpkg). hooks/constitution-enforce.sh's own documented behavior (line ~200-204) is to fail open with a stderr WARN when yq is missing — meaning EVERY Bash/Edit/Write/MultiEdit call on this machine has been passing through constitution-enforce.sh's tooling-rules enforcement as a silent no-op (the AGE-SKEW nudge and invariants-text extraction still run since they don't need yq, but the core forbidden/package_manager/run_prefix enforcement never fires). This is exactly the failure mode loom's own 'gate, don't advise' convention (docs/explanation/gate-dont-advise.md) warns against: a correctness gate that silently degrades to advisory-only because a dependency went missing, with no signal to the user beyond a stderr WARN easy to miss amid other tool output.

Fix options to evaluate: (1) install yq system-wide (simplest, but doesn't prevent future silent regressions on other machines/CI); (2) add a fallback lightweight YAML front-matter parser in the hook itself (python3 has no stdlib yaml, but the front-matter is simple enough for a targeted regex/line parser without a full YAML engine) so the hook degrades gracefully without a WARN-and-skip; (3) surface the yq-missing WARN more loudly — e.g. escalate to session-startup's constitution fingerprint step (1f) so it's caught at cold-start rather than buried per-Bash-call stderr.

Files: hooks/constitution-enforce.sh, possibly docs/reference/constitution-enforce-hook.md
AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE: needs-decision (which fix approach)
close_reason: yq installed by user (nix-profile, yq 3.4.3). Re-verified live 2026-07-09: pip install from loom's top-level root is now correctly BLOCKED (exit 2, correct constitution message); the same command from memory-server/ is correctly ALLOWED (nested constitution shadowing confirmed working with real enforcement active, not just fail-open). Immediate symptom resolved via bd remember key yq-required-for-constitution-enforce; no code change/commit needed since the fix was installing the missing system dependency, not a repo change.

title: Resolve C5 — KG disposition for Dolt substrate
description:
Design-shaped task, no code. Goal: decide whether the ~2300 existing MemPalace KG triples migrate as curated Dolt relational tables (subject/predicate/object rows + indices, matching mempalace_kg_query's temporal valid_from/valid_to + as_of support) or get dropped/auto-re-extracted from drawer text post-migration. Curated triples encode intentional decisions (loom-tdua's soft predicate vocabulary: supersedes_design_of, grounded_in, emits_bead, soundness_tier, depends_on_invariant, etc.) that auto-extraction would not reliably reproduce — lean toward migrate-as-curated unless a concrete blocker surfaces. Output: locked decision + schema sketch (columns, indices, temporal-validity handling) written into drawer_loom_decisions_521e654693797b4f169b4cbd (flip [C5]).

Files: none (MemPalace drawer update only)
AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE: needs-decision (design judgment on KG migration)
notes: RESOLVED 2026-07-09: D7 — KG triples migrate as curated Dolt relational rows (not dropped, not auto-extracted). mempalace_kg_stats: 2600 entities/2312 triples, predicates run into the hundreds of free-text values invented per design-cycle (confirms loom-tdua's soft-schema framing). Schema: kg_triples(id, subject, predicate, object, confidence, valid_from, valid_to, source_closet, current, created_at) with indices on subject, object, and (subject,predicate). Full rationale folded into drawer_loom_decisions_521e654693797b4f169b4cbd (D7).
close_reason: C5 resolved as D7 — curated Dolt relational kg_triples table, schema sketched. See drawer_loom_decisions_521e654693797b4f169b4cbd.

title: Upstream tag_filter param for mempalace_search → MemPalace/mempalace PR
description:
AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE: upstream+attended
Files: mempalace/mcp_server.py, mempalace/searcher.py, tests/test_mcp_server.py
