Source = the rebased-onto-v3.5.0 form from loom-iqwr (fork commit 69c612f reconciled onto v3.5.0's build_where_filter/_metadata_filter_sql seam). Depends on the tag_drawer PR (shared mcp_server.py; and tag_filter reads tags written by tag_drawer). Spawn a watch-bead.

A tag_filter passed while vector_disabled is active is SILENTLY IGNORED (returns unfiltered results). Fine for loom's normal-search usage; for the upstream PR, either thread tag.<key> through the SQLite fallback OR hard-error when both are set. Tests don't exercise that mode.

title: Auto-exclude loom artifacts via .git/info/exclude in non-loom-managed repos (auto guest-lite)
description:
PROBLEM. Loom's GLOBAL SessionStart hook hooks/workflow-mode-onboarding.sh writes .claude/workflow-state.json (and prompts for .claude/workflow.json) into ANY repo that has .beads/ but no loom workflow.json — e.g. e2e-api-tests, a shared team repo. Those loom files surface in the team's git status and annoy teammates who don't use loom.

DESIGN (locked with Frank 2026-07-02). Supply the auto-detect trigger that guest mode (2026-05-06 drawer) deliberately declined ('No auto-detect'). Reuse the existing suppression mechanism: lib/info-exclude.sh writes a BEGIN LOOM/END LOOM block to <repo>/.git/info/exclude (per-clone, NEVER committed — teammates see no diff at all). This partially reverses guest mode's no-auto-detect decision for the narrow artifact-hiding case (grounded_in that drawer).

MECHANISM = .git/info/exclude (Frank's pick), NOT committed .gitignore (a committed .gitignore change is itself a diff the team must accept — defeats 'don't annoy people').

TRIGGER = extend hooks/workflow-mode-onboarding.sh (SessionStart, already computes ROOT + guards on .beads/). Run a new lib/auto-exclude.sh sync BEFORE the hook's early exits.

ARTIFACT SET = .claude/workflow.json, .claude/workflow-state.json, .claude/settings.local.json, /issues.jsonl. LEAVE .beads/ UNTOUCHED — in e2e-api-tests it is the team's shared bd tracker, not a loom artifact (guest-mode 'host has bd -> use it' precedent).

SELF-HEAL + guest interaction (three cases the sync distinguishes by reading workflow.json):
  - workflow.json ABSENT (not loom-managed) -> info_exclude_add(artifact set).
  - workflow.json PRESENT, non-guest (managed) -> info_exclude_remove (artifacts should commit once Frank opts in via /audit-project or mode-pick).
  - workflow.json PRESENT with .guest block (guest mode active) -> PRESERVE the block (guest owns it); do NOT remove.

Files: lib/auto-exclude.sh, hooks/workflow-mode-onboarding.sh, lib/tests/auto-exclude.test.sh

RED: INVARIANT — In a git repo with .beads/ and NO .claude/workflow.json, running the auto-exclude sync yields a BEGIN LOOM block in .git/info/exclude containing .claude/workflow.json, .claude/workflow-state.json, .claude/settings.local.json, /issues.jsonl AND NOT containing .beads/. Given .claude/workflow.json present (non-guest) When sync runs Then the BEGIN LOOM block is removed. Given .claude/workflow.json with a .guest block When sync runs Then the block is preserved. Sync is idempotent (running twice = same file).
notes:
DESIGN REFINEMENT during RED (clobber risk found): bd-worktree-preseed.sh writes its OWN '# BEGIN LOOM' block (.beads/issues.jsonl) using the same marker info-exclude.sh matches on a loose prefix (/^# BEGIN LOOM/). A blanket info_exclude_remove in the managed self-heal path would clobber that block and re-open the loom-x4m data-loss bug. SAFER CONTRACT (supersedes the original 3-case RED): sync is ADD-ONLY. workflow.json PRESENT -> NO-OP (covers managed repos AND guest mode — loom-guest already owns its block; never remove). workflow.json ABSENT -> info_exclude_add(artifact set). Self-heal-on-transition dropped: removal, when a repo becomes truly loom-owned, is the explicit /audit-project adoption path's job, not an every-session blanket remove. Also: in a shared repo you typically want loom artifacts hidden even after picking a loom mode, so no-op-when-present is more correct, not just safer.

REVISED RED: (a) unmanaged repo (.beads/, no .claude/workflow.json) -> sync yields a BEGIN LOOM block in .git/info/exclude containing .claude/workflow.json, .claude/workflow-state.json, .claude/settings.local.json, /issues.jsonl AND NOT .beads/. (b) managed (workflow.json present, non-guest) -> sync is a no-op (adds nothing). (c) guest (workflow.json .guest.active=true) with a pre-existing block -> sync leaves the block UNCHANGED (never removes/clobbers). (d) idempotent. (e) non-git dir -> no-op, no crash.
close_reason: Shipped lib/auto-exclude.sh + wired into workflow-mode-onboarding hook; auto-hides loom artifacts via per-clone .git/info/exclude in non-loom-managed repos (workflow.json absent). ADD-only, no clobber of guest/preseed blocks. 6/6 test + 104-file suite green; live via install.sh symlink. Merged to main. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_04a3e3777a472b2d0993a159.

title: Detect downstream loom-managed convention-drift — a gate/refresh so loom updates don't silently strand managed projects (DESIGN-FIRST: /explore → /design-a-cycle)
description:
AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE: design — needs a human-attended /explore → /design-a-cycle; NOT auto-dispatchable, mechanism unknown.

THE STRUCTURAL PROBLEM (root-cause diagnosis, claude-opus diary 2026-06-11 12:03, liza_base). Loom pushes its conventions to downstream loom-managed projects via TWO channels, and only ONE stays current:
  (1) GLOBAL symlinked skills/hooks/commands (~/.claude/...) — CURRENT (carries dispatch-v2, background-default, the global-path fix, etc.), but only fires IF a session routes into it.
  (2) PER-PROJECT priming (the managed project's own CLAUDE.md, .claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md, recipe-routing) — can go STALE. This is the STEERING WHEEL, and nothing forces it to re-sync when loom ships a convention update.
When loom ships a dispatch-v2-class update, EVERY downstream managed project silently strands on its stale priming until a human notices. liza_base was exhibit A: 26-day-stale priming → 'wasn't using subagents when it should' + grabbed an in-world ritual (/deep-sleep) instead of /wrap-up.

WHY IT'S STRUCTURAL (the meta-level gate-don't-advise gap, loom-wj26.1). /audit-project — loom's existing drift-catcher — is MANUAL-ONLY by design. Nothing detects 'this managed project's conventions drifted from loom's CURRENT set' and forces/nudges a re-sync. So dispatch-v2-class updates silently strand every managed project. This is the gate-don't-advise principle applied at the META level: the drift check must not depend on a human remembering to run /audit-project.

EVIDENCE / FIRST CONCRETE INSTANCE. loom-5x5o (SHIPPED 2026-06-11, merge 34bb095): downstream-routed skills invoked loom helpers by repo-relative scripts/loom-* → silently dark in every downstream project. That fixed ONE class (script-invocation paths) + added one static-lint gate. This bead is the GENERAL detector that would have caught loom-5x5o's class (and the liza_base stale-priming class) automatically, instead of relying on Frank watching a parallel session.

TWO FORKS (from the 12:03 diagnosis; pick during the design phase):
  (a) TACTICAL — a re-sync mechanism that refreshes a managed project's CLAUDE.md + .claude/rules + recipe-routing against loom's current set (extends /audit-project; possibly an --apply-drift mode).
  (b) STRUCTURAL — a general drift DETECTOR (gate/refresh command, or a fingerprint loom stamps + checks) so a managed project knows when its conventions trail loom's current version. Open sub-questions: what's the drift surface (which files/conventions are version-bearing)? how is loom's 'current set' versioned/fingerprinted? nudge-not-block (loom-yb5, human-attended re-sync) vs gate (loom-wj26.1, correctness-must-be-true)? where does it fire (session-startup? a /loom-sync command? on loom install/upgrade)?

NEXT ACTION (the ladder is explore → design → build): open /explore on 'how should loom detect + remediate downstream convention-drift' (this is SUB-design — the mechanism is genuinely unknown), then likely PROMOTE to /design-a-cycle (soundness gate; emits the implementation epic via create-beads). Do NOT jump to implementation — there is no locked mechanism yet, hence no RED:/Files: lines on this bead.

LINEAGE: grounded in diary 2026-06-11 12:03 (root-cause diagnosis) + drawer drawer_loom_decisions_0e3d4794c7a44b95313c59dd (loom-5x5o SHIPPED). Generalizes loom-5x5o (one class) → the meta-detector (all classes). Relates: /audit-project (the manual drift-catcher this would automate), loom-wj26.1 (gate-don't-advise), loom-yb5 (nudge-not-block), loom-7ro (loom-managed project markers + canonical settings).

title: script/gen runs the doc generators + gate on clean regeneration
description:
Goal: Give doc regeneration a canonical home in the script/ convention (loom's script/gen slot is currently empty) + a gate that fails when generated output isn't committed (the gofmt -l pattern). Direct application of the gate-don't-advise principle: 'regenerate' becomes a named command and 'tree is clean after regen' becomes the gate.

Files: script/gen, lib/tests/script-gen-clean-regen.test.sh
RED: INVARIANT: running script/gen regenerates the per-item nav (scripts/loom-docs-gen) and leaves NO uncommitted diff when the tree is already in sync; on a stale tree it regenerates the drift away (gofmt -l shape).
Steps: 1. write the test (clean tree when in sync; regenerates when stale); 2. write script/gen running scripts/loom-docs-gen (extensible to future generators); 3. set constitution canonical_commands.gen from empty -> script/gen; consider CI wiring.
Acceptance: script/gen exists+executable; test green; constitution canonical_commands.gen updated; mkdocs --strict clean.
close_reason: Merged: script/gen runs scripts/loom-docs-gen (extensible GENERATORS list) + gofmt-l clean-regen gate; constitution canonical_commands.gen set. RED→GREEN 4/4, suite 102/102. Part of epic loom-wj26.

title: Wire /audit-project Check 4 to the mechanized loom-docs-catalogue + loom-docs-gen engines
description:
Goal: Fix the recurrence root cause. /audit-project --check=docs Check 4 (inclusion-glob symmetric coverage) re-derives the comparison via naive grep; when run in loom's OWN repo it should DELEGATE to scripts/loom-docs-catalogue --check + scripts/loom-docs-gen --check (the mechanized engines from loom-wjuo/loom-itph). For non-loom managed projects Check 4 keeps its existing generic check until the deferred generic engine (sibling) lands.

Files: skills/audit-project/SKILL.md, agents/project-onboarder.md
RED: INVARIANT: skills/audit-project/SKILL.md Check 4 prose instructs delegation to scripts/loom-docs-catalogue --check AND scripts/loom-docs-gen --check (not a re-derived grep) when those scripts are present.
Steps: 1. doc-presence test asserting the delegation instruction; 2. edit SKILL + onboarder; 3. mkdocs --strict + suite.
Acceptance: Check 4 prose delegates to the engines; lib/tests covers it; suite green.
close_reason: Merged: wj26.1 gate-don't-advise principle (CLAUDE.md + docs/explanation/gate-dont-advise.md); wj26.3 /audit-project Check 4 delegates to loom-docs-catalogue+loom-docs-gen --check (RED→GREEN 14/14). Part of epic loom-wj26.

title: Principle: gate-don't-advise — every loom drift-check must be enforced, not advisory
description:
Goal: Capture the gate-don't-advise principle so future loom checks default to enforcement. Motivated by the loom-9z1.9 -> loom-wjuo recurrence: a detector that only ran on manual /audit-project let drift return.

Principle: every loom drift-detector/check MUST be wired to a REAL enforcement gate (suite test via script/test, a pre-push hook, or CI) — never left as an advisory grep a human must remember to run. An advisory-only correctness check is a latent rot surface. (Distinct from INTENTIONAL nudge-not-block UX per loom-yb5 — that's a deliberate design choice for attended decisions, not an ungated correctness invariant.)

Files: CLAUDE.md, docs/explanation/gate-dont-advise.md
Steps: 1. add a CLAUDE.md convention bullet; 2. write a short project-agnostic docs/explanation page (cite loom-9z1.9->wjuo->itph as THE example, not the only one); 3. nav it (scripts/loom-docs-gen leaves nav alone — this is an explanation page, hand-nav) + cross-link from existing drift-defense docs.
Acceptance: principle in CLAUDE.md + explanation page; mkdocs build --strict clean.
close_reason: Merged: wj26.1 gate-don't-advise principle (CLAUDE.md + docs/explanation/gate-dont-advise.md); wj26.3 /audit-project Check 4 delegates to loom-docs-catalogue+loom-docs-gen --check (RED→GREEN 14/14). Part of epic loom-wj26.

title: docs-drift prevention — gate-don't-advise + reusable reference gates
description:
Generalize the prevention of the loom-wjuo/loom-itph wart: a documentation surface that mirrors a source-of-truth on disk drifts silently. ROOT FAILURE is not 'no detector' — loom HAD one (loom-9z1.9 /audit-project --check=docs Check 4) but it was naive-grep + advisory + only ran on manual /audit-project, so the drift recurred until a human eyeballed the rendered site. The lesson: a detector that does not GATE will rot back.

Scope (user-agreed: cheap + option 3): capture the gate-don't-advise PRINCIPLE (project-agnostic) + harden loom's OWN reference gates into reusable shape NOW; file a generic managed-project docs<->source engine as EXPLICITLY DEFERRED (don't build speculatively — downstream projects lack loom's skills/*/SKILL.md structure; their manifest is endpoints/config/CLI).

Children: (1) principle capture, (2) audit existing advisory checks, (3) wire Check 4 to the mechanized engine, (4) script/gen + clean-regen gate, (5) DEFERRED generic engine.

Lineage: loom-9z1.8/9z1.9 (advisory doc-drift defenses that recurred) -> loom-wjuo (loom-docs-catalogue: index-table gate) -> loom-itph (loom-docs-gen: per-item nav gate). Drawers drawer_loom_decisions_e5a657b534eee751d8a6b5bc + drawer_loom_decisions_0fa813014cb931193d036fb2.
close_reason: docs-drift prevention DELIVERED: wj26.1 gate-don't-advise principle, wj26.3 Check 4 delegates to mechanized engines, wj26.4 script/gen + clean-regen gate, wj26.2 advisory-check audit (no new gaps). wj26.5 generic engine closed not-planned -> parked idea (drawer 183c7348). Wave drawer 4da41168, audit drawer 71ae66d2.

title: Generated per-item Reference nav — scripts/loom-docs-gen (bash) + drift gate
description:
Follow-up to loom-wjuo. The Reference nav gives per-item browsable left-nav for only 6 skills + 9 hooks; all 18 slash-commands and all 4 subagents (and 10 skills) collapse to index-table + 'All ___' verbatim dump with NO per-item nav entry. User wants uniform per-item browsable nav across all 4 categories, GENERATED (no hand-edited nav), drift-gated.

Mechanism (B, chosen over mkdocs-gen-files+literate-nav because loom's constitution forbids pip-install + pins runtime=bash): a bash generator, mirroring the loom-fanout-detect / loom-docs-catalogue deterministic-script + drift-gate pattern.

scripts/loom-docs-gen [--check]:
 - default: for each shipped primitive (skills/*/SKILL.md, commands/*.md, agents/*.md, hooks/*.sh), ensure a nav page exists — a CURATED rich page if one exists (15 today: 6 skill/command pages + 9 hook pages, preserved verbatim, NEVER overwritten), else GENERATE a thin include-wrapper page at docs/reference/<navdir>/<name>.md that include-markdowns (or code-fence includes, for hooks/*.sh) the source. Then rewrite a sentinel-delimited (LOOM-DOCS-GEN:START/END) nav block in mkdocs.yml listing every primitive per category + the 'All ___ (verbatim)' dump last. Idempotent.
 - --check: regenerate into a temp + diff vs committed; exit 1 naming staleness. The drift gate.

Curated-page registry is EXPLICIT in the script (loom-9z1.7 lesson: hard-code is honest, fuzzy-match introduces silent bugs) — handles the constitution-enforce->constitution-enforce-hook.md naming mismatch + the reference/hooks/ subdir pages.

Wire --check into hooks/pre-push-mkdocs-strict.sh (WARN-only) + a suite test. The 6 shared skill/command curated pages cross-list under both Skills and Slash commands (they document both).

Files: scripts/loom-docs-gen, lib/tests/loom-docs-gen.test.sh, mkdocs.yml, docs/reference/slash-commands, docs/reference/subagents, docs/reference/skills, docs/reference/hooks, hooks/pre-push-mkdocs-strict.sh
RED: INVARIANT: scripts/loom-docs-gen --check exits non-zero when any shipped primitive lacks a nav page (curated or generated) OR a generated wrapper page / the mkdocs.yml LOOM-DOCS-GEN nav block is stale vs shipped primitives; exits zero when committed pages + nav block exactly match a fresh generation; bare 'loom-docs-gen' is idempotent.
Grounding: loom-wjuo closing drawer (drawer_loom_decisions_e5a657b534eee751d8a6b5bc) OPEN section; this session's nav-coherence follow-up.
close_reason: Shipped: scripts/loom-docs-gen (bash generator + --check drift gate, WARN-wired into pre-push, 25-clause test) generates per-item nav pages for every primitive lacking a curated page; 36 wrappers created; Skills 16/Commands 18/Subagents 4/Hooks 19 all browsable in left nav. Curated registry explicit. Verified: suite 100/100, shellcheck 0, mkdocs --strict 0 warnings. Merge e31a0c2.

title: Reference-nav coherence: catalogue drift-check + complete index tables + nav restructure
description:
The docs/ Reference section is incoherent and its index inventory tables have drifted from the filesystem.

PROBLEM 1 — completeness (hand-maintained index tables drifted from glob reality):
- Skills index table (docs/reference/skills/index.md) MISSING: explore, loom-adopt
- Commands index table (docs/reference/slash-commands/index.md) MISSING: cleanup-orphans, explore, loom-adopt
- Hooks index table (docs/reference/hooks/index.md) MISSING: constitution-enforce, context-budget-sensor, pytest-tempdir-prune, worktree-bg-inventory; PLUS 2 duplicated table rows (edit-after-failure-guard, edit-write-pwd-guard)
- Subagents index table: complete.
The verbatim 'All ___' dump pages are glob-includes so they stay complete; only the hand-authored inventory TABLES drift.

PROBLEM 2 — nav incoherence (mkdocs.yml): Skills/Slash commands/Subagents each collapse to index + one giant 'All ___' verbatim dump (no per-item nav). Hooks are half-individual. Per-item reference pages that DO exist are scattered under ad-hoc TOPICAL groups (Dispatch & design cycle, Brownfield adoption, Worktree-isolation guards, Helper scripts) instead of under their category, so a skill like explore is documented but unfindable under Skills.

SCOPE (user picked A+B+C):
A. Fix the 4 index tables to completeness (add 9 missing rows, drop 2 dups) + deep-link rows into the dump anchors where stable.
B. Restructure mkdocs.yml nav by CATEGORY (per-item pages under Skills/Slash commands/Hooks/Scripts), retire the ad-hoc topical groups.
C. Add scripts/loom-docs-catalogue --check: a filesystem-vs-index-table drift detector (loom detector-script ethos), tested under lib/tests, wired into the docs preflight/pre-push gate so the tables can never silently drift again.

Files: scripts/loom-docs-catalogue, lib/tests/loom-docs-catalogue.test.sh, docs/reference/skills/index.md, docs/reference/slash-commands/index.md, docs/reference/subagents/index.md, docs/reference/hooks/index.md, mkdocs.yml, hooks/pre-push-mkdocs-strict.sh
RED: INVARIANT: scripts/loom-docs-catalogue --check exits non-zero when a shipped primitive (skills/*/SKILL.md, commands/*.md, agents/*.md, hooks/*.sh) is absent from — or duplicated in — its docs/reference/<cat>/index.md inventory table; exits zero when every shipped primitive appears exactly once across all four categories.
Grounding: session 2026-06-10 pm docs-nav audit (diary SESSION-START-2026-06-10-pm-docs-nav).
close_reason: Shipped: catalogue drift-check (scripts/loom-docs-catalogue + 10-clause test, WARN-wired into pre-push), 4 index tables completed (9 missing rows added, 2 dups removed — 57 primitives each listed once), nav restructured by category (topical groups retired). Verified: suite 99/99, shellcheck 0, mkdocs --strict 0, drift OK. Merge 47abaf8.

title: loom-mine-history: engine silently emits 0 drafts when claude -p fails (2>/dev/null swallow) — retry+backoff+abort-loud
description:
Goal: The loom-mine-history engine SILENTLY emits 0 drafts when the per-unit `claude -p` salience call fails or returns empty, masquerading as "nothing was salient." This made a PAID run waste spend AND made the loom-b10 diagnosis ambiguous (both the original 0/787 e2e dogfood and the loom-rnxp 0/353 re-mine were caused by this, NOT by body-truncation).

ROOT CAUSE (lib/loom-mine-history.sh):
- Line ~536: `reply=$(claude -p "$source_text" --model "$model" --output-format text 2>/dev/null)` — stderr swallowed.
- Line ~547-551: empty/unparseable reply -> salient="" -> `[ "$salient" != "true" ]` -> `continue` (no draft). Indistinguishable from a genuine {"salient":false}.
- Net: if a high fraction of claude calls fail (rate-limit/throttle/auth/transient), the run emits 0 drafts and writes a clean manifest that LOOKS successful.

EVIDENCE (2026-06-09 investigation): loom-b10 fix is CORRECT — engine run FOREGROUND on rich e2e commits emitted full-rationale drafts (3/3 on --since=2026-05-27; 13/16 on a --since=2026-01-01 slice). The 0/353 in loom-rnxp happened because that bg full-sweep fired 353 rapid claude -p calls CONCURRENT with an active main session -> throttled to empty -> swallowed -> silent 0.

FIX (acceptance):
1. Do NOT blanket-swallow claude stderr; capture it for diagnostics (e.g. to a per-run error log).
2. Detect a FAILED/empty claude reply distinctly from a valid {"salient":false} (empty string, non-zero rc, or unparseable-as-JSON = FAILURE, not not-salient).
3. Retry a failed unit with backoff (small N, exponential) before giving up on it.
4. ABORT LOUD: if the running failure fraction exceeds a threshold (e.g. >25% of processed units returned FAILURE), stop the run with a non-zero exit + a clear diagnostic, rather than continuing to write a misleading near-0-draft manifest. A user must never get a clean 0-draft "success" that was actually a throttled run.

RED: Given a stubbed `claude` that returns empty / non-zero for a high fraction of calls  When the engine LLM salience pass runs over >=N gated units  Then the engine exits non-zero with a diagnostic naming the failure fraction (NOT a clean 0-draft manifest).

Files: lib/loom-mine-history.sh, lib/tests/loom-mine-history-claude-failure.test.sh
Relates-to: loom-rnxp (its clean full re-mine depends on this — a 3-hour mine must not silently fail). loom-b10 (the OTHER half of why the e2e dogfood was confusing — b10 fixed truncation; this fixes the silent-failure masking). Lineage: 2026-06-09 systematic-debug, diary d585eaefb3d4.
close_reason: Shipped (merge): _lmh_claude_salience() captures stderr + retries 3x backoff + classifies empty/rc!=0/unparseable as FAILURE distinct from {salient:false}; failure-fraction gate aborts return 4 past 25% over >=4 processed instead of writing a false 0-draft success. New test 7/7, full suite 89/89. Unblocks loom-rnxp.

title: script/test loop — canonical_commands.test 'bash lib/tests/*.test.sh' runs only 1 of 86 files (resolver false-green)
description:
Goal: loom's own test command false-greens — canonical_commands.test='bash lib/tests/*.test.sh' runs ONLY the first matched file (glob expands to positional args; bash runs file1, ignores the rest). Surfaced by dogfooding the oxs.5 /wrap-up resolver path: loom_resolve_command test executes rung-2 via bash -c "$cmd" -> runs 1/86, exits 0, looks green. A load-bearing false-green now that /wrap-up's test gate relies on the resolver. Fix: add an executable script/ test that loops over lib/tests/*.test.sh (exit non-zero on any failure) so resolver rung-1 wins; also point canonical_commands.test at script/test so the rung-2 fallback isn't a false-green either. loom adopts its OWN script/ convention (dogfood closure of epic loom-oxs).
Files: script/test, lib/tests/script-test-runs-full-suite.test.sh, .claude/project-constitution.md
RED: INVARIANT: running the project's resolved test command executes ALL lib/tests/*.test.sh files (failure in any propagates a non-zero exit), NOT just the first; a bare 'bash <glob>' that runs only file1 is forbidden.

title: loom's own mkdocs.yml missing pymdownx.emoji — front-page grid-card icons render as literal text
description:
On the live loom front page (https://fkberthold.github.io/loom/), the Diataxis grid cards in docs/index.md render their icons as LITERAL TEXT — e.g. ':material-school:{ .lg .middle }' and '[:octicons-arrow-right-24: Tutorials]' show verbatim instead of rendered SVG icons.

ROOT CAUSE: loom's OWN mkdocs.yml markdown_extensions is missing the 'pymdownx.emoji' extension. It HAS attr_list + md_in_html (so the '<div class="grid cards" markdown>' wrapper + '{ .lg .middle }' attrs work), but without pymdownx.emoji the ':material-*:' / ':octicons-*:' icon shortcodes fall through as plain text. Confirmed: pymdownx.emoji is the ONLY extension loom's mkdocs.yml lacks vs the template.

NOT a template bug: templates/diataxis/mkdocs.yml.template ALREADY carries the correct, complete block (loom-ad1, 2026-05-26, commit cda3730) — emoji_index: !!python/name:material.extensions.emoji.twemoji + emoji_generator: !!python/name:material.extensions.emoji.to_svg. loom's OWN instance just drifted behind its own template for ~2 weeks.

FIX: add the pymdownx.emoji block to loom's mkdocs.yml matching the template (material.extensions.emoji path is valid for the installed mkdocs-material 9.7.6; both material.extensions.emoji and the legacy materialx.emoji import OK, use the new path to match the template). Then redeploy and VERIFY the live site actually serves rendered icons (compose with the loom-7q1g docs-serving gate / loom-print-deploy-hint -> ./install.sh is unrelated; this needs a docs redeploy).
Files: mkdocs.yml
RED: INVARIANT: loom mkdocs.yml markdown_extensions includes a pymdownx.emoji block (emoji_index material.extensions.emoji.twemoji + emoji_generator material.extensions.emoji.to_svg); after 'mkdocs build --strict', site/index.html renders the grid-card icons as inline <svg> and the literal strings ':material-school:' / ':octicons-arrow-right-24:' do NOT appear in the rendered HTML.
Acceptance: mkdocs build --strict clean; rendered index.html has <svg> icons not literal shortcodes; live site reflects it after redeploy.
close_reason: Added pymdownx.emoji block to loom's own mkdocs.yml (material.extensions.emoji index+generator, matching the diataxis template loom-ad1/cda3730 from which loom's instance had drifted ~2wk). Front-page grid-card icons (:material-* x4 + :octicons-arrow-right-24: x4) now render as SVG instead of literal text: literal shortcodes 5->0, <svg> 14->22 (+8 card icons), mkdocs build --strict clean, suite 80/80. Live site fixes on next Deploy-docs run (this push). pymdownx.emoji was the SINGLE extension loom lacked vs the template. Drift guard tracked + unblocked: loom-p4tf. Merged 17f42ac.

title: Pin bd v1.0.4 global binary + validate-then-retire the loom-0ahj.1 canonicalizer backstop
description:
Follow-up to loom-0ahj.1 (closed): the byte-stable-export keystone shipped the lib/bd-canonical-export.sh canonicalizer but DID NOT upgrade the machine-global bd binary (v1.0.2), because doing so mid-wave would have disrupted the 3 concurrent dispatched workers. D8 frames the canonicalizer as a TEMPORARY remove-on-validation backstop. This bead: (1) upgrade bd to v1.0.4 PINNED (NOT @latest — some OPEN v1.0.4 server-mode regressions noted in C1 research); the upstream determinism fix (sort memory keys, beads #3474 + byte-stability regression #4086) is native in v1.0.4. (2) Smoke-test loom's hooks that shell out to bd (bd-merge-driver/post-rewrite/bd-worktree-preseed). (3) Once native byte-stability is confirmed across all export call sites, RETIRE the canonicalizer wrapper (or keep ONLY as the downstream-on-uncontrolled-bd backstop per D8). Files: install.sh, lib/bd-canonical-export.sh. Grounding: design doc 14f08e6d D8 + C1 research; closing drawer drawer_loom_decisions_088afaeaeef9a58c6675e320. RED: bd export byte-stable on v1.0.4 WITHOUT the canonicalizer.
AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE: machine-global — upgrades bd binary system-wide; central-only, would disrupt concurrent workers (has Files: but must NOT auto-dispatch)
notes: v1.0.4 validated: raw export byte-stable; canonicalizer made version-adaptive (--include-memories feature-detect); suite 80/80.
close_reason: VALIDATED then ROLLED BACK (attended, central-only/machine-global). Upgraded bd v1.0.2->v1.0.4 (prebuilt release, checksum-verified; go install blocked on missing ICU/CGO headers) + confirmed raw export byte-stable on v1.0.4. BUT discovered v1.0.4 auto-export EXCLUDES bd-remember memories by default with no working config (export.include-memories accepted-but-ignored; bd update stripped issues.jsonl 3->0 memory rows) — and loom commits memories INTO issues.jsonl. Determinism is ALREADY delivered by the canonicalizer on v1.0.2 (loom-0ahj.1's purpose), so v1.0.4 is net-negative; rolled back to v1.0.2 (backup). Salvaged + committed cbfa6cb: canonicalizer made version-ADAPTIVE (feature-detect --include-memories, ready for future v1.0.4) + new lib/tests/bd-export-determinism.test.sh (byte-stable + memory-retention, green on any bd) + install.sh advisory flipped to warn AGAINST v1.0.4+. Suite 80/80 on v1.0.2. Re-adoption + upstream report tracked in loom-xuif (blocked on upstream auto-export fix). Diary hsm7-WIP entry + capture drawer follow.

title: Move-3a/D7: scripts/loom-stage-spend — measure-first per-stage token reader
description:
Goal: Budgeting v1 = MEASURE-FIRST. A CENTRAL-SIDE transcript reader (NOT a hook — sidechain Stop/SubagentStop hooks do not fire, per loom-7zy/loom-98x) that walks the session's subagents/agent-*.jsonl, reads cache_read_input_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens (+ output) off each worker's first/last assistant records (loom-7zy proved these parseable), attributes spend per stage (test-author/implementer/verify — central knows dispatch order), and emits per-stage spend NET of the ~9-20K invariant SessionStart preamble (loom-nsb). Optionally tee a compact tally into workflow-state (additive, like the loom-0zr dispatched/inline counters) + surface in statusline. Sibling of scripts/loom-retro-prescan (reuse its transcript-walk engine).

title: [epic] substrate consolidation — n1sk churn-fix + lib/ DRY extraction + per-stage budgeting
description:
Goal: Implement the substrate-consolidation program from the /design-a-cycle (design-doc drawer drawer_loom_decisions_14f08e6d6b4c40f0a63fb9cf, Tier-0 GREEN, 9 decisions D1-D9), grounded in the /explore dogfood (exploration drawer drawer_loom_decisions_0bc491f815721dea8bef4f08) + two literature rounds + two research rounds (C1/C3).

Thesis: loom's leverage is its SUBSTRATE (hooks/ergonomics/plumbing), NOT its org chart — so perfect the substrate, add NO agent roles. Three moves: (1) kill the loom-n1sk export churn (D8: upgrade bd v1.0.2->v1.0.4, the determinism fix already shipped, + a thin canonicalizer backstop); (2) harvest DRY debt to lib/ (D2); (3) per-stage budgeting = tokens x model-tier x thinking-depth (D3/D5/D7), measure-first.

Decisions locked: D1 substrate>org-chart; D2 sequence (Move-1 -> {Move-2,Move-3a,Move-3b} -> D6); D3 inverted model-tier rule; D4 anti-scope protect content-bridge/Files:-fan-out/MemPalace; D5 adopt model-tier invariant + validate via telemetry; D6 capture fan-out via existing drawer-author+kg-extractor; D7 budgeting v1 = measure-first central-side reader; D8 bd upgrade + canonicalizer; D9 DEFER the bigger dual-store retirement to a separate cycle.

Absorbs: loom-n1sk (P1, superseded by Move-1). Related Move-3 increments: loom-li8h (bg-dispatch default), loom-u9es (D6 companion). Adjacent (separate): loom-7q1g.
Source-of-truth: design doc 14f08e6d.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Wrap-up docs-deploy SERVING gate for full-Diataxis projects (verify the site serves, not just build-green)
description:
Goal: On full-Diataxis projects, wrap-up / session-close must verify docs ACTUALLY SERVE — not just that the build is green. Motivated by a live silent-green outage (2026-06-08): every Deploy-docs run succeeded AND gh-pages was a valid site, but https://fkberthold.github.io/loom/ 404'd for an unknown period because GitHub Pages was DISABLED. Neither 'mkdocs build --strict' NOR session-startup step 1c (which only flags RED Deploy-docs runs) caught it. Fixed live by enabling Pages; this bead prevents recurrence.

The gate must check three layers: (1) mkdocs build --strict (build integrity); (2) latest Deploy-docs run conclusion=success; (3) the site ACTUALLY SERVES — curl -sI <site> -> HTTP 200 (or Pages API status=built / not 404). Layer 3 is the new part that catches dead-Pages/silent-green.

Surfaced: Frank 2026-06-08 item 3. Root-cause finding: drawer drawer_loom_decisions_c3994a92d4bfb6d5a0e41981 (Finding A). Composes with session-startup step 1c (red-run detection).

Files: skills/bead-lifecycle-shell/SKILL.md (phase D / wrap-up) or a wrap-up skill, scripts/loom-docs-serving-check (new helper), skills/session-startup/SKILL.md (optional 1c extension), docs/, lib/tests/
RED: INVARIANT: the docs-deploy gate verifies actual SERVING (HTTP 200 / Pages status built), not merely mkdocs build success, and only fires on full-Diataxis projects (docs/ + mkdocs.yml present, no docs/.no-diataxis); INFO/nudge per loom posture.
Acceptance: a wrap-up stage verifies serving; a test pins the curl-200 / Pages-status check + the full-Diataxis gating + graceful skip on non-Diataxis projects.
close_reason: Shipped scripts/loom-docs-serving-check (3-layer: mkdocs --strict + Deploy-docs CI success + actual SERVING via curl HTTP 200 / GitHub Pages API status=built) gated to full-Diataxis projects, INFO/nudge never-blocks, degrades offline; wired into bead-lifecycle-shell phase D1b wrap-up + session-startup 1c pointer. RED 0/9→9/0. Live run on loom site all-green. Merged 7a91cd6.

title: Make run_in_background the documented default for dispatched workers
description:
Goal: Central currently dispatches agents in the FOREGROUND and holds the turn idle until they return (observed all session 2026-06-08: the parallel wave + both /dispatch-middle pipelines). This contradicts dispatch-v2's lean-central goal — central should YIELD the turn and resume on each agent's completion event (run_in_background:true), free to converse/plan/stage meanwhile. Make background dispatch the documented DEFAULT in the dispatch skills + conventions; foreground reserved for 'next step is immediate integration with nothing else interleavable.'

Surfaced: Frank 2026-06-08 — 'Is there a reason we're spinning up agents and just waiting on them, instead of backgrounding them and letting their return trigger an event?' Live hazard that motivates it: foreground-wait + the harness auto-backgrounding long loops produced TWO suite runs racing (the loom-fx9m close detour); a TaskStop'd suite task left orphan bd-post-rewrite child processes that raced on git/bd state -> false 63/2 suite result. So document the orphan-process / concurrency caution too (never run two full-suite loops in one repo at once; TaskStop may not reap grandchildren).

Files: skills/dispatch-middle/SKILL.md, skills/bead-lifecycle-shell/SKILL.md, .claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md, CLAUDE.md, lib/tests/
RED: INVARIANT: the dispatch skills/conventions document run_in_background as the DEFAULT dispatch mode (foreground reserved for immediate-integration), citing lean-central, plus the no-two-suite-loops / orphan-process concurrency caution.
Acceptance: dispatch skills + conventions state the background-default with rationale; a grep-contract test pins it; suite green.
close_reason: Documented run_in_background:true as the DEFAULT dispatch mode (foreground reserved for immediate-integration) across dispatch-middle + bead-lifecycle-shell SKILLs, .claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md, CLAUDE.md; cites dispatch-v2 lean-central + the no-two-suite-loops/orphan-process (TaskStop grandchildren) concurrency caution. New grep-contract test 20/0. Merged 42aca05.

title: [epic] /explore — collaborative-exploration primitive (upstream of /design-a-cycle)
description:
Goal: New upstream collaborative-exploration primitive /explore — the front-door to /design-a-cycle (which is the front-door to create-beads). When the user has a nascent idea, /explore blends FOUR source tiers (T1 self-reasoning on current context, T2 repo+docs dig, T3 web common-usage, T4 peer-reviewed literature) in a HYBRID loop (light tiers in-thread; heavy tiers dispatched as a deep-research round when the conversation calls for one; central synthesizes back) to converge WITH the user on shared understanding. Tracked + resumable via ONE light exploration drawer (state header; session-startup discoverable); NO soundness gate (deliberately lighter than design-a-cycle). Two user-declared exits: REST (drawer stands as a standing understanding artifact) or PROMOTE (/design-a-cycle decisions grounded_in this drawer).

Design source-of-truth: drawer_loom_decisions_2ee82f47ed6bc219866cd5c4 (brainstorm checkpoint 2026-06-08; forks F1-F4 locked via AskUserQuestion: positioning=new-upstream-primitive / loop=hybrid / lifecycle=tracked-resumable-light / exit=user-declared-two-soft-exits).

Lineage: mirrors design-a-cycle substrate-as-state + session-startup-discovery (loom-tdua) in a lighter form; honors dispatch-v2 lean-central discipline (loom-5m94); tag-not-room precedent from loom-mine-history (provenance:mined).

Children: T1 template [foundation] -> {T2 skill (dep T1), T3 session-startup scan, T4 CLAUDE.md+docs} -> T6 dogfood (dep T1-T4). Standalone sibling T5 (deep-research peer-review default, P3, separable).
close_reason: EPIC COMPLETE — /explore collaborative-exploration primitive shipped + dogfooded. All 5 children closed: .1 template, .2 orchestrator (via content-bridge /dispatch-middle), .3 session-startup scan, .4 CLAUDE.md+docs, .5 dogfood. /explore is live/installed/documented and PROVEN — its first real run produced loom's substrate-consolidation roadmap (design cycle 14f08e6d). title: bd-close-capture hook false-positives on the literal close-phrase inside a --description (sibling of loom-9ng)
description:
hooks/bd-close-capture.sh is a PreToolUse Bash hook that parses bead IDs from commands invoking the close subcommand. It matches the two-word close-phrase as a substring of the whole command string, so when that phrase appears INSIDE a --description (or any non-close context) of a DIFFERENT command (e.g. bd create --description=...the close subcommand...), the hook fires, fails to parse a bead ID, and ABORTS the legitimate command with 'Could not parse bead ID'. HIT LIVE 2026-06-08 filing loom-n1sk: the create aborted because the description text contained the close-phrase; refiled by de-literalizing the wording. This is the exact sibling of loom-9ng (edit-after-failure-guard false-positived on the '0 failed' substring) — a hook matching a bare substring where it should match a command-shape. FIX: anchor the detection to the command actually INVOKING the close subcommand (e.g. parse argv where the subcommand token is 'close', not a substring match anywhere in the command), so the phrase appearing in a --description/-m/string arg of another command does not trigger. RED test: a bd create whose --description contains the close-phrase must NOT be intercepted; an actual close-subcommand invocation must still be parsed.
Files: hooks/bd-close-capture.sh, lib/tests/bd-close-capture.test.sh
close_reason: Reproduced + fixed: bd-close-capture.sh false-positived on a line-anchored \n...bd close regex matching the close-phrase inside a quoted --description (sibling of loom-9ng substring-vs-command-shape class). Fix: single authoritative shlex argv pass — detection now requires an adjacent bd→close token pair in real command position; quoted values stay single tokens. Test 41/2→43/0 (+5 cases: single/multi-line desc, git commit -m, genuine-close + chained-close regression). Merged 53ac5bb.

title: /dispatch-middle skill step 2/3 worktree-sharing mechanism doesn't match Agent tool reality (auto-named isolated worktrees can't be shared)
description:
SURFACED BY THE loom-t0l3 DOGFOOD (2026-06-07). The /dispatch-middle SKILL.md step 2 says 'Agent with isolation: worktree, pointed at frank/<bead>' and step 3 says 'Agent, pointed at the same worktree' so the implementer sees the test-author's committed RED test on disk. But the Agent tool's isolation:worktree AUTO-NAMES the worktree (.claude/worktrees/agent-<hash>) and gives no way to target a named branch or REUSE a prior agent's worktree — two isolation:worktree calls get two DIFFERENT worktrees branched from main, so the implementer would NOT see the test-author's RED commit.

WHAT ACTUALLY WORKS (used in the dogfood): central creates the shared worktree MANUALLY ('git worktree add .claude/worktrees/dm-<bead> -b frank/<bead> main'), then dispatches BOTH agents withOUT isolation:worktree, each instructed to 'cd .claude/worktrees/dm-<bead>' as its first bash step and use relative paths thereafter. The implementer then reads the test-author's committed RED test on disk in the shared tree. (Alternative: dispatch the test-author with isolation:worktree, capture its returned worktree path from the completion notification's <worktree> field, then point the implementer's cd at that path.)

FIX: reconcile skills/dispatch-middle/SKILL.md steps 1-3 with one of these real mechanisms (prefer the manual-shared-worktree+cd one — central controls the path, both agents join it). Update the brief templates' STEP 0 to include the 'cd into the shared worktree' instruction + the 'this pipeline runs no bd; bd-list smoke warning is non-blocking' note. Extend lib/tests/dispatch-middle-contract.test.sh to pin the corrected mechanism.

Also note (minor): the dispatch-nudge hook fires an advisory on the dispatched worker's own Edits ('you are hand-editing in the central session') — harmless but noisy inside a /dispatch-middle worker; consider suppressing when already inside a dispatched worktree.

Files: skills/dispatch-middle/SKILL.md, lib/tests/dispatch-middle-contract.test.sh

Follow-up to loom-t0l3 (dogfood). The pipeline SEMANTICS (independent test-author→implementer, implementer sees test-as-artifact-only) are validated + correct; only the worktree-plumbing prose is wrong.
notes:
DOGFOOD EVIDENCE #2 (2026-06-08, building loom-ld1q.2 /explore orchestrator via /dispatch-middle): a THIRD mechanism was used + validated — the CONTENT-BRIDGE. Test-author runs in its OWN isolation:worktree, writes the RED test, and RETURNS the verbatim test-file content. The implementer runs in a SEPARATE fresh isolation:worktree (off main), briefed with that verbatim content: 'create the test file with EXACTLY this content, confirm RED, then implement to GREEN, do not weaken it.' Independence invariant HELD (implementer never saw the author's reasoning — only the artifact). Result: contract 12/12, no shared worktree needed.

TRADEOFF vs the two mechanisms in the description:
(+) BOTH agents keep full isolation:worktree -> ZERO main-leak risk. The manual-shared-worktree+cd mechanism runs agents WITHOUT isolation, so a forgotten 'cd' writes into MAIN.
(+) No worktree-path plumbing / no completion-notification <worktree> capture.
(-) Central RELAYS the test text through the implementer's brief -> central's context carries the full test (~132 lines here); for a LARGE test this re-imports some of the 'central accumulates churn' cost dispatch-v2 fought. Plus verbatim-transcription-fidelity risk (mitigated here: central re-ran the test post-merge + grepped assertion count).

=> The fix now has THREE candidate mechanisms with an ISOLATION-SAFETY vs CENTRAL-CONTEXT tradeoff. Candidate resolution: document content-bridge as DEFAULT for small/medium tests (isolation safety wins, no main-leak) and manual-shared-worktree for large tests (keeps central lean). USER TO DECIDE canonical default before the SKILL.md rewrite.
close_reason: content-bridge documented as DEFAULT /dispatch-middle worktree mechanism. contract 40/40, full suite 65/0 (verified clean — earlier 63/2 was orphan-process concurrency from a TaskStop'd suite task, not a regression; both tests 25/0+14/0 in clean loop). Built THROUGH the content-bridge (dogfood). merged 9785f03

title: T2: bead-lifecycle-shell — test-author→implementer middle, central writes nothing
description:
Goal: Rewrite bead-lifecycle-shell's dispatch discipline so the variable middle is the test-author -> implementer split via /dispatch-middle, and central writes NOTHING in the middle.
Files: skills/bead-lifecycle-shell/SKILL.md
Update the Dispatch-discipline section: (1) the variable middle's default is /dispatch-middle (test-author then implementer, different agents) — NOT 'one worker does RED->GREEN' (the loom-yb5 model), NOT inline. (2) sharpen the CENTRAL/WORKER line: central does ONLY conversation + contract-lock (M1 = user dialogue) + integration (verify/merge/close/capture, cwd-sensitive + bd-authoritative); workers do RED-test + GREEN-code + research + review, each with MINIMAL scoped context. (3) the friction-inversion rationale: dispatch is now a single cheap command, so inline's only remaining justification is the mechanical threshold (<=15 lines / single non-test file / no new test) — and even that PREFERS /dispatch-middle. (4) cross-ref the dispatch field + the updated nudge (T3).
Steps: 1. Rewrite the section. 2. Reconcile/extend any phase-B test (lib/tests/bead-lifecycle-shell-phase-b.test.sh) that pins the old dispatch prose. 3. Full loom suite.
Acceptance: shell states the test-author->implementer-via-/dispatch-middle default + central-writes-nothing + the sharpened line; suite green.
DEPENDS ON T1 (references /dispatch-middle). File-disjoint from T3/T4 -> parallel-wave member.

title: T1: /dispatch-middle command + skill (test-author→implementer pipeline)
description:
Goal: The /dispatch-middle command + skill — the low-friction primitive that orchestrates a bead's variable middle as a test-author -> implementer (-> optional verify) pipeline of INDEPENDENT subagents, so central invokes once and writes nothing. The friction-inversion lever (make dispatch cheaper than inline).
Files: commands/dispatch-middle.md, skills/dispatch-middle/SKILL.md, lib/tests/dispatch-middle-contract.test.sh
The skill scripts central's sequence: (1) ensure a worktree frank/<bead>; (2) dispatch TEST-AUTHOR (Agent isolation:worktree) with a brief built from ONLY the locked CONTRACT (the bead's RED: line / M1 spec / acceptance) + the interface under test + the smoke battery + 'write the RED test that pins the contract, commit it, return the failure verbatim; do NOT implement'; (3) dispatch IMPLEMENTER (Agent, SAME worktree) with a brief containing ONLY the RED test file path + the code area + 'make the RED test pass with the minimal change; do NOT modify/weaken the test; if the test looks wrong, STOP and report to central'; receive GREEN counts; (4) OPTIONAL verifier (requesting-code-review / code-reviewer agent); (5) hand back a summary so central does verify+merge+close+capture. Ship the TWO brief templates (test-author + implementer) with contract/RED-test slots + the context-scoping rule (central extracts the minimal slice per brief; no session-history dump). commands/dispatch-middle.md = disable-model-invocation user door, pass-through <bead>.
Steps: 1. Author skills/dispatch-middle/SKILL.md (the pipeline prose + 2 brief templates + worktree-sharing + independence + context-scoping) + commands/dispatch-middle.md. 2. Grep-contract test lib/tests/dispatch-middle-contract.test.sh: the skill documents the test-author->implementer sequence, the 'implementer does NOT author/modify the test' independence rule, the context-scoping discipline, the central-writes-nothing posture, and the hand-back-for-merge. 3. install.sh + verify invocable. 4. Full loom suite.
Acceptance: skill orchestrates the 2-agent split with independent context; brief templates present; independence + context-scoping documented; installed + invocable; suite green.
FOUNDATION (blocks T2/T3/T4). Interlocks loom-tdua: the contract slot = the bead's RED: line.

title: Dispatch architecture v2: /dispatch-middle test-author→implementer pipeline (central writes nothing)
description:
Goal: Get loom to push ALL subagent-able work to subagents, keeping the MAIN thread for planning + executive function with the user. Closes 3 problems with central doing work in-thread (Frank, 2026-06-07): (1) eats context needed for planning/executive function; (2) test-writer and code-writer become the SAME agent — a demonstrated anti-pattern (no independent verification; the test becomes a tautology shaped by the implementation); (3) central accumulates random unrelated context. Extends loom-yb5 (dispatch posture nudge+fan-out+worker-default).

REFRAME: loom-yb5 was a PUSH (nudge pressures toward dispatch); the deeper fix is a PULL — make dispatch LOWER-FRICTION than inline so the right thing is the easy thing. And the NEW piece loom-yb5 never addressed: SPLIT the variable middle's test-author and implementer into DIFFERENT agents.

Architecture: /dispatch-middle <bead> = a command+skill scripting central's 2-(or-3-)agent pipeline; central invokes once and WRITES NOTHING in the middle. One shared worktree (frank/<bead>), sequential INDEPENDENT agents: TEST-AUTHOR (briefed with ONLY the locked contract + interface -> RED test, commit, return failure verbatim) -> IMPLEMENTER (briefed with ONLY the RED-test-as-FILE + code area; 'make it pass, do NOT modify the test, stop-and-report if the test looks wrong' -> GREEN, commit; NEVER sees the author's reasoning, inherits the test as an ARTIFACT not a shared mind) -> optional VERIFIER (existing requesting-code-review) -> hand back to central for verify+merge+close+capture. Clean line: central = conversation + contract-lock (M1=user dialogue) + integration; workers = RED-test + GREEN-code + research + review, each with minimal context.

COMPOSITION: /dispatch-middle owns the WITHIN-bead test/code split; loom-yb5 fan-out detector owns ACROSS-bead parallelism (multiple beads each via /dispatch-middle). The dispatch-nudge hook is UPDATED to point at /dispatch-middle (push+pull). INTERLOCK with loom-tdua (design-phase epic): the test-author's contract IS the bead's RED: line (design emits contracts -> dispatch consumes them).

Children: T1 /dispatch-middle command+skill [foundation]; T2 bead-lifecycle-shell rewrite [dep T1]; T3 dispatch-nudge hook update [dep T1]; T4 bugfix/feature recipe M-step updates [dep T1]; T5 dogfood [dep T1-T4]. T1 -> {T2,T3,T4} 3-wide file-disjoint wave -> T5. DEFERRED (not v1): Workflow-tool multi-bead fan-out version, mandatory-verifier, context-scoping lint.
close_reason: Dispatch architecture v2 COMPLETE. T1 loom-0k68 (/dispatch-middle cmd+skill), T2 loom-6bv1 (bead-lifecycle-shell central-writes-nothing), T3 loom-l95f (dispatch-nudge→/dispatch-middle + test-file trigger), T4 loom-8crd (bugfix/feature M3/M4 RED-author vs GREEN-implementer split), T5 loom-t0l3 (dogfood: /dispatch-middle run on loom-9ng, independence verified). The friction-inversion pull primitive is live + dogfood-validated.

Precipitate the three prose research drawers (loom-l0f 010d480b, loom-5w6 ed1f4600, loom-dwn d673e1e5) + the build-brainstorm checkpoint (957e6a40) into the layered substrate the system prescribes: an L1 KG spine (design-predicate triples linking the decisions) + a proper L2 DESIGN DOC drawer (with the state-header + locked-decisions + grounding) for the design-phase-system itself. Precipitate the locked decisions (two-tier soundness, layered substrate, above-bead orchestrator, etc.) into KG triples + a consolidated L2 design-doc drawer with the state header. 3. description:

title: T2: /design-a-cycle orchestrator skill + command
description:
Goal: The /design-a-cycle orchestrator skill + command — the above-bead campaign/arc primitive that drives the Plan->Research->Architect cadence over the layered substrate and hands off to create-beads. NOT a bead-lifecycle recipe; it ORCHESTRATES (spawns research-a-beads + an implementation epic) and is itself stateless beyond the substrate.
Files: skills/design-a-cycle/SKILL.md, commands/design-a-cycle.md
The skill: on invocation, READ the design-substrate STATE (the L2 design-doc drawer header + L1 KG) for <topic>; if none, scaffold one from templates/design-doc/ into the project wing. Then drive ONE cadence step (or loop), proposing the next action from state: Plan (invoke brainstorming to set/refine direction) -> Research (for each open [CLARIFICATION] marker that needs grounding, spawn a research-a-bead and record its ID in the header) -> Architect (precipitate each newly-locked decision into L1 KG triples [soft recommended design-predicate set] + the L2 Decisions-locked section + optional L3 executable spec) -> SOUNDNESS check (Tier-0: no unresolved markers + every locked decision cites grounding + constitution-consistent; Tier-1 optional) -> loop until green -> HANDOFF: run create-beads against the locked decisions to spawn the implementation epic; each bead from a Tier-1 decision carries a RED: line (its Given-When-Then / invariant) parallel to the Files: line. command = disable-model-invocation user door.
Steps: 1. Author SKILL.md (cadence + soundness + precipitate + handoff prose) + command. 2. Light contract/grep test that the skill documents the cadence steps + soundness gate + RED:-handoff (prose-skill test pattern). 3. install.sh re-run + verify invocable. 4. Full loom suite.
Acceptance: skill drives the cadence from substrate-state; soundness gate prose present; handoff emits RED: lines; installed + invocable.
DEPENDS ON T1 (references templates/design-doc/). File-disjoint from T3/T4.

title: Design-phase system: /design-a-cycle above-bead orchestrator + layered substrate (v1)
description:
Goal: A loom design-phase system — an above-bead /design-a-cycle ORCHESTRATOR (a new campaign/arc unit, NOT a bead) that drives a Plan->Research->Architect cadence over a LAYERED living-design-substrate, with two-tier soundness, handing off to create-beads. Closes the long-identified gap: loom is implementation-anchored (bead = verified change, RED->GREEN), but the design phase produces decisions/understanding with no red-green test and had no loom home. GENERAL loom use, not just dreamer-engine.

Design source: brainstorm checkpoint drawer_loom_decisions_957e6a400c44af2cdc654670; research chain 010d480b82d00234d1e953b9 (loom-l0f survey) -> ed1f460072b7ede5711a3e21 (loom-5w6 two-tier soundness) -> d673e1e52ae1696f6d8e955f (loom-dwn layered KG-spine substrate).

Architecture (v1 = recipe + convention + 1 template): SUBSTRATE 3 layers (all MemPalace/existing) — L1 KG spine (source-of-truth; soft recommended design-predicate set: supersedes_design_of/grounded_in/emits_bead/soundness_tier/depends_on_invariant) + L2 design-doc drawer (templates/design-doc/ skeleton with a structured STATE HEADER above prose) + L3 optional executable specs (Given-When-Then / invariant-as-prose -> bead RED test). CADENCE: read-state -> Plan (brainstorming) -> Research (spawn research-a-beads) -> Architect (precipitate locked decisions into L1+L2+optional L3) -> soundness (Tier-0 coherence: no-open-markers + grounding + constitution-consistent; Tier-1 optional) -> loop until green -> handoff (create-beads implementation epic; Tier-1 decisions' beads carry a RED: line). STATE = substrate-as-state (no new bd entity); DISCOVERY = session-startup surfaces active design cycles. POSTURE: opinionated about the structured destination (L1/L3) + cadence; permissive about the prose reasoning surface (L2); generative about human Diataxis (deferred).

DEFERRED follow-ups (NOT v1, dogfood-gated by T5): scripts/loom-render-design (L1->L2 projection), hooks/design-drift-check.sh (drawer<->KG reconciliation), Diataxis-scaffold-from-KG generator.

Children: T1 templates/design-doc/ skeleton [foundation]; T2 /design-a-cycle skill+command [dep T1]; T3 session-startup discovery; T4 CLAUDE.md conventions; T5 dogfood-recapture-own-design [dep T1-T4]. T1/T3/T4 file-disjoint -> parallel wave.
close_reason: Design-phase system v1 COMPLETE. T1 loom-dhra (templates/design-doc L2 template), T2 loom-k0s8 (/design-a-cycle orchestrator skill+command), T3 loom-9s7x (session-startup active-cycle discovery), T4 loom-sbth (CLAUDE.md design-cycle conventions), T5 loom-70ye (dogfood: recaptured its own design into the layered substrate). The above-bead campaign/arc primitive + layered KG-spine substrate is live, installed, and dogfood-validated. Interlocks loom-5m94 (dispatch-v2) via the RED: line contract.

title: Research: agent-optimal vs human-optimal representation for design specs/research docs (structured/KG/compressed/formal vs prose) + the agent-substrate→Diátaxis generation model
description:
Extends loom-l0f + loom-5w6 (design-phase system research; drawers 010d480b + ed1f460072). Frank's reframe: this design-phase capability is for GENERAL loom use, not just dreamer-engine. KEY INSIGHT he raised: the prior two research passes surveyed only HUMAN-readable formats (Spec-Kit, OpenSpec, ADR, Gherkin, TLA+ — all markdown/prose for a person). But "optimal for a HUMAN to read" != "optimal for an AGENT to read and MAINTAIN." Loom needs BOTH: (1) human-facing Diataxis docs (all 4 quadrants), generated + kept in sync with every software change, optimized for human reading; (2) an AGENT-FACING working substrate — the research docs + design specs the AGENT reads and maintains for its OWN use — optimized for the agent, NOT human-optimal. The popular tools won't answer this. Goal: ground what representation is optimal for an agent to work from.

Operational question: What does current research/practice say about the representation an LLM/coding-agent reads and MAINTAINS most effectively for design specs + research docs + living architecture — as distinct from human-readable docs? And what does that imply for loom's two-representation model (agent-substrate as source-of-truth + Diataxis as a generated human projection), and for WHERE on loom's existing spectrum (prose-drawer -> AAAK-compressed -> KG-triples) the agent-optimal spec should sit?

FRAMING: loom is opinionated-on-process. The proposal forming: loom should be OPINIONATED about the agent-substrate format (a specific structured representation the agent maintains) and GENERATIVE about the human docs (render Diataxis FROM the substrate). The agent-substrate must be: token-efficient, structured/unambiguous, queryable/retrievable (not a monolithic doc), and cheap for an agent to UPDATE-IN-PLACE (vs re-weaving prose). Diataxis (human) is downstream-generated. This deepens the loom-l0f/5w6 "drawer-home, Diataxis-downstream" resolution with the real reason: agent-optimal != human-optimal -> they must be different artifacts with a generation relationship.

DIRECTIONS TO RESEARCH (M3, current-year filter; for each: what it is + source + does it favor STRUCTURED over PROSE for agent consumption + does it support cheap agent MAINTENANCE/update + retrieval/queryability):
1. Structured vs unstructured/prose context for LLMs: does the research show LLMs read+reason better from structured (JSON/YAML/typed-schema/tables) vs narrative prose? Token cost, ambiguity, instruction-following. (e.g. structured prompting, schema-guided generation, any empirical comparisons.)
2. Knowledge-graph grounding for agents: GraphRAG (Microsoft), KG-RAG, structured retrieval; KG-as-spec. Does a graph representation beat a doc for agent retrieval + maintenance? loom already has the MemPalace KG — is the SPEC better as graph than prose?
3. Agent memory / context-engineering architectures: how do agent frameworks store + retrieve + UPDATE their own working knowledge (vs human docs)? MemGPT/Letta, generative-agents memory, "context engineering" (the shift from prompt-eng), reflection/maintenance loops. What's the state of the art for an agent maintaining its OWN evolving knowledge artifact?
4. Spec formats for code-generation AGENTS specifically: what spec representation makes agentic code-gen most reliable? (spec-driven-dev for agents, intent specs, formal-ish specs as agent input, "specs as the new code" current-year discourse.)
5. Emerging agent-doc CONVENTIONS: llms.txt (llmstxt.org), AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md/cursor-rules, "docs for agents" — are these just human-markdown-relabeled, or genuinely agent-optimized? What do they get right/wrong about agent-vs-human representation?
6. Token-efficient / semantic compression for agent consumption: AAAK-style entity-coded compression, prompt compression (LLMLingua etc.), summarization-for-recall. Is a compressed/encoded spec more agent-optimal than full prose, and at what readability cost?
7. Formal/unambiguous-for-agents: does giving an agent a more FORMAL spec (typed contracts, invariants, state machines) reduce hallucination/re-derivation vs prose? Ties to loom-5w6 Tier-1 (executable specs). Is formality MORE agent-optimal even though humans find it harder?

M2 (internal): loom/HAW prior art on agent-vs-human representation — AAAK spec (the diary compression), the KG, the drawer format, any prior decision on "agent-optimal docs"; CLAUDE.md/rules as agent-facing; does any sibling project already separate agent-substrate from human-docs?

SYNTHESIZE (M4, opinionated): (a) what the research says is agent-OPTIMAL for a maintained spec/design artifact (structured? graph? compressed? formal? hybrid?) and WHY, distinct from human-optimal; (b) the recommended SHAPE of loom's agent-substrate (where on prose-drawer->AAAK->KG spectrum; one format or layered?); (c) the generation relationship (how the human Diataxis is rendered from the agent-substrate, and whether that's realistic to keep in sync); (d) does this CHANGE the loom-l0f/5w6 conclusions (e.g. is the living-design-doc actually a KG/structured artifact rather than a prose status-drawer?); (e) the product/process implication (what loom should be OPINIONATED about vs GENERATIVE about). Relate to loom-l0f composite + loom-5w6 two-tier soundness.

DELIVERABLE (M5, draft for central): decision-drawer body (extends the loom-l0f/5w6 chain) + 3-5 KG triples + follow-up bead candidates. Keep source URLs inline. NO code. Be opinionated; flag unverified; explicitly call out where the popular human-doc tools are the WRONG model for the agent-substrate.
close_reason: Research output (extends loom-l0f/5w6). CONFIRMS the layered hypothesis + sharpens it. The living-design-document is a 3-LAYER substrate: L1 MemPalace KG = structured SPINE / source-of-truth (queryable, update-in-place; already carries design predicates); L2 prose drawer = working-surface/rendering (DEMOTED from source-of-truth); L3 = loom-5w6 Tier-1 executable specs. Diátaxis renders downstream (scaffold+drift-signals from L1, prose authored-into — NOT full auto-render). KEY GUARDRAIL: structured-beats-prose holds for READ/RETRIEVE/UPDATE but HURTS constrained GENERATION (Let Me Speak Freely, arXiv 2408.02442) → reason-in-prose(L2), precipitate-into-structure(L1/L3); loom OPINIONATED about the structured destination, PERMISSIVE about the reasoning surface. SHARPEST NEGATIVE: AGENTS.md/llms.txt/cursor-rules are the WRONG model (minimal operational context, exclude architecture). REVISES loom-l0f #2 (living-doc != single prose drawer). Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_d673e1e52ae1696f6d8e955f + 5 KG + 5 follow-up candidates.

title: Research: spec↔test↔docs convergence (BDD, property/model-based, TLA+, Diátaxis-as-spec) — refine loom design-phase soundness + living-doc home
description:
Extends loom-l0f (design-phase system research, drawer_loom_decisions_010d480b82d00234d1e953b9). Frank pushed on the SOUNDNESS model: the first pass concluded "fitness functions = BUILD layer; design soundness = coherence checklist." Frank surfaces three test<->spec / design<->docs boundary patterns that may REFINE that. Research each + synthesize the implications for the loom design-phase system.

Operational question: Do the spec-as-test patterns (BDD, property-based, model-based/formal) provide a richer DESIGN-phase soundness model than the coherence-checklist -- specifically by being specs that are ALSO executable, authored during design and verified during build (bridging design->build) -- and does Diataxis-as-spec (doc-first) answer where the living design doc should live (MemPalace drawer vs repo file)?

FRAMING (Frank's): the three thoughts are the three boundaries around the design phase -- spec-cap-test (behavioral)=BDD; spec-cap-test (structural/high-level)=property/model-based; spec-cap-docs=Diataxis-as-spec. The loom design-phase system sits in the middle; the question is which boundaries to collapse.

DIRECTIONS (M3 -- for each: what it is + canonical source + how it fits loom's 5 ethos axes + at what ALTITUDE it operates):
1. BDD as test-AND-spec: Gherkin / Given-When-Then, Cucumber "living documentation", Specification by Example (Gojko Adzic). Behavioral-contract specs authored during design that become executable acceptance tests at build. ASSESS: does the design artifact EMIT the RED acceptance tests the implementation beads turn GREEN? Altitude = behavior/use-cases, NOT architecture structure -- name that limit.
2. Property-based + model-based + formal specification at HIGH level: QuickCheck (Claessen/Hughes) + Hypothesis (properties-as-specs); model-based testing (abstract model as spec+oracle); TLA+ (Lamport) and Alloy (Jackson) -- formal DESIGN specs that are model-checkable BEFORE building. These specify invariants/models/laws = the architecture altitude. ASSESS: do these REVISE loom-l0f's "fitness functions = build only"? I.e. is the architectural invariant/model a DESIGN artifact authored at design time that is also checkable? Where do TLA+/Alloy fit loom (too heavyweight for a markdown+bash ethos? or the formal end of a spectrum loom can gesture at without adopting)?
3. Diataxis-as-spec / doc-first: README-Driven Development (Tom Preston-Werner), doc-driven development, design-docs-as-spec. In some orgs Diataxis Reference/Explanation is written FIRST as a spec; in others pure downstream. ASSESS: should the loom living-design-document BE a doc-first Diataxis Explanation page (a repo file that matures into shipped docs) vs a MemPalace status-drawer -- this answers loom-l0f fork-2 (living-doc home). Loom currently treats Diataxis as downstream (docs-scaffold, loom-9z1).

Also (M2 internal): does loom/HAW already touch BDD, property-based testing, TLA+/formal methods, or doc-first? (HAW is a live system -- check both wings + diary.)

SYNTHESIZE (M4): (a) how each refines the loom design-phase soundness model; (b) whether design soundness should include EXECUTABLE specs at multiple altitudes -- BDD (behavioral) + property/model (structural) -- that bridge design->build by emitting the bead RED tests; (c) the REVISED view on fitness-functions-at-design-time; (d) the living-doc-home recommendation (MemPalace drawer vs doc-first Diataxis vs both, with the drawer-vs-repo precedence rule in mind); (e) a one-paragraph spec<->test<->docs boundary map for loom. Relate everything back to the loom-l0f composite (Spec-Kit shape + ADR-as-drawers + create-beads handoff + constitution-gate).

DELIVERABLE (M5, draft for central to file): decision-drawer body (extends 010d480b; question / answer-up-front / per-direction fit + altitude / revised-soundness-model / living-doc-home recommendation / WHY-NOT / OPEN / lineage) + 3-5 KG triple proposals + follow-up bead candidates (title + one-line scope). Keep source URLs inline. NO code, no worktree. Be opinionated; flag anything unverified.
close_reason: Research output (extends loom-l0f). Changes TWO loom-l0f conclusions: (1) design soundness is TWO-TIER — Tier-0 coherence (floor, always) + Tier-1 OPTIONAL executable-spec emission at behavioral (BDD Given-When-Then) + structural (invariant/property) altitudes that bridge design->build by emitting the bead RED test; (2) fitness functions DO have a design-time role — the INVARIANT is a design artifact, only the CHECK is build-time (loom-l0f conflated authoring-time with runtime). Living-doc home RESOLVED: MemPalace status-drawer authoritative-on-intent, doc-first repo file rejected as home / downstream-render-only (RDD anti-waterfall). MAJOR internal finding: the 3 patterns are ALREADY LIVE in sibling HAW projects (OpenSpec malleus/e2e, rapid malleus, TLA+/Apalache sir/liza/tla_puzzles). Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_ed1f460072b7ede5711a3e21 + 4 KG + 5 follow-up candidates.

title: Research: design-phase system that fits loom's tools + ethos (ADR/RFC/Shape-Up/arc42/fitness-functions survey)
description:
Goal: Determine what design/architecture-PHASE system best fits loom's tools (Claude Code + beads + MemPalace + recipes/hooks) and ethos (decision-capture, hooks-enforce, bead=verified-change, living MemPalace substrate), for the Plan->Research->Architect loop whose Architect step is an EVOLVING DESIGN DOCUMENT (Frank's framing, drawer_loom_decisions_af817d22ba51f9a69be86703). Loom is implementation-anchored (bead = verified change, RED->GREEN); the design phase produces understanding/decisions with NO red-green test and currently has no loom home. Survey external prior art, assess fit against the loom ethos, and synthesize the minimal loom-native shape.

Operational question: Which existing design-phase methodology/artifact-pattern(s) map cleanly onto MemPalace-as-living-substrate + bead-handoff, and what is the minimal loom-native shape (a recipe + a capture primitive + a soundness/verification analog) for the Plan->Research->Architect loop?

INTERNAL prior art to check (M2): loom/decisions (design-phase? constitution loom-8jz/ld4? Diataxis loom-9z1 Explanation quadrant?), hundred_acre_woods/decisions, the loom retrospective-dig (loom-z3m), bd memories, the claude-opus diary. NOTE: A1 quick scan found NO prior loom decision on a design-phase system -> treat as fresh territory, but confirm.

EXTERNAL prior art to survey + assess (M3) -- for EACH, what it is, and how well it fits loom (MemPalace-native? bead-handoff? lightweight? soundness model?):
- ADRs (Michael Nygard Architecture Decision Records) -- maps onto MemPalace decision drawers directly?
- RFC processes (Rust RFC, IETF, company RFC) -- the review-gated evolving design doc
- Design docs / narrative memos (Amazon 6-pager / PRFAQ, Google design docs)
- Shape Up "shaping" (Basecamp) -- a NAMED structured pre-build design phase that hands off to "building"; assess the shaping->betting->building handoff vs design->create-beads
- arc42 + C4 model (architecture documentation templates/structure)
- Evolutionary architecture + FITNESS FUNCTIONS (Neal Ford / Building Evolutionary Architectures) -- candidate analog for the missing RED->GREEN "soundness" check in the design phase
- Diataxis (already in loom -- Explanation quadrant)
- AGENT-NATIVE / spec-driven-development: any recent (current-year) tooling or methodology for LLM/agent-driven design phases, spec-first / intent-doc workflows, "living design doc" tools. Use a year filter.

Assess each against loom's ethos explicitly: (1) MemPalace-native (living, revisable, provenance-tracked) (2) bead-handoff (design -> create-beads) (3) capture-discipline (4) lightweight-not-heavyweight (5) a soundness/verification model that fits design (coherence/grounding, not RED->GREEN).

DELIVERABLE (M5, draft for central to file): a decision drawer synthesizing (a) which external pattern(s) fit + WHY NOT the others, (b) the minimal loom-native design-phase shape it suggests (recipe cadence + the living-design-document capture primitive + how it hands off to create-beads), (c) the "soundness verification" analog for the design phase, (d) OPEN questions. Plus 3-5 KG triples for the design-family relationships. NO code. Round-trippable.

title: skill-redirect hook: superpowers:brainstorming → beadpowers:brainstorming in beads projects
description:
Goal: When the model spontaneously invokes the Skill tool with superpowers:brainstorming in a beads-tracked project, redirect it to beadpowers:brainstorming. The two skills have WORD-FOR-WORD identical descriptions, so on auto-trigger the model has no distinguishing signal and defaults to superpowers; loom wants beadpowers (design lands as beads). We do NOT control the plugin descriptions (plugin-owned, overwritten on upgrade), so a loom-owned PreToolUse hook is the robust fix. Bites every beads project, not just dreamer-engine.

Confirmed feasibility (2026-06-06, via claude-code-guide + this session transcript): Skill IS a matchable PreToolUse tool_name (the Agent SDK adds the Skill tool to allowedTools). Empirically the PreToolUse payload is tool_name=Skill and tool_input is {skill: "<name>", args: "<args>"} -- the skill name lives in tool_input.skill. exit 2 blocks tool calls and the hook stderr is fed back to the model on block (same as other loom hooks).

Design:
- hooks/skill-redirect.sh: PreToolUse. Parse tool_name + tool_input.skill (jq with no-jq sed fallback; mirror hooks/edit-write-pwd-guard.sh input-parse). Only act when tool_name=Skill. Gate on a .beads/ directory present in the project (walk up from cwd; skip silently otherwise). Redirect table (config-as-code, currently ONE entry): superpowers:brainstorming -> beadpowers:brainstorming. On a mapped skill in a beads project: exit 2 + stderr naming the replacement (e.g. "beads-tracked project: use beadpowers:brainstorming (design lands as beads), not superpowers:brainstorming"). Bypass LOOM_SKILL_REDIRECT_SKIP=1 (literal-1 match, per loom-b1l convention). Non-mapped skills / non-Skill tools / non-beads projects: exit 0 silently.
- Register the PreToolUse Skill matcher in settings.snippet.json (install.sh merges it into live settings.json; hooks/*.sh are auto-symlinked at install.sh ~line 185, so no separate install.sh edit needed).

WHY BLOCK, NOT NUDGE: a non-blocking nudge would let superpowers:brainstorming load and the wrong brainstorm starts -- only exit 2 prevents the load so the model re-picks. This is a legitimate block case (differs from the loom-yb5 dispatch-nudge by design); the env bypass keeps it escapable and recovery is trivial (re-invoke the right skill).

TDD (lib/tests/skill-redirect.test.sh, house fixture style -- feed PreToolUse JSON on stdin, assert exit code + stderr):
(a) {tool_name:Skill, tool_input:{skill:"superpowers:brainstorming"}} + .beads present -> exit 2, stderr names beadpowers
(b) {skill:"beadpowers:brainstorming"} -> exit 0 (no redirect loop)
(c) {skill:"feature-a-bead"} (unmapped) -> exit 0
(d) superpowers:brainstorming but NO .beads in the project -> exit 0 (gate)
(e) LOOM_SKILL_REDIRECT_SKIP=1 set -> exit 0 (bypass)
(f) non-Skill tool (e.g. tool_name:Bash) -> exit 0 (passthrough)

Acceptance: redirect fires only for the mapped skill in a beads project; blocks with a beadpowers-naming stderr; bypass + .beads-gate + unmapped-skills + non-Skill-tools all pass through exit 0; registered in settings.snippet.json. Full loom suite green.

POST-MERGE (central, the one undocumented behavior): verify the LIVE blocked-Skill UX -- confirm that exit 2 on a Skill call actually tells the model to pick beadpowers (vs failing oddly). If the UX is poor, file a follow-up.

Files: hooks/skill-redirect.sh, lib/tests/skill-redirect.test.sh, settings.snippet.json
bugfix-a-bead.
close_reason: skill-redirect PreToolUse hook: superpowers:brainstorming → beadpowers:brainstorming in .beads/ projects (identical descriptions → model coin-flipped to superpowers). exit-2 block (required — nudge would let wrong skill load), config-as-code redirect table, LOOM_SKILL_REDIRECT_SKIP=1 bypass. 7/7 tests; suite 58/0. LIVE-VERIFIED: invoking superpowers:brainstorming in loom was blocked + redirected end-to-end (confirms Skill is harness-hookable + exit 2 steers the model). Worker-dispatched. Merged.

title: history-mine full e2e run still 0 drafts post-loom-bzl (foreground-validated 1/5, background full-run 0/787)
description:
Goal: explain + fix why the FULL background mine of e2e-api-tests still emits 0 drafts AFTER loom-bzl fixed the parse (which was foreground-validated at 5 gated -> 1 salient draft).

Observations (2026-06-06): post-bzl full background run (out=~/.loom/mine-e2e) = 1833 harvested -> 787 gated -> .processed has 787 entries (all ran) -> 0 drafts, 0 triples. The capped FOREGROUND validation (--since=2026-05-15 --max-units=5) produced 1 draft with non-empty verbatim/synthesis/decision-triple. So the parser works in the foreground; the full background run yields nothing.

Hypotheses to test (systematic-debugging):
1. Background/headless claude invocation differs from foreground (auth / TTY / model resolution) so every reply comes back empty -> salient != true -> 0 drafts. Capture a raw reply from inside a backgrounded run (tee the claude output to a file) to confirm.
2. Rate-limiting across 787 sequential calls -> replies start erroring/empty partway; but that would still leave SOME early drafts, not 0.
3. Genuinely-low salience: e2e-api-tests history is dominated by test-infra churn (fix test / bump / update packages) that legitimately states no rationale -> high salient=false. But 0 from 787 including ~200 merged PRs (which have bodies/rationale) is implausibly low.
4. Interaction with the loom-mec hard-drop dropping more than intended, or the loom-bzl fence/format change behaving differently in bulk.

Repro: run the wrapper full (background) -> 0 drafts; same with a small --since + --max-units in the foreground -> >=1 draft. Diff the two contexts.

Files: lib/loom-mine-history.sh (likely the claude-invocation or a context-sensitive branch), lib/tests/loom-mine-history.test.sh; manifest evidence preserved at ~/.loom/mine-e2e/ (candidates.jsonl populated, drafts.jsonl empty, .processed=787).

bugfix-a-bead / systematic-debugging. Blocks productive e2e (and any real) mining. Lineage: loom-bzl (fixed the parse), e2e dogfood retrospective drawer_loom_decisions_4c31458cd8eabbe4e200fb90.
close_reason: Merged --no-ff. loom-6bv1: bead-lifecycle-shell dispatch-discipline→/dispatch-middle default, central-writes-nothing (41/0). loom-8crd: bugfix/feature M3/M4 RED-author vs GREEN-implementer split (17/0). loom-b10: ROOT CAUSE bash read truncates multi-line commit body at first newline→model saw no rationale→0 drafts; fix awk collapses newlines pre-join (RED→GREEN). Suite green.

title: T4: recipe prose — worker-default + mechanical inline threshold (bead-lifecycle-shell)
description:
Goal: Flip the recipe's stated default to worker-dispatch and replace the vague 'trivial/contained → inline' escape with a MECHANICAL threshold the nudge (T2) can reference.
Files: skills/bead-lifecycle-shell/SKILL.md (Dispatch discipline section); cross-ref from CLAUDE.md if needed.
Steps: 1. Rewrite Dispatch discipline: worker-dispatch is the DEFAULT for any bead with a RED->GREEN cycle; inline is the explicit, justified exception. 2. Define the mechanical inline escape: inline without justification ONLY for <=~15 lines AND single non-test file AND no new test (docs/config/prose edits qualify); anything with a RED->GREEN cycle defaults to worker. 3. Cross-reference T2's nudge + the dispatch field. 4. Verify prose-contract grep if applicable.
Acceptance: bead-lifecycle-shell states worker-default + the mechanical threshold; the inline escape is checkable (line/file/test based), not vibes.
Independent file (skill md) → parallel-safe. SOFT-PRECEDES T2.
