T1: workflow-state dispatch field + inline/dispatched counters + statusline (13/13). T3: scripts/loom-fanout-detect + session-startup step 6a + Files: convention (10/10). T4: bead-lifecycle-shell worker-default + mechanical inline threshold. Integrated 9b35297/94ef389/12e845b; suite 56/0. T2 (loom-h5s) now unblocked.

title: T3: fan-out detector at selection + Files: declaration convention (Component B)
description:

Files: skills/session-startup/SKILL.md, commands/working-a-bead.md (router) [or skills/*router*], optional scripts/loom-fanout-detect, lib/tests/loom-fanout-detect.test.sh
Steps: 1. RED (if helper): given a set of ready beads with dep edges + Files: lines, the detector groups beads that have NO dep edge between them AND NO overlapping Files:; a bead with no Files: declared → 'unknown, not safe to auto-parallelize'. 2. Implement detector (parse bd ready --json + each bead's Files: line + dep graph). 3. Wire into session-startup/router: when >=2 independent ready beads, surface 'loom-X/Y/Z are independent — dispatch N parallel workers? [y/edit/serial]' as the DEFAULT proposal (propose, not auto-dispatch). 4. Document the Files: declaration convention (CLAUDE.md / bead-lifecycle-shell): beads should declare Files: so the detector works; degrades conservative without it.
Acceptance: independent ready beads (no dep + no Files: overlap) are proposed as a wave; file-colliding-but-dep-independent beads (bn7.2/bn7.3 case) are NOT proposed parallel; missing Files: → conservative.

description:
Goal: Foundation for the dispatch-posture epic — a per-bead dispatch decision field + session drift counter, both surfaced on the statusline. Subsumes Component C.

RED: workflow-state set/get 'dispatch' accepts worker | inline:<reason> | (unset); setting dispatch=worker increments a session 'dispatched' counter, dispatch=inline increments 'inline'; statusline renders 'dispatch:<v>' + 'inline:N/dispatched:M'. 2. Implement in workflow-state (new field + two counters, auto-increment on set) + statusline render. 3. Verify round-trip + counters + render.

title: T2: per-bead dispatch-nudge hook (Component A)
description:
Goal: Non-blocking forcing function nudging central toward worker-dispatch as the default, once per bead at the decision point.
Files: hooks/dispatch-nudge.sh, install.sh (wire PreToolUse Edit/Write), lib/tests/dispatch-nudge.test.sh
Steps: 1. RED: hook fires ONCE per bead when bead in_progress AND workflow-state dispatch UNSET AND target is a source file (not docs/config/test) → emits non-blocking system-reminder 'Default is dispatch a worker for the variable middle; set dispatch=worker (then dispatch) or dispatch=inline:<reason>'. Once dispatch set → silent. If dispatch=worker but central still Edits source → softer 'declared-worker-but-editing-inline' reminder. Non-blocking (exit 0 always; reminder via stderr/JSON). 2. Implement + wire into install.sh. 3. Verify: unset→nudge once, inline:<reason>→silent, worker+edit→mismatch reminder, docs/test target→silent.
Acceptance: nudge fires once/bead on unset+source-edit; silent after dispatch set; never blocks; install.sh links it; tests green.
DEPENDS ON T1 (reads/sets the dispatch field). SOFT-PRECEDED BY T4 (the nudge message references the worker-default + inline-threshold prose). Files disjoint from T3/T4.
close_reason: Component A: non-blocking per-bead dispatch-nudge PreToolUse hook (hooks/dispatch-nudge.sh). Fires once/bead when in_progress + workflow-state dispatch unset + source-file edit → reminder that worker-dispatch is the default; dispatch=worker+source→mismatch reminder; inline/docs/test→silent. Bypass LOOM_DISPATCH_NUDGE_SKIP=1. 16/16 tests; suite 57/0; registered live. Worker-dispatched (dogfood). Merged.

title: Restore central-agent dispatch posture (worker-default + parallel-by-default)
description:
Goal: Fix the observed drift where the main/central agent does most work INLINE and SERIAL instead of dispatching workers by default and parallelizing — the user had to explicitly tell it to dispatch/parallelize most of the time (named 2026-06-06; the 7-bead bn7 session is exhibit A: all inline, all serial).

Root causes (from that session's retrospective): (1) the inline escape is a ratchet — each inline bead builds context that makes the next feel more justified; (2) dispatch discipline lives only as PROSE in the recipes, and per loom's own 'hooks enforce, prose slips' lesson it slips; (3) bd ready is popped as a serial queue — independence is computed at bead CREATION (splitting heuristic) but never re-surfaced at WORK time.

Design (brainstorm 2026-06-06, beadpowers): make worker-dispatch + parallelism the VISIBLE DEFAULT via nudges + visibility, NOT hard blocks (blocks become bypass-magnets — edit-after-failure-guard was marker-bypassed 4x in one session). Three components over a shared workflow-state 'dispatch' field:
- A (T2): per-bead non-blocking dispatch-nudge hook.
- B (T3): fan-out detector at selection (dep-graph + Files: overlap).
- C (folded into T1): inline/dispatched drift counter on statusline.
Plus T4: recipe prose flipping the default to worker + a mechanical inline threshold.

Honest scope: tooling makes the right behavior low-friction + makes drift visible; it does not GUARANTEE compliance (nudge-not-block by design). The rest is central's posture.

Children: T1 (foundation: dispatch field + counter + statusline), T2 (nudge hook, dep T1), T3 (fan-out detector + Files: convention), T4 (recipe prose). T1/T3/T4 are file-disjoint → a parallel-safe 3-wide wave; T2 depends on T1; T4 soft-precedes T2. Eat-our-own-dogfood: build T1/T3/T4 as a parallel worker wave.
close_reason: Epic complete: central-agent dispatch posture restored. T1 dispatch field+counter+statusline, T2 nudge hook (live), T3 fan-out detector+Files: convention, T4 worker-default+mechanical inline threshold. Built largely via parallel worker waves (dogfood). Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_f7301fa96b87c3b0e8c71f92.

title: edit-after-failure-guard false-positives on 'N failed' substring in GREEN test output
description:
hooks/edit-after-failure-guard.sh scans recent Bash output for test-failure markers; its marker detection matches the bare substring 'failed', so GREEN summaries like 'Tests: 15 passed, 0 failed' trip it. Since the test RUN is the last action (no test-file edit follows a run), it then blocks the next legitimate source edit. Recurred 4x in the 2026-06-03 bn7 session (loom-6ah, bn7.5, loom-kx2/mec) — each time worked around via the loom-n1q marker bypass (touch .claude/no-edit-after-failure-guard), which is friction + risks leaving the guard disabled.

Fix: the failure-marker regex must NOT match '0 failed' / 'N failed' / '0 failures' in an otherwise-passing summary. Match real failure signatures instead — e.g. a nonzero count ('[1-9][0-9]* failed', 'FAILED', 'FAIL:', non-zero exit markers) and explicitly exclude '0 failed'. RED test: feed a transcript-tail fixture containing 'Tests: N passed, 0 failed' + a subsequent source edit; assert the guard does NOT block (exit 0). Sibling-class to loom-6iy (both naive-substring bugs), but a different file. Files: hooks/edit-after-failure-guard.sh + its test. bugfix-a-bead.
close_reason: loom-9ng: edit-after-failure-guard FAIL_RE count-alternatives tightened to [1-9]\d* so '0 failed'/'N passed, 0 failed' GREEN summaries no longer block; non-zero failures + FAIL: still block (target 50/0, suite 62 green). loom-t0l3: DOGFOOD of /dispatch-middle SUCCEEDED — ran test-author→implementer split on loom-9ng in one shared worktree; implementer made GREEN from the RED-test-as-artifact alone, never seeing the author's reasoning; independence verified by construction.

title: history-mine gate over-admits routine commits (upgrade/lint/chore/update-packages) — expand junk-drop
description:
Surfaced by the e2e-api-tests dogfood (loom-bn7.5 recon, 2026-06-03): a bounded dry-run gated 36/337, of which ~1/3 were routine non-decisions — 'upgrade ck go packages', 'update ck go packages', lint fixes, 'fix readings.facility test', '(FOR NOW) make ttls longer'. The heuristic gate's hard-drop list in _lmh_score_commit (lib/loom-mine-history.sh) covers wip|fixup|squash|typo|bump|merge|revert but NOT upgrade|update.*packages|lint|chore — so dependency bumps and lint/test-fix churn survive to the (paid) LLM pass. The LLM trust-gate drops most (no rationale) but it inflates the gated count = cost.

Fix: extend the junk-subject hard-drop case in _lmh_score_commit with: upgrade*, *upgrade*, 'update * packages'/'update * pkgs', lint*, *lint, chore*, and possibly 'fix * test'/'de-flake'. Keep conservative — only DROP subjects that are clearly routine; don't drop substantive commits that merely mention these words in a rationale.
RED test: lib/tests/loom-mine-history.test.sh — add junk subjects 'upgrade ck go packages', 'fix lint errors', 'chore: bump deps' to the gate fixture; assert dropped.
Files: lib/loom-mine-history.sh, lib/tests/loom-mine-history.test.sh. bugfix-a-bead.
close_reason: Dogfood-driven (first live /loom-mine-history dry-run on e2e-api-tests). loom-kx2: wrapper default wing → verbatim basename (e2e-api-tests not e2e_api_tests), agrees with audit-project. loom-mec: gate unconditionally hard-drops routine churn (upgrade/lint/chore/update-deps) — e2e gated 36→30. Engine 87/0, cmd 24/0, suite 54/0. Merged 17e9f9a.

title: loom-mine-history default-wing uses -→_ (wrong for dash wings like golden-path)
description:
Surfaced reconciling loom-6ah. scripts/loom-mine-history derives the default MemPalace wing from the repo basename with -→_ (dash→underscore). That is wrong for dash-style wings: a repo 'golden-path' → wing 'golden_path', but the actual wing is 'golden-path'. loom-mine-history FILES mined drawers into this wing; audit-project (loom-6ah, now via scripts/loom-audit-resolve) SEARCHES the VERBATIM wing. For a dash-named repo they DIVERGE → audit would miss the mined drawers.

Fix: change scripts/loom-mine-history default wing to VERBATIM basename (no _↔- substitution), matching scripts/loom-audit-resolve + skills/audit-project/SKILL.md. Verbatim is the only rule correct for both underscore (liza_base) and dash (golden-path) wings.
RED test: extend lib/tests/loom-mine-history-cmd.test.sh — a repo dir 'golden-path' resolves wing 'golden-path' (verbatim), not 'golden_path'.
Files: scripts/loom-mine-history, lib/tests/loom-mine-history-cmd.test.sh. bugfix-a-bead.
NOTE: the bn7.4 close drawer claimed -→_ 'mirrors audit-project --wing' — it did not; audit uses verbatim. This reconciles the cross-primitive convention.
close_reason: Dogfood-driven (first live /loom-mine-history dry-run on e2e-api-tests). loom-kx2: wrapper default wing → verbatim basename (e2e-api-tests not e2e_api_tests), agrees with audit-project. loom-mec: gate unconditionally hard-drops routine churn (upgrade/lint/chore/update-deps) — e2e gated 36→30. Engine 87/0, cmd 24/0, suite 54/0. Merged 17e9f9a.

title: history-mine tier-2: skill-side arc filing + anchor->drawer_id cross-link upgrade
description:

1. After the per-unit drafts.jsonl filing loop, read <out>/arcs.jsonl; for each arc: mempalace_check_duplicate(drawer_body) -> if not dup, mempalace_add_drawer(wing, room=decisions, content=drawer_body) -> mempalace_tag_drawer(id, provenance, mined) + tag synthesis=arc.
2. UPGRADE the constituent links: the lib emits arcs linking constituents by source ANCHOR (PR#/SHA) because it has no drawer_ids. The skill files per-unit drawers FIRST (collecting their returned drawer_ids keyed by source_id), THEN files arcs, rewriting each constituent anchor -> the real drawer_id (or mp:// URI) so the arc drawer cross-references filed drawers, not just anchors.
3. Surface 'filed K arc drawer(s) linking N per-unit drawers' in the adoption summary.
4. Pass --synthesize through commands/loom-mine-history.md.

title: history-mine watermark: skill-side KG round-trip (read --since-sha, write history_mined_through)

Also wire --resume into the skill's interrupted-run recovery story.

title: /audit-project --mine-history flag + project-onboarder informational gap line
description:
Goal: Make audit-project aware of unmined decision history. Default audit (no flag) only FLAGS the gap; the --mine-history flag delegates to /loom-mine-history.

Files: skills/audit-project/SKILL.md, commands/audit-project.md, agents/project-onboarder.md
Steps: 1. project-onboarder: add an INFORMATIONAL gap line ('N units of unmined decision history in git/PRs') — like the existing Diataxis-informational check; NOT tagged [AUTOFIX] (too expensive to auto-apply). 2. audit-project skill + command: add --mine-history flag that, when set, delegates to the /loom-mine-history engine after the audit. 3. Tests/verify.
Acceptance: default audit reports the history gap informationally; --mine-history runs the mine; no autofix tagging.
COORDINATION: this bead touches commands/audit-project.md + skills/audit-project/SKILL.md + agents/project-onboarder.md — the SAME files as loom-6ah (rescued --root/--wing portability WIP). Sequence after / rebase onto loom-6ah to avoid conflict; ideally land loom-6ah first.

Default never auto-mines. 8/8 contract tests; suite 54/0. Merged 5c4aa8f.

title: /loom-adopt orchestrator: full-treatment brownfield onboarding
description:
Goal: One-shot 'make this repo fully loom-standard' orchestrator composing existing primitives + history-mine.

Files: commands/loom-adopt.md, skills/loom-adopt/SKILL.md
Phases (dependency-ordered): P1 workflow-infra (DELEGATE to audit-project --apply — DRY, do NOT re-enumerate the checklist) -> P2 scripts/ scaffold [skip if loom-oxs not landed] -> P3 docs-scaffold -> P4 history-mine (needs wing from P1) -> P5 constitution + onboarding beads + wing tunnels [skip if constitution not landed]. P2/P3 order-independent.
Behavior: PER-PHASE CHECKPOINT interactivity (announce->confirm->run->show->proceed; not one-shot autonomous, not per-file nag); history-mine phase nests its own cost-preview gate. GRACEFUL DEGRADATION: unbuilt-primitive phases skipped with logged reason; phase list enumerated dynamically from what's installed. IDEMPOTENT+RESUMABLE: re-run = re-audit skip-satisfied + incremental mine via watermark; interrupted -> resume unfinished phase. Doubles as 'refresh to current loom standards'. OUTPUT: adoption report (installed/scaffolded/mined/skipped-why/beads-filed).
Steps: 1. SKILL.md phase machine + checkpoint logic. 2. command wrapper. 3. Tests: phase sequencing, graceful-skip of unbuilt primitives, idempotent re-run, mid-phase resume. 4. install.sh re-run + verify invocable.
Acceptance: composes the live primitives; skips unbuilt ones with reason; re-run is idempotent; resumable.
Soft-depends loom-oxs (scripts/) + loom-8jz/ld4 (constitution) — degrades gracefully, does NOT block.
close_reason: wave-2 parallel: DRY hook-helpers (13 hooks refactored, behavior-preserved), capture-fanout default at D3, /loom-adopt orchestrator, history-mine watermark round-trip. All merged --no-ff, 70/70 suite green.

title: /loom-mine-history command + skill (standalone primitive entry)
description:
Goal: The standalone user entry point that wraps the history-mine engine. Thin orchestration over lib/loom-mine-history.sh.

Files: commands/loom-mine-history.md, skills/loom-mine-history/SKILL.md
Steps: 1. Write SKILL.md: invoke the engine against a target repo (default cwd git root, --root override), surface the cost-preview gate, present the adoption-of-history summary. 2. Write commands/loom-mine-history.md (disable-model-invocation, pass-through flags --root/--since/--since-release/--max-units/--dry-run). 3. Verify install.sh --check shows the new symlinks WOULD link (globs skills/*/SKILL.md + commands/*.md); run install.sh; confirm /loom-mine-history invocable.
Acceptance: command + skill installed and invocable; flags pass through to the engine; cost-preview gate surfaces to the user.
NOTE: per the loom-k2g.7 finding this session — RUN install.sh after creating, recipe is not invocable until symlinked.
close_reason: /loom-mine-history command + skill + scripts/loom-mine-history wrapper shipped; wraps bn7.1 engine, two-pass cost gate + MCP filing loop; 24/24 wrapper tests green; installed+invocable. Merged 4614983.

title: history-mine incremental: watermark + range flags + resumability
description:
Goal: Make history-mine cheap to re-run and bounded on huge repos. Additive over the core engine.

Files: lib/loom-mine-history.sh (extends core engine — SEQUENTIAL, shares file), lib/tests/loom-mine-history.test.sh
Features:
- Watermark: store last-mined HEAD SHA as KG fact <repo>->history_mined_through-><SHA>; re-runs harvest only units AFTER the watermark.
- Range flags: --since=<date>, --since-release=<tag>, --max-units=N (cap, most-recent-first). Default remains full-history.
- Resumability: checkpoint filed drawers; interrupted run resumes from last filed unit; mempalace_check_duplicate keeps re-files idempotent.
Steps: 1. RED tests: re-run after watermark harvests only new units; --since/--max-units bound the candidate set; interrupted+resumed run does not re-file. 2. Implement. 3. Verify watermark advances + reads back; re-run is incremental + idempotent.
Acceptance: second run mines only post-watermark units; range flags bound correctly; resume is idempotent.
NOTE: sequential after core engine (shared file).
close_reason: Lib half shipped: --since-sha watermark-consume bound (precedence over --since-release), <out>/watermark emit on real pass, --resume no-re-spend checkpoint via <out>/.processed; pinned --since/--since-release range bounding. 68/68 tests (was 49). Merged f307a98. Skill-side KG round-trip = follow-up loom-zcv.

title: history-mine core engine: harvest + heuristic gate + LLM salience/draft + cost-preview gate
description:
Goal: The minimum SAFE history-mine primitive — tier-1 per-unit decision archaeology with the mandatory cost gate. Independently useful before synthesis/incremental land.

Files: lib/loom-mine-history.sh, lib/tests/loom-mine-history.test.sh
Pipeline (stages 1-3+5 tier-1):
1. HARVEST — detect gh access (gh auth status + gh repo view); enumerate merged PRs (gh pr list --state merged --json + review threads via gh api); ALWAYS git log merge/squash commits + release tags. Unified candidate list tagged by source (PR# or SHA).
2. HEURISTIC GATE (no LLM) — score candidates: PR=review-discussion/body-len/breaking-design-RFC-labels/closes-issue/decision-shaped-files(schema,migrations,*.proto,interfaces,config)/merge-not-revert; commit=substantial-msg/not-{wip,fixup,typo,bump,merge main}/decision-files/release-tag. Above-threshold survive.
3. LLM SALIENCE+DRAFT (per survivor) — TRUST GATE: skip if source text does NOT state a rationale; else draft drawer = verbatim quote block (commit msg/PR body/key review comments) + clearly-separated synthesis + source anchor (SHA/PR#/URL/date/author).
5. FILE — mempalace_check_duplicate first; add_drawer room=decisions + provenance:mined tag; KG triples (PR#->decided->X, ->mined_from->repo, ->authored_by->Z).
COST-PREVIEW GATE (MANDATORY): real dry-run BEFORE the LLM pass — 'N harvested -> M gated -> ~M LLM reads, est cost/time' -> confirm/abort. Plus a --dry-run mode that emits drafts WITHOUT filing (makes the whole thing testable without a live palace).

Steps: 1. RED tests (gh stubbed via PATH-prepended fake binary echoing canned JSON, per upstream-a-bead + bd-stub pattern; git-fixture repo with known commits/PRs/tags). 2. Implement stages. 3. Verify gate filters, salience SKIPS no-rationale units, drawer carries verbatim+provenance tag+anchor, --dry-run files nothing, cost-preview prints before any LLM call.
Acceptance: tests pass; no-rationale units produce no drawer; --dry-run is side-effect-free; cost-preview gate cannot be bypassed silently.
close_reason: history-mine core engine shipped: lib/loom-mine-history.sh (HARVEST+GATE+cost-preview+LLM-draft, lib-drafts/skill-files seam, 49 fixture tests). Merged --no-ff. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_490284f114c5a773ee3456cf.

title: history-mine tier-2 synthesis: cluster salient units into narrative arc drawers
description:
Goal: Add the second tier — synthesize a HANDFUL of narrative arc drawers ('how auth evolved', 'the great DB migration') from the salient per-unit results. Additive over tier-1; ships after the core engine.

Files: lib/loom-mine-history.sh (extends core engine — SEQUENTIAL after core, shares the file), lib/tests/loom-mine-history.test.sh
Steps: 1. RED test: given a set of salient units clustered by theme/file-area/time, assert a narrative arc drawer is produced that LINKS to the per-unit drawers (cross-references their drawer_ids/anchors). 2. Implement clustering + arc-narrative drafting. 3. Verify arc drawers link back; verbatim discipline preserved.
Acceptance: tier-2 produces few high-value arc drawers, each linking its constituent per-unit drawers; no duplication of tier-1 content.
NOTE: shares lib/loom-mine-history.sh with the core engine bead — sequential, NOT parallel-dispatchable.
close_reason: Lib half shipped: --synthesize opt-in tier-2 — bash-cluster salient units by decision-file-area (_lmh_cluster_key), one LLM call per >=2-unit cluster narrates an arc drawer linking constituents by anchor, no verbatim duplication, <out>/arcs.jsonl. Fixed latent grep-c 0\n0 count bug. 82/82 tests. Merged bd6e6e6. Skill-side arc filing = follow-up loom-68r.

title: [epic] Brownfield adoption: decision-archaeology history mining + /loom-adopt orchestrator
description:
Close the brownfield asymmetry: a greenfield loom project accretes MemPalace memory naturally; a brownfield repo starts with an empty palace but a rich decision record already sitting in git + GitHub PRs. Mine that history into 'decision archaeology' (provenance-tagged decision drawers + KG triples) so loom's recall loop (bug-family-search, design-moment recall) works on day one.

Full design locked in MemPalace drawer drawer_loom_decisions_e43e3693c8ee82e3bc6e34c6 (loom/decisions, 2026-05-29 brainstorm).

Two new surfaces:
- /loom-mine-history (NEW primitive) — 5-stage pipeline: HARVEST (gh PRs+reviews where access; git log merge/squash/tags always) -> HEURISTIC GATE (no LLM) -> LLM SALIENCE+DRAFT (trust gate: skip if source doesn't STATE a rationale; verbatim quotes + separated synthesis + source anchor) -> SYNTHESIS tier-2 (narrative arc drawers) -> FILE (check_duplicate, add_drawer decisions+provenance:mined tag, KG triples). Full-history default + flags, MANDATORY cost-preview dry-run gate, watermark for incremental, resumable.
- /loom-adopt (orchestrator 'full treatment') — composes audit-project --apply (infra, DELEGATED/DRY) + scripts/ scaffold + docs-scaffold + history-mine + constitution; per-phase checkpoint, graceful degradation, idempotent+resumable.

Plus /audit-project --mine-history flag (informational gap, not autofix) + docs.

Dependency: history-mine engine blocks the command, audit flag, and orchestrator. /loom-adopt soft-depends on in-flight loom-oxs (scripts/) + loom-8jz/ld4 (constitution) but degrades gracefully.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: cwd-drift-guard hook (loom-d2o) false-positive on workers in agent-worktrees blocks bd update --claim
description:
## Symptom

Workers dispatched via Agent + isolation: worktree run in .claude/worktrees/agent-<id>/. The cwd-drift-guard hook (hooks/cwd-drift-guard.sh, loom-d2o) intercepts bd update/close, git merge/push, bd dolt push when cwd matches .claude/worktrees/agent-*/ — designed for CENTRAL drift detection but matches ANY cwd in that pattern, including legitimate worker contexts.

## Reproduction (3x observed in loom-k2g wave 1, 2026-05-27)

- Worker loom-k2g.2 first dispatch: burned 14 tool calls trying to bypass the hook with LOOM_CWD_DRIFT_GUARD_SKIP=1 prefix. Hook reads env at scan time (before bash parses the assignment), so prefix-style bypass doesn't work. Worker returned without code; central had to cleanup + re-dispatch.
- Worker loom-k2g.1: apparently DID get it to work via env-var bypass (unclear exact mechanism — contradicts worker .2's experience).
- Worker loom-k2g.6: skipped claim, recommended central handle it.

## Hypothesis

cwd-drift-guard's match pattern '.claude/worktrees/agent-*/' is too broad. Designed for CENTRAL's persistent-bash drifting into a worker's worktree post-dispatch (per loom-d2o drawer drawer_loom_decisions_*). Worker agents legitimately operate FROM that path and should be exempted.

## Candidate fixes

- (a) Detect worker context — e.g., AGENT_ID env var, parent process inspection, or sentinel file dropped by Agent harness. Skip hook when worker context detected.
- (b) Make LOOM_CWD_DRIFT_GUARD_SKIP work at exec-time (post-Bash-tool-call), not scan-time. Then workers can prefix.
- (c) Document the workaround pattern (central pre-claims, brief skips claim step) as canonical worker discipline — codify in .claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md and bead-lifecycle-shell.

## Acceptance

- Workers can run bd update --claim from their own worktree without bypass ceremony OR docs explicitly capture the pre-claim+skip-brief pattern as canonical.
- Regression test: dispatched-agent simulation in lib/tests/ that exercises bd update --claim from a fake .claude/worktrees/agent-*/ path.

## Prior art

- loom-d2o (original hook design, 2026-05-27 same day) — central-drift focus, no worker-context handling.
- loom-k2g wave 1 (2026-05-27) — 3 workers, 3 different experiences with the hook.
- drawer_loom_decisions_a6e64f9cfb21a9d16fc47604 'Friction surfaced' section captures the in-flight observations.
- drawer (filing now after this bead) loom-k2g WAVE 1 SHIPPED — full friction writeup.

## Execution

bugfix-a-bead recipe. Worktree on frank/<this-bead-id>. RED test reproduces hook block on simulated worker context. GREEN fix per the chosen option above (a/b/c).
close_reason: cwd-drift-guard worker-context exemption shipped in merge 56c12c444fccad5c2362a25b870541e9239d9172 (worker commit 905cdf4). hooks/cwd-drift-guard.sh now checks PreToolUse Bash payload for top-level agent_id / agent_type (CC's documented subagent markers — different schema from SessionStart payloads, so NOT reused via lib/subagent-detect.sh). Workers in .claude/worktrees/agent-*/ paths legitimately allowed; central-drift detection regression-guarded. 8 new fixture cases (29→37/37 GREEN), including regression guard for original loom-d2o behavior. Eliminates the wave-2 workaround (pre-claim centrally + skip-claim brief); env-var bypass still works.

title: /audit-project: ~/.loom/upstream/ health + gh auth status checks
description:

- agents/project-onboarder.md — add corresponding inspection items
- lib/tests/audit-apply-flags.test.sh — bump AUTOFIX-inventory guards

Steps:
1. Read skills/audit-project/SKILL.md to find natural insertion point
2. Item N+1: ~/.loom/upstream/ orphan-clone scan — list dirs without upstream:watch bead reference; [AUTOFIX:loom-upstream-gc-handoff] points at /loom-upstream-gc
3. Item N+2: gh auth status — non-zero → WARN; [AUTOFIX:gh-auth-prompt] points to running gh auth login
4. Update project-onboarder accordingly
5. Bump audit-apply-flags.test.sh guards for the two new tags
6. Smoke-test audit run

Acceptance: both items surface correctly when conditions met; AUTOFIX paths documented; tests pass.

Design source: drawer_loom_decisions_a6e64f9cfb21a9d16fc47604

title: /loom-upstream-gc slash command: interactive prune of ~/.loom/upstream clones
description:
Goal: New slash command for interactive cleanup of stale ~/.loom/upstream/<owner>/<repo>/ clones. Refuses removal if any open upstream:watch bead references the repo OR if dir has uncommitted changes. Asks user per-dir before destructive ops.

Files:
- commands/loom-upstream-gc.md (new)
- lib/tests/loom-upstream-gc.test.sh (new) — covers refusal on uncommitted-changes; refusal on open watch-bead reference; clean prune with user assent

Steps:
1. Write commands/loom-upstream-gc.md — list dirs; per dir check git status --porcelain + bd list --label=upstream:watch for refs; AskUserQuestion before rm; never auto-destructive
2. Reuse lib/loom-upstream.sh helpers from T2 where applicable (parse_watch_bead_repo_ref)
3. Fixture tests: cleanly stub bd + git status

Acceptance: never removes dir with watch-bead pointing at it; never removes dir with uncommitted work; user assent required for every removal.

Design source: drawer_loom_decisions_a6e64f9cfb21a9d16fc47604
close_reason: /loom-upstream-gc slash command shipped in merge f1cd2a492e580b81bcc86e79d2ee2e0788b0df57 (worker commit 4011db1). Interactive prune of stale ~/.loom/upstream clones. Two structural refusals BEFORE prompt: uncommitted-changes + open-watch-bead-reference. AskUserQuestion per-clone; no --all/--force/--yes; never auto-destructive. Empty owner-dirs cleaned up after prune. 23/23 GREEN. Worker correctly chose NOT to source lib/loom-upstream.sh refuse_if_stale (over-refuses on unpushed branches — wrong gate for GC). Design source: drawer_loom_decisions_a6e64f9cfb21a9d16fc47604.

- lib/tests/check-upstream-prs.test.sh (new) — covers MERGED auto-close, CLOSED user-surface, OPEN no-op, malformed URL graceful skip

Steps:
1. Read skills/feature-a-bead/SKILL.md and skills/bugfix-a-bead/SKILL.md for shape/economy
2. Draft skills/upstream-a-bead/SKILL.md per design (drawer drawer_loom_decisions_a6e64f9cfb21a9d16fc47604) — M1 designing (central), M2 cloning (worker), M3 red, M4 green, M5 draft, M6 review (central), M7 file (central)
3. Cover both --issue-only and --issue+pr lanes; lane decided at M1
4. Write commands/upstream-a-bead.md (single-line invocation)
5. Update install.sh; update session-startup table
6. Smoke-test: Skill tool can load upstream-a-bead

Acceptance: skill loadable, install.sh creates symlink, follows sibling-recipe economy, both lanes documented, drawer cited.

Design source: drawer_loom_decisions_a6e64f9cfb21a9d16fc47604
close_reason: upstream-a-bead recipe shipped in merge c3b211f (worker commit 3bf7d85). 7th sibling activity recipe; 610-line SKILL.md + 33-line slash command + session-startup table row. Cites bead-lifecycle-shell phases A/B/C/D, owns M1-M7 (designing→cloning→red→green→draft→review→file). Both --issue-only and --issue+pr lanes documented; intractable/upstream-pending captured as third M1 outcome per loom-98x. install.sh auto-globs new files; no edit needed. title: audit-project Item 12: encode additive-hook-layering + add real AUTOFIX paths (skip-worktree + commit-removal)
description:
## Why this exists

Item 12 of /audit-project (duplicate hook detection via find-hook-dups.sh) WARNs on duplicates but its resolution guidance has no AUTOFIX path. The currently-implied mitigation (empty-arrays in settings.local.json to cancel inherited hook registrations) was VERIFIED INERT on 2026-05-27 in e2e-api-tests: Claude Code's hook layering is **additive across all layers** (plugin + user-global ~/.claude/settings.json + project-tracked .claude/settings.json + project-local .claude/settings.local.json). Empty arrays don't cancel; they just add zero entries to the union. After applying the override, bd prime still fired 3 times in a fresh SessionStart.

Frank's session captured this as KG fact: `Claude Code settings hooks → merge_semantics_are → additive across all layers` — durable. Resolution that actually worked was `git update-index --skip-worktree .claude/settings.json` + clean the hooks locally. Manual, brittle on upstream changes (any future pull touching the file errors with 'would be overwritten').

## What this delivers

Three sub-deliverables under audit-project's Item 12 lineage:

### A. Skip-worktree AUTOFIX path

Tag: `[AUTOFIX:dedup-hook-skip-worktree]`. When find-hook-dups.sh detects a project-tracked SessionStart/PreToolUse hook that's ALSO registered by the plugin or user-global layer, /audit-project --apply-onboarding offers to:
1. `git update-index --skip-worktree <project>/.claude/settings.json`
2. Strip the duplicate hook from the local copy
3. Log the recovery snippet to .claude/loom-audit-state.json (or a sidecar) for the inevitable next upstream change to settings.json

Recovery snippet baked into AUTOFIX log:
```bash
git update-index --no-skip-worktree .claude/settings.json
git stash
git pull
git stash pop
# then re-apply skip-worktree + strip via /audit-project --apply-onboarding
```

Per-user, reversible. The default AUTOFIX path for this Item.

### B. Commit-removal AUTOFIX path

Tag: `[AUTOFIX:dedup-hook-commit]`. Same detection, different resolution: remove the duplicate hook from the tracked file and commit. Requires a confirmation prompt because it changes shared content — non-loom devs touching the repo would lose the hook registration.

Confirmation prompt explicitly names: 'This commits a change to .claude/settings.json that assumes loom adoption for all devs on this repo. Non-loom devs lose <hook-name> registration. Proceed? (y/N)'.

Behind the confirmation, the AUTOFIX writes the commit with subject `audit: dedup <hook-name> SessionStart hook (loom-managed; plugin + user-global handle registration)`.

### C. docs/reference/claude-code-hook-layering.md

New Diataxis reference page. Documents the additive-layering reality with:
- The four layers (plugin → user-global ~/.claude/settings.json → project-tracked .claude/settings.json → project-local .claude/settings.local.json)
- Verified-empirical anchor: 3 fires in a fresh e2e-api-tests session (2026-05-27) when project + user-global + plugin all registered bd prime
- Why empty-arrays-in-local-don't-cancel: layering is union, not override
- Pointer to /audit-project Item 12 + the two AUTOFIX paths above
- Pointer to docs/reference/loom-env-vars.md for the env-block sibling pattern (loom-7ro lineage)

Doubles as the discovery surface for future Claude Code hook-layering findings.

## Acceptance

- /audit-project Item 12 documents both AUTOFIX paths in agents/project-onboarder.md
- skills/audit-project/SKILL.md --apply-onboarding walk handles both tags
- skip-worktree AUTOFIX is the default offer; commit-removal is behind explicit confirmation
- docs/reference/claude-code-hook-layering.md registered in mkdocs.yml
- find-hook-dups.sh output remains the canonical detection mechanism (no script changes needed for this bead — already enumerates the tuples correctly)
- Tests under lib/tests/ cover the skip-worktree AUTOFIX (fixture: project with duplicated hook in tracked settings.json + plugin manifest → apply-onboarding produces the right git state + sidecar log)
- audit-apply-flags.test.sh AUTOFIX-inventory guards bumped to accommodate the two new tags

## Anti-scope

- Do NOT touch find-hook-dups.sh — detection works; only resolution is the gap.
- Do NOT add a third AUTOFIX path (e.g., 'modify the plugin's manifest') — out of loom's reach.
- Do NOT auto-commit the commit-removal AUTOFIX without explicit user y/N confirmation; the AUTOFIX inventory's binary 'apply' shape doesn't fit this case, so plumb a prompt through.

## Provenance

Surfaced 2026-05-27 by Frank during e2e-api-tests /audit-project session immediately after loom-7ro shipped. Empirically verified: fresh-session SessionStart fires 3 bd prime registrations from the three sources (plugin + user-global + project-tracked) regardless of settings.local.json overrides. KG triple from that session: `Claude Code settings hooks → merge_semantics_are → additive across all layers` (2026-05-27, e2e-api-tests/decisions wing).

## Sibling beads

- loom-7ro (closed) — env-block opt-out in project-level settings.json. Pure-additive shape works for ENV merge but not for HOOKS layering (loom-7ro's keys are scalars that overwrite; hooks are arrays that union).
- loom-1tq, loom-8hg, loom-a29 — audit-project + --apply-onboarding pattern (this bead extends it)
- loom-9pr — audit-for-hardcoded-loom/main references — orthogonal but the AUTOFIX inventory updates will touch the same test surfaces.
Files: skills/audit-project/SKILL.md, agents/project-onboarder.md, docs/reference/claude-code-hook-layering.md, mkdocs.yml, lib/tests/audit-apply-flags.test.sh
close_reason: audit-project Item 12: encoded additive-hook-layering (4 layers, union-not-override) + 2 AUTOFIX paths — [AUTOFIX:dedup-hook-skip-worktree] (default, per-user, reversible) + [AUTOFIX:dedup-hook-commit] (behind y/N confirm naming shared-content consequence). New docs/reference/claude-code-hook-layering.md in nav. find-hook-dups.sh untouched (anti-scope). audit-apply-flags.test.sh 154→192/0, inventory guard 6→8. mkdocs --strict rc=0. Merged cff89d3.

title: script/ convention: design + lock canonical set + workflow.json overlap
description:
## Goal

Lock the canonical script/ set for the loom-managed-repos convention (parent: loom-oxs). Decide which scripts are in the canonical set, what each is contractually required to do (exit conventions, no-op behavior for projects that don't need them), and how the convention interacts with the existing workflow.json `.deploy` field.

## Open design questions

1. **Canonical set.** Tentative list in loom-oxs (bootstrap, setup, update, server, test, lint, cibuild, deploy). Confirm or revise. Add/drop based on what loom-installed primitives actually consume + what 'predictability for a new agent walking in' demands.
2. **No-op behavior.** Should missing scripts be 'no-op exit 0' (script exists but does nothing) or 'absent entirely' (caller checks existence)? Tradeoff: the absent-entirely shape forces audit-project to surface gaps; the always-present-exit-0 shape lets callers blindly invoke.
3. **workflow.json overlap.** workflow.json today has `.deploy` (string command). Options:
   - Drop `.deploy` — `script/deploy` is the new convention; workflow.json keeps mode/v but not deploy.
   - Keep `.deploy` as an override — projects that want a one-off deploy command can stay declarative without a script file.
   - Have `.deploy` default to `script/deploy` — gives a uniform fallback.
4. **Project-type templates.** Should templates/scripts/ ship one canonical skeleton, or skeletons per project type (Go, Python, bash-only, Node)? Single-skeleton is simpler; per-type catches more out-of-box.
5. **Where the scripts live.** `script/` (GitHub blog convention, singular) vs `scripts/` (loom and many projects' current convention, plural). Loom-the-repo uses `scripts/`. Pick one and document.

## Deliverables

- A decision drawer in loom/decisions locking the canonical set + answers to the open questions.
- Update to loom-oxs description with the final list (replaces 'tentative').
- File T2-T6 children (templates skeleton, audit-project extension, /wrap-up migration, docs page, primitives audit) with concrete scope each, once the design is locked.

## Acceptance

- Locked canonical set published in a decision drawer.
- All five design questions answered with rationale.
- Children filed for the implementation work.

## Recipe

research-a-bead (design dialog with user; no code changes in this bead).
AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE: design — 5 open design questions; decision-drawer + child-bead deliverable
close_reason: Design locked: 5 questions + surfaced constitution-overlap pivot resolved via brainstorming dialogue. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_67171b609b2772cb76660928 captures the locked convention; loom-oxs description updated to the locked set; 7 children filed (loom-oxs.1-.7) with wave deps; 6 KG triples. Foundations .1/.2/.3 dispatched as bg workers.

title: Standardize GitHub-style script/ convention for loom-managed repos
description:
## Why this exists

Loom-managed repos have no project-agnostic entry points for common ops. The value isn't "how many loom primitives consume it today" — it's that a human or agent walking into an unfamiliar loom-managed repo *knows what to expect*. Same logic as loom standardizing on Diataxis + GitHub Pages docs. Walk in -> script/test, script/setup, script/server do what they say. No archaeology.

Frank quote (2026-05-27): "it ends up being like loom standardizing on diataxis and publishing it via github pages, if you walk into a loom managed repo, you know what to expect."

## Canonical set — LOCKED (loom-adm, 2026-06-09)

Directory: **script/** (singular) is the default; the convention ACCEPTS **scripts/** when a repo already uses it (graceful, no forced rename). Recognizers match either; templates ship script/.

The GitHub-faithful 8:
- script/bootstrap — one-time machine setup
- script/setup — fresh checkout -> runnable
- script/update — pull + re-setup
- script/server — start the app locally (dev verb maps here)
- script/test — run tests; exit 0 = green
- script/lint — run linters
- script/cibuild — what CI runs
- script/deploy — deploy step

Dropped console. build/gen live as constitution canonical_commands STRINGS, not skeleton files (recognizer still accepts any script/X a project adds).

## Layering — LOCKED

Three surfaces -> two layers + one state file:
- script/ = EXECUTABLE impl layer.
- project-constitution.md canonical_commands = DECLARATIVE pointer; **script/X is the default impl of canonical_commands.X** (present -> it's the command; absent -> canonical_commands string; both absent -> warn, NEVER pass).
- workflow.json = pure workflow-STATE (mode/version/guest). .deploy DEPRECATED -> migrated to canonical_commands.deploy.

No-op semantics: scripts present (uniform); a no-op ALWAYS echoes a clear message. Genuinely-N/A -> exit 0; unimplemented gap -> exit non-zero (code 2). Skeleton ships in the non-zero "not implemented" form until edited.

## Children (filed 2026-06-09, parented here)

- loom-oxs.1 (T2): templates/scripts/ skeleton (8 stubs, per-type comment hints). FOUNDATION.
- loom-oxs.2 (T3): lib/loom-script-resolve.sh resolver (resolution contract). FOUNDATION.
- loom-oxs.3 (T4): add deploy to canonical_commands; deprecate workflow.json .deploy. FOUNDATION.
- loom-oxs.4 (T5): audit-project recognizer + scaffold + .deploy migration. [dep .1, .3]
- loom-oxs.5 (T6): /wrap-up step 2 uses the resolver. [dep .2]
- loom-oxs.6 (T7): docs/reference/script-convention.md.
- loom-oxs.7 (T8): sweep loom primitives hardcoding test/lint/build. [dep .2]

Wave: .1/.2/.3 independent foundations -> .4/.5/.7 -> .6 anytime.

## Acceptance

- Canonical set documented in loom/decisions drawer + reference docs page (loom-oxs.6).
- templates/scripts/ skeleton exists; /audit-project can scaffold into a fresh repo.
- /wrap-up uses the resolver when present; falls back otherwise.
- A new loom-managed repo can adopt the convention in one /audit-project pass.

## Design source-of-truth

loom-adm decision drawer (loom/decisions, 2026-06-09). Modeled on GitHub "Scripts To Rule Them All". Related: loom-9pr (hardcoded-ref audit, adjacent to loom-oxs.7); Diataxis docs convention (same standardization shape).
close_reason: EPIC COMPLETE 8/8: script/ convention standardized — adm design + .1 templates + .2 resolver + .3 deploy migration + .4 audit recognizer + .5 wrap-up resolver + .6 docs + .7 invariant guard. Suite 86/86.

title: /wrap-up: mechanical discovery of merged-but-open beads in step 1
description:
## Why this exists

`commands/wrap-up.md` step 1 asks the user to enumerate which bead(s) are wrapping up. That puts the user's memory in the critical path: if a session merged 5 beads in parallel-dispatch and the user invokes a bare `/wrap-up`, the command has nothing to bind to. Surfaced 2026-05-27 when loom-7p6.2-.6 were merged to main 2026-05-26 but never closed — the next session caught it via state reconciliation, not via the wrap-up flow.

## What this delivers

Enhance `/wrap-up` step 1 with a discovery sub-step BEFORE the user-enumerate prompt:

1. Grep `git log main` for `Merge loom-XXX:` patterns (and bare `loom-XXX:` subject lines for fast-forward / squash merges) since the last bd close timestamp (or a sensible default like 'since 1 week ago').
2. Extract bead IDs (regex `loom-[a-z0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)*`).
3. For each, call `bd show <id>` and check status; collect any that are still open or in_progress.
4. If non-empty, present as the default close-set: 'Found N beads merged-to-main but still open: ... Close all?' — user can accept, edit, or override.
5. If user enumerates manually anyway, fall through to current behavior.

## Acceptance

- A bare `/wrap-up` after a session that merged N beads surfaces all N as the proposed close-set.
- User still confirms (no auto-close).
- No regression for the single-bead case (the discovery just returns one ID, prompts as before).
- Idempotent: re-running /wrap-up after a successful close finds zero merged-but-open beads.

## Files

- commands/wrap-up.md (primary)
- possibly a small helper script under scripts/ if the grep+cross-check logic is non-trivial enough to extract

## Provenance

Conversation between Frank and Claude 2026-05-27 during loom-7p6 state-reconciliation session. See diary entry diary_wing_claude-opus_20260526_220031711485_4ff3fed1e79b.
close_reason: /wrap-up step 1 now mechanically discovers merged-but-open beads BEFORE asking the user. Shipped in merge ad-hoc (commit 1d5b24d after merge of frank/loom-6p6 + zsh-portability polish). Prefix-agnostic (project-symlinked), inline bash bounded by # DISCOVERY:START/:END markers, 15-case fixture test including zsh portability regression. Dogfooded self-referentially: the new mechanism correctly identifies loom-6p6 itself as merged-but-open. Follow-up loom-9pr tracks audit for other hardcoded 'loom-' / 'main' references in installed primitives.

title: wrap-up section 6 hard-codes loom-only install.sh; should hint at project's own deploy via workflow.json
description:
## Symptom

`/wrap-up` invoked in non-loom projects has been telling users to run loom's `./install.sh` as part of the close ritual. install.sh is loom-specific (symlinks loom files into ~/.claude/); running it from another project is nonsensical.

## Root cause

`commands/wrap-up.md` is symlinked into every project's `~/.claude/commands/`. Section 6 of that file ("(loom only) Re-deploy via install.sh") guards loom-detection with two conditions (lines 75–78):

- `pwd` repo root contains `install.sh` AND `settings.snippet.json`
- `install.sh` header first line is `#!/usr/bin/env bash` AND contains literal `loom install.sh` in its comment

The literal IS still present in loom's install.sh:2 — but the guard is convention-fragile. Claude reading wrap-up.md in a non-loom project often elides the literal-check ("install.sh exists, looks like a deploy script → run it"), surfacing loom-specific guidance to the wrong audience. `install.sh` is a common filename in unrelated projects.

Deeper problem: project-specific deploy guidance is hard-coded into a globally-shared command file. Detection-by-literal-match is brittle.

## Design (decided 2026-05-26 with frank)

**Mechanism:** Add `.deploy` field (string command, optional) to `<project>/.claude/workflow.json` schema. Document in `lib/workflow-config.sh` header alongside `.v`, `.mode`, `.guest`. wrap-up reads it via a new `workflow_resolve_deploy` resolver; if absent or empty → section 6 silently skips.

**Behavior:** Surface only. wrap-up prints `Next step (project deploy): <command>` after section 5 and stops. User decides when to run. No auto-exec.

**Loom's own config:** Set `.deploy: "./install.sh"` in loom's `.claude/workflow.json`. Loom user reading wrap-up output sees the hint and runs install.sh themselves (one keystroke vs current auto-run).

## Scope

In-scope:
- (1) `lib/workflow-config.sh`: add `.deploy` to the documented schema; add `workflow_resolve_deploy` reader (prints command string or empty).
- (2) `commands/wrap-up.md`: replace section 6 entirely. New section: "Surface project deploy hint (if configured)". No loom-specific detection.
- (3) `.claude/workflow.json` (loom's own): set `.deploy: "./install.sh"`.
- (4) `lib/tests/`: add `workflow-config-deploy.test.sh` covering: absent field → empty output, present field → string returned, malformed workflow.json → empty (graceful).

Follow-up beads (not in-scope for this bead):
- (5) `/audit-project` integration: detect projects with no `.deploy` set, prompt user to set or explicitly skip.
- (6) `docs/reference/workflow-json.md`: document the schema if not already documented.

## Acceptance

- `/wrap-up` in loom prints `Next step (project deploy): ./install.sh` and stops; does not auto-run.
- `/wrap-up` in a project without `.deploy` set skips section 6 silently.
- `workflow-config-deploy.test.sh` passes (3 cases: absent, present, malformed).
- No remaining mention of `install.sh` literal-detection in `commands/wrap-up.md`.
- Manual smoke: `/wrap-up` in a non-loom project (e.g., HAW or any other repo with no `.deploy` set) does NOT mention install.sh or loom.

## Cross-references

- Sibling/companion to loom-cuk (filed earlier 2026-05-26): both are about loom-isms leaking into / surviving past their proper scope. loom-cuk is install-time (worktree-path baked into symlinks); this bead is wrap-up-time (loom guidance leaking into other projects' wrap-up).
- workflow.json schema lives in `lib/workflow-config.sh`; existing test coverage in `lib/tests/workflow-config-guest.test.sh`.
close_reason: surface project deploy via workflow.json .deploy field (drops loom-only install.sh literal-match auto-run). Worktree commit 7763c28, merged --no-ff into main. workflow_resolve_deploy + scripts/loom-print-deploy-hint + section 6 rewrite + loom's own workflow.json. 11/11 deploy tests, 21/21 guest sibling no-regression. See drawer drawer_loom_decisions_9fb2868e288751d22c6dd7ec.

title: install.sh from a worktree bakes worktree-path symlinks; worktree cleanup breaks all loom primitives
description:
## Failure mode

`install.sh:17` resolves `LOOM_ROOT` via `cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd`. When install.sh is invoked from inside a worktree path like `.worktrees/loom-yjo/install.sh`, every symlink it creates in `~/.claude/{hooks,agents,commands,lib,scripts}/` bakes that worktree path as the target. The symlinks survive past worktree cleanup → become dangling en masse → every loom primitive silently breaks at once.

## Observed

Session start on 2026-05-26 after the loom-yjo worktree was removed post-merge (commits 6c7fc3d + d4ab97e). Two SessionStart:clear hooks surfaced as visible errors:

- `~/.claude/hooks/workflow-mode-onboarding.sh` → dangling
- `~/.claude/hooks/bd-prime-wrapper.sh` → dangling

A `find ~/.claude -maxdepth 4 -type l ! -exec test -e {} \; -print` showed **95 dangling symlinks** total: hooks (12), agents (4), lib (9), commands (9), scripts (5), plus per-skill/lib/test files.

## Immediate fix applied 2026-05-26

Re-ran `bash /home/frank/repos/loom/install.sh` from the main checkout. install.sh's "Pruning dangling loom-owned symlinks" step (line 98) plus re-linking from $LOOM_ROOT healed all 95 in one pass. Verified 0 dangling remain; both SessionStart hooks smoke-tested exit=0.

## Prevention options

1. **Refuse from worktree.** install.sh detects `git rev-parse --git-dir` returning `.git/worktrees/...` (or `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` not matching the main worktree) and refuses to run, instructing the user to invoke from the main checkout. Simplest, no behavior surprises.

2. **Resolve to main worktree.** install.sh uses `git worktree list --porcelain | awk '/^worktree/ && !seen{print $2; seen=1}'` to find the main worktree, and re-derives `LOOM_ROOT` from there even when invoked from a worktree path. Most ergonomic but couples install.sh to git internals.

3. **Doctor check.** Add a `bd doctor`-adjacent or stand-alone check (`scripts/loom-doctor` or a hook) that scans `find ~/.claude -maxdepth 4 -type l ! -exec test -e {} \;` and flags any dangling symlinks. Complementary to (1) or (2); catches the failure even if it slips through prevention.

Recommended scope: (1) + (3). (1) prevents the root cause cheaply; (3) catches any pre-existing damage and any future variant.

## Acceptance

- install.sh refuses (with a clear error) when invoked from a linked worktree path, OR resolves LOOM_ROOT through the main worktree
- A test under `lib/tests/` exercises the refusal/resolution path
- A doctor-style check exists that reports dangling `~/.claude/` symlinks
- A regression smoke battery (worktree → run install → cleanup worktree → check ~/.claude) is captured somewhere durable
close_reason: install.sh refuse-from-worktree (M4 prevention) + scripts/loom-doctor (M5 dangling-symlink surface) shipped; bug-family lineage to loom-x4m + loom-4um confirmed; reused TOPLEVEL==dirname(COMMON_DIR) idiom from loom-rebase-worktree/loom-worktree-python convention; 17 new tests GREEN; live doctor reports clean; install.sh dry-run from main passes through cleanly; mkdocs --strict clean.

title: bd-state rebase-replay drift: git pull --rebase=merges lands stale issues.jsonl in HEAD (loom-4um follow-up)
description:
Loom-4um (2026-05-15) shipped the .gitattributes merge=bd-export driver to prevent line-based three-way merge from silently reverting closed beads to in_progress. That structural fix covers MERGE but NOT REBASE-REPLAY.

SYMPTOM:
After bd close + git commit + git pull --rebase=merges + git push, the working tree shows .beads/issues.jsonl modified again. Diff shows recently-closed beads reverted to in_progress state in HEAD. Dolt (authoritative) has them correctly CLOSED. Working tree's bd export (regenerated from dolt) is correct.

REPRO (canonical):
1. bd close <id>  (auto-export regenerates issues.jsonl with status:closed)
2. git add .beads/issues.jsonl && git commit
3. git pull --rebase=merges  (rebase replays merges; merge driver fires again during replay)
4. git status shows .beads/issues.jsonl modified
5. git diff shows status reverted to in_progress in HEAD; working tree (re-exported from dolt) has closed

ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS:
git's rebase machinery replays the merge driver against the ORIGINAL pre-merge tree state, not the post-bd-close state. The driver runs bd export, but the dolt state inside that replay context may be the pre-close snapshot. Need to verify by reading scripts/bd-merge-driver.sh + adding diagnostic logging.

HITS OBSERVED (2 in 2 consecutive days):
- 2026-05-25 -> commit db8fc74 'bd: post-close issues.jsonl re-export for loom-x2q' (yesterday)
- 2026-05-26 -> commit c8ebc6b 'bd: post-rebase issues.jsonl re-export (loom-ad1 + loom-tww closed)' (this session)

Both hits resolved by post-rebase re-export commit (working tree's bd export is authoritative; just commit it).

CURRENT WORKAROUND (works but noisy):
After git pull --rebase=merges, if git status shows .beads/issues.jsonl modified:
  git add .beads/issues.jsonl
  git commit -m 'bd: post-rebase issues.jsonl re-export'
  git push

CANDIDATE STRUCTURAL FIX (next session investigates):
Install a 'post-rewrite' git hook that runs 'bd export' after rebase completes. Hook fires on rebase + commit --amend; resyncs issues.jsonl from dolt unconditionally. Composes with the existing merge-driver fix.

Hook file would go in hooks/post-rewrite.sh, wired via install.sh into .git/hooks/post-rewrite (similar to how install.sh wires hooks/pre-push-mkdocs-strict.sh).

LINEAGE:
- Parent: loom-4um (2026-05-15) — original three-way-merge structural fix via .gitattributes + merge driver
- This is a follow-up for the same bug class (bd-state silent drift) but a DIFFERENT git operation (rebase vs merge)
- Related: pull.rebase=merges is a loom convention (see CLAUDE.md 'Session Completion' section) — preserves --no-ff bead-merge bubbles. Switching to plain merge-pull is NOT a fix; it loses bubble preservation.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. Diagnostic logging added to scripts/bd-merge-driver.sh confirms which code path fires during rebase replay vs initial merge.
2. Structural fix shipped (likely hooks/post-rewrite.sh + install.sh wiring + regression test).
3. lib/tests/ test fixture reproduces the rebase-replay scenario and asserts post-rewrite hook restores correct state.
4. CLAUDE.md 'Session Completion' section updated: the post-rebase re-export workaround line gets struck through or removed.

NOTES:
- N=2 in 2 days qualified this for P2 not P3.
- Plain git merge (no rebase) does NOT exhibit this — only --rebase=merges. Suggests the bug is in the replay path, not the merge-driver itself.
- The drift is COSMETIC: dolt is always correct, so 'git push' after the workaround commit yields the right state on origin. Risk is human confusion + commit noise, not data loss.
close_reason: Shipped hooks/post-rewrite.sh + install.sh wiring + lib/tests/bd-post-rewrite.test.sh (25 fixture cases including end-to-end real git rebase). Composes orthogonally with loom-4um bd-merge-driver (merge) and loom-x4m bd-worktree-preseed (fresh worktree). Hook re-exports from dolt after rebase/amend and auto-commits the delta if jsonl diverges from HEAD; degrades to no-op when bd unavailable, detached HEAD, staged changes, or .beads absent. Env bypasses: LOOM_BD_POST_REWRITE_SKIP=1, LOOM_BD_POST_REWRITE_NO_COMMIT=1. Full loom suite 40/41 (1 pre-existing loom-n1q FAIL). Merged via 58cb2e6 (bd-claim) + merge bubble. Acceptance criterion 1 (diagnostic logging in bd-merge-driver) deferred to closing drawer — structural fix is symptom-targeted.

title: docs-scaffold: DOCS-SCAFFOLD-FIXME sentinel convention for unreplaced placeholders (loom-vca gap 3)
description:
Closes the third + final gap under loom-vca umbrella. mforth (downstream loom-managed project) shipped templates/diataxis/docs/how-to/install.md's placeholder prose verbatim because the scaffold provides no signal that the file is unreplaced. Fix: introduce a DOCS-SCAFFOLD-FIXME broken-link sentinel that mkdocs build --strict catches via its built-in link checker (zero new infrastructure). See design drawer drawer_loom_decisions_56bfe66ecf7d9db3a3c555b0 for the locked design.

INVENTORY: 4 sentinels across 3 template files:
- templates/diataxis/docs/how-to/install.md.template (×2: install-cmd, install-verify)
- templates/diataxis/docs/tutorials/getting-started.md.template (×1: quickstart-invocation)
- templates/diataxis/docs/explanation/mental-model.md.template (×1: mental-model, page-level)

SENTINEL SHAPE:
[!! DOCS-SCAFFOLD-FIXME: replace this section before publish !!](docs-scaffold-fixme-<token>)

Own line, directly above each placeholder block. Per-token diagnostic targets so mkdocs error names the specific instance.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. Four sentinel lines added per inventory above.
2. lib/tests/diataxis-template.test.sh extended with RED + GREEN assertions: scaffold without replacement -> mkdocs build --strict fails AND each of the 4 tokens appears in stderr; sed-replace all four -> mkdocs build --strict passes.
3. New docs/reference/docs-scaffold-fixme.md reference page (~100 lines): what the sentinel is, why hard-fail, how to replace, inventory of tokens, grep self-audit instructions.
4. /docs-scaffold command's M6 summary mentions the sentinels + the grep -r DOCS-SCAFFOLD-FIXME docs/ self-audit.
5. README.docs-pointer.md.template gets one sentence pointing at the grep self-audit.
6. mkdocs.yml.template gets a one-line comment referencing the convention.

OUT OF SCOPE (YAGNI):
- Dedicated /docs-doctor slash command. Bare grep is sufficient.
- mkdocs Python plugin / hooks. Broken-link weaponization gives the same forcing function with zero new infra.

LINEAGE:
- Parent: loom-vca umbrella (this is gap 3; on merge umbrella auto-closes).
- Wave 1 ship drawer: drawer_loom_decisions_682c0af9c299538551e0b026 (loom-ad1 + loom-tww, 2026-05-26).
- Design lock drawer: drawer_loom_decisions_56bfe66ecf7d9db3a3c555b0 (2026-05-26).
- mforth downstream ref (the EDIT this MECHANISM prevents recurrence of): commit 3cef9df.

EXECUTION:
Activity recipe feature-a-bead. Worktree on frank/<this-bead-id>. TDD: write RED test first (assert mkdocs --strict fails + names all 4 tokens), then add sentinels (going GREEN), then GREEN regression assertion.

PARKED for fresh-session execution per /wrap-up brainstorm 2026-05-26.
close_reason: Shipped DOCS-SCAFFOLD-FIXME sentinel convention (4 sentinels across 3 templates) + lib/tests/diataxis-template.test.sh RED+GREEN extension (24/0 post-impl) + docs/reference/docs-scaffold-fixme.md reference page + skill/template/mkdocs.yml.template surfaces. Merged at this commit. Closes loom-vca umbrella gap 3.

title: edit-after-failure-guard: false positives on git merge CONFLICT text + bypass env var can't propagate from tool calls
description:
The hook trips on benign signals that don't represent test/build failures, blocking edits the agent legitimately needs to make.

REPRODUCTION (observed in mforth session 2026-05-24, 3/3 docs subagents + main orchestrator hit it)

Symptom 1 — git merge conflict output as 'failure':
A parallel-dispatch wave's serial-merge step runs:
    git merge --no-ff <branch>
which on the second + later merges produces:
    Auto-merging .beads/issues.jsonl
    CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in .beads/issues.jsonl
    Auto-merging mkdocs.yml
    CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in mkdocs.yml
    Automatic merge failed; fix conflicts and then commit the result.
plus the Bash tool wrapping it with 'Exit code 1'.

The hook then refuses the very next Edit/Write — which is the conflict-resolution edit the workflow REQUIRES. The agent is being blocked from doing the thing the previous command literally instructed it to do.

Likely FAIL_RE match (hook's pattern after loom-7j5 hardening):
    r'\bexit code:\s*[1-9]'
matches against the Bash tool's 'Exit code 1' framing of the merge-with-conflicts exit, OR
    one of the failed-test patterns is matching 'merge failed' or similar.

Symptom 2 — Python traceback from benign exploration:
A subagent ran a one-shot 'python3 -c …' for a docs spot-check, forgot PYTHONPATH=src, got an ImportError traceback. The traceback was followed immediately by the successful spot-check (with PYTHONPATH set), and dozens of passing operations after that. Hook stayed in 'failure observed' state for the rest of the session.

The pattern r'\bTraceback \(most recent call last\)' fires on ANY traceback, including ones the agent recovered from in the next command. There's no notion of 'recovered' or 'TTL' on the failure signal.

Symptom 3 — bypass env var doesn't propagate:
LOOM_EDIT_AFTER_FAILURE_GUARD_SKIP=1 only works if set in the harness's process environment. Setting it via a Bash tool call (export VAR=1) doesn't affect the hook's env because the hook is spawned by the harness, not by the agent's shell.

This means the documented bypass is effectively unavailable to agents at runtime — only available to the human running 'claude code' if they remembered to export it at launch.

IMPACT
- Forced workarounds: agents route Write/Edit through Bash heredocs (cat > file <<EOF) and Python string-replace one-liners (python3 -c). Works, but introduces noise and bypasses the Edit tool's diff-display.
- In a parallel-dispatch wave, the orchestrator hits this on every merge after the first (the merge conflict signal latches).
- Doc-shape beads spend disproportionate time fighting the hook for what should be one-line nav edits.

SUGGESTED FIXES (in increasing scope)

1. Tighten FAIL_RE to NOT match git-merge CONFLICT/conflict-resolution text. Specifically, exclude:
   - 'Auto-merging ' or 'CONFLICT (content):' framing → these are merge-state indicators, not failure markers
   - The 'Exit code 1' framing when the preceding stdout mentions 'CONFLICT' — git uses exit 1 to signal 'conflicts present, fix and commit', which IS the resolution opportunity

2. Add a TTL or 'recovered' notion: if the last Bash result was successful AND it touched files mentioned in the prior failure trace, treat the failure as recovered. Or: only count failures from the last 1-2 Bash calls, not arbitrary tail history.

3. Surface the bypass to agents at runtime. Options:
   - Read bypass from a per-project file (e.g., .loom/no-edit-after-failure-guard) that agents CAN create
   - Read bypass from a header in the tool_input ({"file_path": ..., "_loom_skip_guard": true}) — agents can include it when intentionally bypassing
   - Read bypass from a sentinel in the file path or commit message

ACCEPTANCE
- Repro 1 (parallel-dispatch wave with 3+ merges) no longer trips the hook on the merge-resolution edits.
- Repro 2 (single Python traceback followed by recovery) no longer latches for the rest of the session.
- Either bypass is reachable from agent tool calls, OR (preferred) the false-positive patterns above are tightened so bypass isn't needed for these cases.
notes:
Surfaced empirically during the mforth docs gap survey session 2026-05-24.

Cross-references in the mforth wing (mempalace_search 'mforth docs survey' OR 'parallel-dispatch'):
- drawer_mforth_decisions_3ebc7434c1efc22b93b428a1 — how-to wave ship report, includes 'edit-after-failure-guard friction' section with the full reproduction context and the suggested fix shape
- drawer_mforth_decisions_3b6d475201c08f5fbd03f0b7 — reference wave ship report (first wave that hit symptom 1)
- KG triple: 'loom edit-after-failure-guard hook → false_positive_triggers → git merge CONFLICT text + Python tracebacks from benign spot-checks; bypass env var doesn't propagate from tool calls'

Successor to loom-7j5 (which hardened FAIL_RE the first time after a similar prose-false-positive incident).

Hook source: /home/frank/repos/loom/hooks/edit-after-failure-guard.sh (FAIL_RE is at lines ~115-127)

Test scaffolding already exists: /home/frank/repos/loom/lib/tests/edit-after-failure-guard.test.sh — add regression cases for both symptoms here.
close_reason: edit-after-failure-guard false-positive trifecta + hermetic tests shipped. CONFLICT whitelist (Auto-merging/Automatic merge failed → clear latch), last-Bash-only TTL (clean Bash clears prior failure), per-project .claude/no-edit-after-failure-guard marker bypass, test fixture unsets LOOM_EDIT_AFTER_FAILURE_GUARD_SKIP at startup. Worktree commit a40aaf8, merged --no-ff into main. 45/45 fixture tests + full loom suite green + mkdocs --strict clean + e2e smoke confirmed. Successor of loom-7j5. See drawer drawer_loom_decisions_39d19d547859e7fc72586d4c.

title: UMBRELLA: docs-scaffold template polish post-real-publish-usage (mforth surfaced 3 gaps)
description:
2026-05-24 — mforth is the first real-world consumer-facing-publish stress test of /docs-scaffold. Three gaps surfaced same day, all sharing the root pattern: 'template ships content/config sized for a contributor audience or as a placeholder, which doesn't serve well as published end-user surface and provides no build-time signal that it should be customized.'

THREE MANIFESTATIONS:

1. **loom-ad1** (P2 bug) — templates/diataxis/mkdocs.yml.template missing pymdownx.emoji config. Causes :material-* and :octicons-* icon shortcodes (used by the template's own docs/index.md grid-cards landing) to render as literal text. RENDERING REGRESSION. Downstream mforth fix: commit 1ab928c.

2. **loom-tww** (P2 bug) — templates/diataxis/docs/{tutorials,how-to,reference,explanation}/index.md ship contributor-facing '## Discipline' h2 sections (~14 lines each, ~60 total) enumerating Diataxis discipline rules. Reads weirdly to end-users landing on published quadrant indexes. AESTHETIC/AUDIENCE REGRESSION. Downstream mforth fix: commit 419554b.

3. **(this umbrella) — templates/diataxis/docs/how-to/install.md** ships explicit placeholder text 'Replace this step with the project's actual installation command' with a Go/Python/Node/Bash menu of common shapes. Was never replaced for mforth → published site shipped the placeholder verbatim. The placeholder is honest (says 'replace this') but the scaffold provides no signal that the file is unreplaced — no build error, no warning, no FIXME marker. UNREPLACED-PLACEHOLDER REGRESSION. Downstream mforth fix: commit 3cef9df.

UNDERLYING ISSUE:
The scaffold treats the template as a starting point the consumer will hand-edit. That's the right intent. But:
- (a) Some content is wrong for ANY consumer (gaps 1+2) — no consumer-edit will fix mkdocs.yml missing pymdownx.emoji + ALL consumers should drop the Discipline meta. These should be FIXED in the template itself.
- (b) Some content IS rightfully a placeholder (gap 3 — install commands are genuinely project-specific) — but the scaffold provides NO MECHANISM that ensures the consumer notices + replaces. No build-time check, no scaffold-output reminder list, no FIXME-style sentinel that lints catch.

RECOMMENDED FIXES (multi-bead — this umbrella tracks the family):
- For (a): direct template fixes per loom-ad1 + loom-tww.
- For (b): introduce a MARKER convention in template placeholders. Either:
  * Literal sentinel like '!!UNREPLACED_PLACEHOLDER!!' that mkdocs build --strict treats as an error
  * Or a comment like '<!-- DOCS-SCAFFOLD-FIXME: replace this section before publish -->' that the scaffold appends + a /docs-doctor command (new) scans for and warns about
  * Or augment the scaffold's M6 'Next steps' summary to enumerate the placeholder files that need user-editing before publish (templates that contain 'Replace this step' or similar markers → list explicitly)

PAIRS WITH loom-ad1 + loom-tww (children of this umbrella). Future gaps in the same family should attach here.

Future-USR's downstream FYI: until this umbrella is addressed upstream, future loom-managed projects using /docs-scaffold will need to manually audit their docs/ post-scaffold for the same three issues. mforth's 3 commits (1ab928c, 419554b, 3cef9df) are the canonical reference for the downstream fixes.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: docs-scaffold quadrant indexes ship contributor-facing Discipline sections to end-user docs sites
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-24 by mforth (downstream loom-managed project) — USR noticed the published Material site at https://fkberthold.github.io/mforth/tutorials/ has a 'Discipline' section between the intro paragraph and the 'Start here' content list. The Discipline section enumerates the Diataxis rules for what content belongs in that quadrant ('A tutorial earns its place when it names the defined starting point...'). Reads weirdly for users; reads correctly for docs contributors.

The pattern is consistent across ALL FOUR quadrant indexes:
* templates/diataxis/docs/tutorials/index.md — '## Discipline' section ~14 lines
* templates/diataxis/docs/how-to/index.md — '## Discipline' section ~16 lines
* templates/diataxis/docs/reference/index.md — '## Discipline' section ~14 lines
* templates/diataxis/docs/explanation/index.md — '## Discipline' section ~15 lines

Total: ~60 lines of contributor-facing meta-content shipped to every project that runs /docs-scaffold + publishes their site.

This is different from loom-ad1 (missing pymdownx.emoji): loom-ad1 is a rendering regression (icons leak as literal text), this is content-shape (the content renders fine but is wrong for the audience).

ROOT THINKING: the loom-template documents Diataxis to docs writers learning the discipline. That's correct for loom-internal/loom-template-consumer onboarding, WRONG to ship into the consumer project's published site verbatim. Each quadrant's intro paragraph + 'What X is not' navigation blockquote already communicate the discipline implicitly (the prose voice + the cross-links). The explicit '## Discipline' h2 section is redundant for users + odd-looking.

FIX OPTIONS:

(a) Delete the '## Discipline' sections from all 4 templates. Cleanest. The intro paragraphs already orient users; cross-links to the other quadrants handle the 'what is not' framing.

(b) Move the Discipline content into a single 'docs/about-these-docs.md' page in the template, linked from a footer or sidebar. Preserves the discipline-as-prose for projects that want to dogfood Diataxis discipline as part of their published site (loom itself probably wants this).

(c) Wrap the Discipline sections in HTML comments OR gate them behind a scaffold flag (--include-discipline) defaulting OFF. Lets sophisticated projects re-enable them by editing the index.md after scaffold lands.

Recommend (b): move into a single about-these-docs.md page that loom-managed projects can choose to link or not. Preserves the dogfooded discipline content (which IS valuable as a reference for the project's own docs contributors) without polluting end-user landing pages. The scaffold could nav-link it under a 'Meta' or 'About' section (off by default in nav, present as a file).

DOWNSTREAM mforth FIX commit (reference): https://github.com/fkberthold/mforth/commit/419554b (took option a — simple deletion across all 4 indexes, 67 lines removed).

ACCEPTANCE:
* All four templates/diataxis/docs/{tutorials,how-to,reference,explanation}/index.md no longer ship the '## Discipline' h2 section verbatim on the user-facing landing page.
* Decision recorded in a MemPalace drawer about whether to delete (a) or relocate (b) — capture upstream rationale.
* Re-running /docs-scaffold against a fresh project produces user-facing quadrant indexes by default.

PAIRS WITH loom-ad1 — both are 'template ships content/config sized for a contributor audience that doesn't serve well as published end-user content/config' regressions surfaced by the same downstream mforth publish flow. Worth filing a tiny umbrella for 'docs-scaffold template polish post-real-publish-usage' if more emerge.
close_reason: Removed contributor-facing ## Discipline h2 sections from all 4 templates/diataxis/docs/{tutorials,how-to,reference,explanation}/index.md (option a). 67 lines removed, matches mforth downstream commit 419554b exactly. Test extended in lib/tests/diataxis-template.test.sh with 4 absence-of-Discipline assertions. 17/17 green including mkdocs build --strict integration. Merged 44cc689, worker commit f2251fb.

title: docs-scaffold mkdocs.yml.template missing pymdownx.emoji breaks Material grid-cards icons on landing page
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-24 by mforth (downstream loom-managed project) using /docs-scaffold then seeing literal ':material-school:{ .lg .middle }' on the published Diataxis front page instead of rendered SVG icons.

ROOT CAUSE: templates/diataxis/mkdocs.yml.template's markdown_extensions block includes:
  - attr_list (good — needed for the icon-attribute syntax)
  - admonition, def_list, footnotes, md_in_html, tables, toc
  - pymdownx.details, pymdownx.highlight, pymdownx.inlinehilite,
    pymdownx.snippets, pymdownx.superfences, pymdownx.tabbed
But NOT pymdownx.emoji. The template's docs/index.md.template uses Material's grid-cards UI pattern (the canonical Diataxis landing-page idiom):

  <div class="grid cards" markdown>
  -   :material-school:{ .lg .middle } **New here — teach me**
      [:octicons-arrow-right-24: Tutorials](tutorials/index.md)
  -   :material-tools:{ .lg .middle } **I'm trying to do a specific thing**
  ...

Without pymdownx.emoji + Material's icon-emoji-index, those :material-*: and :octicons-*: shortcodes leak through as literal text in the rendered HTML. Visible regression on every project that scaffolds + publishes.

FIX (3 lines in templates/diataxis/mkdocs.yml.template, after pymdownx.details):

  - pymdownx.emoji:
      emoji_index: !!python/name:material.extensions.emoji.twemoji
      emoji_generator: !!python/name:material.extensions.emoji.to_svg

Note: 'material.extensions.emoji' is the namespace for mkdocs-material 9.x. Older 'materialx.emoji' is for pre-9. mkdocs-material 9 has been stable since 2023; pinning to material.extensions.emoji is correct for current usage.

DOWNSTREAM mforth FIX commit (reference): https://github.com/fkberthold/mforth/commit/1ab928c

ACCEPTANCE:
* templates/diataxis/mkdocs.yml.template gains the pymdownx.emoji block in markdown_extensions
* templates/diataxis/docs/index.md.template's icon shortcodes render as SVGs (not raw text) in mkdocs build output
* Re-running /docs-scaffold against a fresh project produces a working grid-cards front page out of the box
* requirements.txt continues to pin mkdocs-material>=9.5,<10 (no version bump needed)

KG TRIPLE filed in mforth MemPalace: '/docs-scaffold mkdocs.yml.template → missing pymdownx.emoji extension config breaks → Material grid-cards icon shortcodes on the Diataxis landing page'.

This is a small template patch that should ship with no migration needed — adding the extension is purely additive.
close_reason: docs-scaffold mkdocs.yml.template gains pymdownx.emoji block (3 lines after pymdownx.details). New regression test lib/tests/diataxis-template-pymdownx-emoji.test.sh: 4/4 green. End-to-end verified: scaffolded tmp project, mkdocs build --strict rc=0, 22 inline SVGs rendered, 0 literal :material-school: text. Merged 95b3829, worker commit cda3730. Mirrors mforth downstream fix 1ab928c.

title: /audit-project: detect Python preflight + solo workspace, offer overrides
description:
On fresh non-Go non-team projects, `/audit-project` currently passes its checks
but leaves two defaults wrong for the project's shape:

1. bd's `preflight.template` ships with a Go checklist (`go test`, `go vet`, ...).
   Python, Rust, Node projects inherit it and see meaningless preflight output.
2. The CLAUDE.md "BEADS INTEGRATION" block generated by `bd init` hardcodes
   `bd dolt push` + `git push` lines that error/no-op on solo workspaces
   (no Dolt remote configured). loom-hsb shipped the guard pattern in loom's
   own CLAUDE.md, but new projects don't get it.

Surfaced by `/audit-project` against fresh ~/repos/mforth (Python, solo) on
2026-05-21 — both fell through the cracks since current checks only validate
loom-managed shape, not language or workspace topology.

Extends `/audit-project` to detect language (via canonical markers) and
workspace mode (via `bd dolt remote list`), surface a check row per the
existing audit-apply-flags pattern, and offer mechanical fixes for the
common cases. Greenfields-friendly: when no language markers exist yet,
prompts the user interactively (with a "skip" path that's remembered so
re-runs don't re-nag).
design:
## Detection logic (in agents/project-onboarder.md)

**detect_project_language() → python | go | rust | node | shell | unknown**
- python: pyproject.toml / setup.py / setup.cfg / requirements*.txt
- go: go.mod
- rust: Cargo.toml
- node: package.json (and NOT also pyproject.toml)
- shell: scripts/ + *.sh + bats fixtures, no other markers
- unknown: none of the above OR polyglot (tie-break: never guess)

**is_solo_workspace() → bool**
- TRUE if `bd dolt remote list --json` returns [] (no remotes)
- FALSE if any remote
- TRUE if command errors (degrade-safe; tolerate old bd / missing dolt)

## Check rules

`preflight-language-match`:
- PROMPT if language=unknown AND preflight.template is unset OR bd-default
  (user picks language; write override to .beads/config; PASS)
- WARN if language=python/rust/node AND preflight.template starts with "go"
- PASS otherwise (incl. polyglot — surface unknown, don't arbitrate)
- "skip" answer writes .claude/loom-audit-state.json memo → silent PASS on re-runs

`claude-md-solo-aware`:
- WARN if is_solo_workspace() AND CLAUDE.md BEADS INTEGRATION block has
  unguarded `bd dolt push` line (regex: not wrapped in loom-hsb-style
  `if bd dolt remote list ...; then ... fi` guard)
- PASS otherwise (incl. no BEADS INTEGRATION block at all)
- Fix mechanically rewrites the canonical block to use loom-hsb guard shape
- If block has been hand-edited beyond recognition, fix refuses with message;
  user gets pointer to loom CLAUDE.md as reference shape

## User-facing flow

Both checks render via existing PASS/WARN/PROMPT row pattern. WARNs offer
y/N/skip with diff preview before applying. Skip path writes state-file memo.

## State file

`.claude/loom-audit-state.json` (per-project, gitignored). Stores per-check
skip memos so re-runs respect "user said no". Not a config file — never
read by anything except `/audit-project`.

## Test mocking surface
