- Possibly a tiny bd wrapper at ~/.local/bin/bd-loom that exec's bd with --workspace
- lib/tests/bd-resolve.bats (new)

Approach (pick whichever bd actually supports — verify by reading bd --help):
- Option A: bd respects BEADS_DIR env var → export it from session startup.
- Option B: bd has a --workspace flag → wrap bd in a shim that injects it.
- Option C: bd walks up from cwd looking for .beads/ → create a symlink at ~/.loom/guests/<repo-key>/.beads/ inside the repo (defeats guest mode; reject).

Repo-key: <basename(toplevel)>-<sha8(toplevel-path)>. Example: ~/repos/atlas → atlas-a3f8b2c1.

Steps:
1. Investigate bd's workspace resolution; pick option A or B.
2. Write bats covering: in non-guest mode, bd resolves cwd .beads/; in guest+host mode, bd resolves cwd .beads/ (host's); in guest+personal mode, bd resolves external workspace.
3. Implement.
4. Add `loom guest init` initialization step that creates the external .beads/ directory and runs `bd init` against it (when bd_mode=personal is chosen).

Acceptance: bats pass; bd ready in a guest+personal repo only shows beads from the external workspace.

Depends on: task 2 (workflow-state guest.bd_mode + repo_key). Coordinates with task 1 (which initializes bd_mode and triggers external bd init).

title: Loom guest mode — workflow without polluting host repos
description:
Goal: A per-repo flag that lets loom run its full workflow (recipes, hooks, MemPalace capture, statusline) inside repos where the user is a guest, without writing artifacts to the host tree.

Design: drawer_loom_decisions_12d7f8163e8855be037a007c (loom/decisions, 2026-05-06).

Activation: /loom-guest on (persistent), /loom-guest off (reversible), /loom-guest status. No auto-detect.

Storage: in-tree files (workflow.json, settings.json) hidden via .git/info/exclude (per-clone, not committed). docs/ never scaffolded. .beads/ uses host's if present, otherwise optional external personal workspace at ~/.loom/guests/<repo-key>/.beads/. MemPalace wing uses same project-named convention (concern is not polluting host repos, not siloing palace).

bd integration — three cases:
- A: host has bd → use it; bd remember disabled (would commit); bd dolt push disabled unless host has remote.
- B: no host bd → prompt once for personal-external or skip.
- C: --personal-bd flag forces case B even when host bd exists.

Hooks/recipes: /docs-scaffold and /audit-project AUTOFIX hard-refuse with /loom-guest off hint. Activity recipes, MemPalace capture, code-review skills run unchanged. Statusline shows [GUEST] (or [GUEST/personal-bd]) prominently. Drawer-author prefixes guest-project drawers with [guest project: <repo>].

9 child tasks. Parallel-safe: 1, 6, 9. Sequential: 2 unblocks 3-5, 7, 8.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: bd-close-capture: real artifact verification + regex fix + honest error message
description:
The bd-close-capture PreToolUse hook is broken in two ways and degrades workflow integrity. Locked design lives in MemPalace drawer drawer_loom_decisions_2fbf2d5f4c0f5e50ab84e628 (loom/decisions, 2026-05-06).

CURRENT STATE (verified by reading hooks/bd-close-capture.sh end-to-end):

Bug A — cosmetic regex captures words from --reason text. Line 42 greps the entire command line, so `bd close liza_base-dab --reason "...re-dispatch...rule-based..."` parses out tokens like 're-dispatch' and 'rule-based' as if they were bead IDs. Confusing error messages.

Bug B — the hook performs no verification at all. In full mode with no bypass flag, it unconditionally exits 2. There is no MemPalace query, no drawer lookup, no KG check, no diary check. The error message ("until the bead's decision drawer + KG triples + diary entry have been captured") implies a verification the code does not perform.

Consequence: /wrap-up itself can't close cleanly. Its step 4 runs bare `bd close`, which hits the unconditional block. Every close path requires BD_CLOSE_FORCE=1, including the canonical ritual. Two consecutive liza_base trials (2026-05-04, 2026-05-05) had to bypass.

NEW DESIGN (locked):

Pure (b) — real artifact verification with broader matchers. The hook scans for evidence of capture for <bead-id> in any of these forms:

1. Drawer in any room of the project's wing (not just `decisions`) mentioning the bead ID
2. KG triple where subject or object references the bead ID or its work
3. Diary entry mentioning the bead ID since claim time
4. bd memory containing or tagged with the bead ID
5. Substantive close --reason — ≥200 chars AND references a commit SHA or drawer ID

Pass condition: ANY ONE of the five. Block only when ZERO evidence exists.

Why broader matchers: surveyed liza_base (135 drawers across 5 rooms) and tla_puzzles (213 drawers across 11 rooms — audit_results, curriculum, pedagogy_rules, expertise, etc.). Drawers don't only live in `decisions`. Wave-batch closes, repairs, and epic roll-ups have lineage in substantive close_reasons or sibling drawers, not always in their own canonical triple.

Why pure (b) over (c) sentinel-from-/wrap-up: /wrap-up can fail mid-ritual (mempalace -32000 errors), subagent-to-file gap (drawer drafted but never filed), explicit user skip (no drawer flag). Sentinel would silently grant lineage-loss. Verification catches /wrap-up's own bugs — defense in depth. Cost is cheap: filesystem grep against MemPalace JSON files is millisecond-fast and race-free.

ERROR MESSAGE FORMAT (mandatory: explicit "what's missing"):

```
[bd-close-capture hook] No capture evidence found for beads-cve.

Looked for (need ANY ONE):
  ✗ Drawer in liza_base/* mentioning beads-cve
  ✗ KG triple referencing beads-cve
  ✗ Diary entry since claim mentioning beads-cve
  ✗ bd memory tagged with beads-cve
  ✗ Substantive close --reason (≥200 chars + commit/drawer ID)

Recommended:
  /wrap-up                            — full ritual (drawer + KG + diary)

Or, for wave-batch closes, add lineage to --reason:
  bd close beads-cve --reason "Wave 1 voice pass: filter haiku→sonnet,
  drives saturation 1.0→0.8. Commit abc1234. Sibling drawer
  drawer_liza_base_decisions_<id>."

Bypass (use sparingly):
  BD_CLOSE_FORCE=1 bd close beads-cve   # auditable; recorded in workflow state
```

When SOME evidence is found, mark each matcher with ✓/✗ and allow with a one-line warning if at least one matcher passed.

SCOPE (single hook file, single bead, single branch):

1. Replace unconditional block with 5-matcher artifact verification
2. Fix bead-ID regex to parse only the `bd close <ids...>` arg list, ignore --reason "..." contents
3. Map bead-prefix → MemPalace wing (reuse loom-c9z's wing auto-detect from lib)
4. Filesystem-direct queries for drawer/KG/diary (no MCP cache lag, race-free per loom-2xh)
5. Rewrite error message to enumerate what's missing + actionable fixes
6. Shell-fixture tests under lib/tests/ covering: full-ritual pass, wave-batch substantive-reason pass, missing-everything block, regex-parses-only-bead-args, multi-room drawer match
7. Once shipped: supersede loom-2xh — its race diagnosis is wrong; this fix resolves the same trial symptom

Bypass paths preserved unchanged: --force, BD_CLOSE_FORCE=1, mode != full. Goal is to make the canonical path actually work so bypass becomes the genuine exception again.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Bare `bd close <id>` succeeds when any of the 5 matchers fires; blocks otherwise with the explicit message
- /wrap-up's step 4 close succeeds without BD_CLOSE_FORCE in full mode
- Cosmetic regex bug fixed: --reason text no longer leaks into BEAD_IDS
- Test suite under lib/tests/ covers all 5 matchers + the regex scope
- loom-2xh closed or superseded once this lands

REFERENCES:
- Drawer (verbatim design + reasoning): drawer_loom_decisions_2fbf2d5f4c0f5e50ab84e628
- Locked decision #2 (2026-05-02): hundred_acre_woods/decisions WORKFLOW INFRASTRUCTURE PLAN
- Source trials: liza_base 2026-05-04 + 2026-05-05 BD_CLOSE_FORCE fallbacks
- Related (misdiagnosed): loom-2xh
close_reason: Real artifact verification + regex fix shipped (commits ab1a9de, f71b631; merge 8295102). 5-matcher kernel via python3+sqlite3 reads against palace/chroma.sqlite3 (FTS5) + palace/knowledge_graph.sqlite3 (triples). Bug A regex scoped to bd close arg list via shlex (--reason content cannot leak). Tests 30/30 GREEN, full suite 108/108. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_791d132361dd2875f5555e71. Self-test reported 3/5 matchers (✓✓✓✗✗) on bare bd close, the first canonical close path that actually verifies.

title: bead-lifecycle-shell: within-bead M-step parallelism nudge
description:
Extend the existing 'Decision: parallel vs sequential' section of skills/bead-lifecycle-shell/SKILL.md to cover within-bead parallelism in addition to across-bead. Today the section only addresses bead-level parallelism (when bd ready shows multiple unblocked beads); the new sub-section covers M-step parallelism within a single bead's variable middle.

The nudge: when an activity recipe's variable middle has 2+ independent M-steps (different files, no shared state, no sequential dependency), dispatch them via superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents with isolation: worktree. Cost-payoff threshold: parallelism wins when each task is non-trivial (more than a one-line edit) — for tiny edits, sequential is faster than dispatch overhead. Cross-reference the loom-tag worker-leak finding (drawer_loom_decisions_df73c725b47dd67832935e3a): parallel workers must use relative paths in prompts to stay sandboxed inside the worktree.

Cross-reference the creation-time CLAUDE.md heuristic (loom-XXXa) — the heuristics are paired.

~10 lines added. Umbrella: loom-csu.
close_reason: parallelism prompts shipped: CLAUDE.md splitting heuristic + bead-lifecycle-shell within-bead nudge

title: CLAUDE.md: bead-creation splitting heuristic (creation-time fan-out prompt)
description:
Add a 'Splitting heuristic at bead creation' rule to CLAUDE.md (loom's project-instructions Conventions section): when filing 2+ candidate items, ask whether they are independent (no shared files, no sequential dependency); if yes, file as sibling beads under an umbrella feature/epic; if no, file as one bead.

The heuristic is project-instructions level (CLAUDE.md), not a plugin skill, because the bead-creation skills (beadpowers:create-beads, beadpowers:brainstorming) are plugin-owned and not loom-editable. CLAUDE.md is loom-owned, loaded at session start, and applies whenever bd create is being authored.

Cross-reference the recipe-middle within-bead parallel nudge (loom-XXXb in bead-lifecycle-shell) — the heuristics are paired: this one prevents the slip at the source; the recipe-middle one catches what slips through.

~5 lines added. Umbrella: loom-csu.

title: Parallelism prompts at bead-creation + recipe-middle (umbrella)
description:
Umbrella feature for two small adds that systematize the 'can this fan out?' question. Two miss-classes today: (a) at bead-creation, candidate items get filed as one bead with bullet-list subtasks instead of sibling beads under an epic — bd ready never surfaces them as parallel; (b) at recipe-middle, independent M-steps within a bead get done sequentially because no nudge points at superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents.

Children: loom-XXXa (CLAUDE.md splitting heuristic), loom-XXXb (bead-lifecycle-shell within-bead parallel nudge).

Loom-tag worker-leak finding (drawer_loom_decisions_df73c725b47dd67832935e3a) is per-dispatch, not per-prompt, so adding two prompts does not multiply that hazard. Each child cross-references the loom-tag finding so workers use relative paths.

Lineage: 2026-05-05 conversation following loom-c9z close — user observation that parallelism is under-prompted both at design-time and execution-time, with both worth catching.

title: lib/docs-generated.sh: Signal 2a false-positives on documentation comments + echo strings
description:
The docs-generated detector's Signal 2a regex (looks for 'docs/' tokens in scripts/*.sh, Makefile, package.json, pyproject.toml) fires on documentation cross-references inside scripts that aren't actually building docs/. Two false-positive sources observed in loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-05:

- bootstrap_liza_home.sh L110 — comment: '# Subdirectories per the v2 design (docs/v2-design.md...)'
- bootstrap_liza_home.sh L520 — echo string: '(See ${LIZA_BASE}/docs/setup.md...)'

Neither is a build target. The detector treats them as evidence that docs/ is generated, which would cause /audit-project AND /docs-scaffold to refuse on this project. Both customer trials (loom-b6o, loom-wxo) involved generated-OR-not-generated decisions; loom-b6o was true-positive (docs/ was actually built by scripts/build-docs.sh) but loom-wxo was false-positive (docs/ is hand-written; the bootstrap script just CITES doc paths in messages).

The detector spec already says it 'accepts some false-positives in exchange for simplicity' — but that simplicity-tradeoff was set before customer trials proved how often it fires. With 1-of-2 trials hitting an FP, the heuristic is too loose.

Required fix: tighten Signal 2a to require a write context. Reject matches where:
- Match is inside a shell comment (line starts with optional whitespace + #)
- Match is inside an echo / printf string literal that doesn't contain 'cp', 'mkdir', or '>'
- Match is in a docstring or comment in package.json/pyproject.toml

Accept matches where:
- 'docs/' appears as a target of cp/mkdir/install (left-of- the path) in a shell line
- 'docs/' appears as a Make target (right-side of ':' in Makefile rule)
- 'docs/' appears as a build script target in package.json scripts

Severity: P2 — the detector is the foundation of loom-qp0 + loom-3hb (both P2 fixes). An FP-prone detector pushes the false-positive rate of those skills up. With only 2 trials we already see one FP; future trials will hit more.

Source: drawer drawer_liza_base_decisions_cbab33a93655a34b50ab0390 (liza_base/decisions, friction Signal 2a section). Trial: loom-wxo. Tests: lib/tests/docs-generated.test.sh — should add FP cases for shell comments and echo strings.

Related:
- loom-qp0 (audit-project generated-docs detection — uses this detector)
- loom-3hb (docs-scaffold generated-docs refusal — uses this detector)
- A symmetric P4 follow-up could narrow package.json scope-2c (already noted in Wave 1 close-out drawer)
close_reason: Signal 2a tightened: skip comment-only lines and echo/printf without write operators. 4 new RED→GREEN tests + 1 echo-redirect positive guard. Full lib/tests/ suite (78/78) clean. Merged --no-ff to main as 28338cd.

title: audit-project: drop _→- normalization in wing auto-detect; use filesystem basename verbatim
description:
The audit-project skill's auto-detect normalizes filesystem project names from snake_case to kebab-case (e.g., liza_base → liza-base, tla_puzzles → tla-puzzles) when resolving the MemPalace wing slug. But the MemPalace's de-facto convention is snake_case (loom, hundred_acre_woods, tla_puzzles, liza_base, liza). The normalization invents wing slugs that don't match the actual convention, producing a [WING WARN] every time:

  [WING WARN] auto-detected wing may not be canonical
    resolved:   liza-base (0 drawers, basename auto-detect from liza_base → _→-)
    variants:   liza_base (105 drawers), wing_liza_base_session_assistant (9 drawers)
    suggested:  re-run with --wing liza_base

This WARN fires on EVERY audit of liza_base. Same pattern hit tla-puzzles in loom-b6o (tla-puzzles filesystem, tla_puzzles canonical wing with 197 drawers). The normalization is the bug, not the naming.

Fix: drop the _→- normalization in auto-detect. Use filesystem basename verbatim. The wing-variant detection logic (loom-p1l) still fires correctly when there ARE genuine variants — it just won't fire when filesystem-name == canonical-wing-slug, which is the common case.

Likely location: skills/audit-project/SKILL.md Step 1 (wing resolution) + agents/project-onboarder.md (the actual auto-detect impl).

Follow-up to verify: re-running /audit-project against tla-puzzles after this fix should produce no [WING WARN] (since tla_puzzles wing matches tla_puzzles filesystem). Re-running against liza_base should likewise produce no WARN.

Known edge cases that should still WARN (to preserve loom-p1l behavior):
- filesystem name with REAL non-slug chars (spaces, capitalization that doesn't match) → still WARN with the normalization-applied alternatives
- multiple actually-similar wings (e.g., 'liza_base' filesystem with both 'liza_base' and 'wing_liza_base_session_assistant' wings) → still WARN about the multiplicity, just not invent a third nonexistent slug

Severity: P2 — the WARN appears on every audit; users have to either pass --wing explicitly every time or accept the noise. Trivial fix; high recurring annoyance.

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_cb8647655ba0d62a257c96ef (loom/decisions, wing-naming friction surfaced 2026-05-05). Trial: loom-wxo (and historically loom-b6o).
close_reason: dropped _→- normalization in audit-project wing auto-detect; use filesystem basename verbatim

title: audit-project: AUTOFIX bypassed per-item approval gating in --dangerously-skip-permissions session
description:
First end-to-end exercise of /audit-project --apply-onboarding (Wave 2 / loom-a29) shows the per-item approval contract is broken silently.

Observed in loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-04. The audit transcript shows:

  **Item 1:** Create `.claude/workflow.json` with `{"v":1,"mode":"full"}`. Apply suggested fix? (yes / skip / edit)
  Done — `.claude/workflow.json` written.

The 'Done — written' line appears with no user turn between the prompt and the application. Same pattern for Item 2 (.gitignore append) and Item 3 (.claude/rules/tests.md draft + write).

Possible causes (need investigation):
(a) The session was running with --dangerously-skip-permissions, which bypasses Claude Code's tool-call permission prompts. The audit-project skill may be (incorrectly) treating skipped permission prompts as user-approval, then auto-answering its own 'yes/skip/edit' question.
(b) The skill's per-item-approval flow has a bug where the prompt-and-apply happen in the same model turn, with no genuine pause for user input.
(c) The new --apply-onboarding flag was applied without --apply-onboarding being explicitly set, OR the skill defaults to apply-all when the flag is absent.

Required investigation:
1. Reproduce the bypass in a NON-skip-permissions session — does the per-item gate work there?
2. Check the skill's text for any 'when --dangerously-skip-permissions, treat as auto-yes' logic.
3. Determine whether the user explicitly passed --apply-onboarding (they didn't say so in the loom-side conversation; the parallel session may have inferred it).

Required fix (after diagnosis):
- The per-item contract MUST be respected. AUTOFIX items should still pause for explicit user 'yes' even with --dangerously-skip-permissions (which is about TOOL permissions, not USER approval). The two are different gates.
- If --apply-onboarding was implicitly enabled, that's a separate bug — the flag should be explicit.

Severity: P2 because the new flags are now untrusted until this is fixed. Future trials may apply changes the user didn't sanction.

Source: loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-04. Trial drawer: drawer_loom_decisions_1101f772a4ac7660a0f45aa1 (Wave 2 close-out, where the per-item contract is documented). Adoption drawer in liza_base: drawer_liza_base_decisions_aaad51050ba624209965e671.
close_reason: Closed

title: docs-scaffold + customer-trial protocol: handle generated docs/ (skip-or-warn) and update step 5 conditionality
description:
Two-part fix needed. Discovered during loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial 2026-05-04 when the trial's step 5 ('Run /docs-scaffold') was implicitly skipped because docs/ in tla-puzzles is generated by scripts/build-docs.sh (gitignored, regenerated from README.md on every build). Running scaffold against generated docs/ would write template files that the next build cycle erases — silent breakage.

Part 1 — /docs-scaffold should detect generated docs/:
- Detect via .gitignore entry for docs/, OR docs/* referenced as a build target in any scripts/*.sh, Makefile, package.json scripts, etc.
- On detection, refuse to run with [SCAFFOLD SKIP][GENERATED] message pointing at the build script and explaining why.
- This shares the detection logic from loom-qp0 (audit-project's same problem); should probably be extracted into a shared helper.

Part 2 — customer-trial activity recipe (the loom-b6o pattern documented in loom/decisions km8.6 closing drawer) needs step 5 to be conditional:
- Currently step 5 says 'Run /docs-scaffold — capture friction at M1-M6, with attention to the partial-Diataxis docs/ shape.'
- Should be: 'Run /docs-scaffold UNLESS docs/ is generated (in which case scaffold itself should refuse — capture that as the only friction signal for this step).'
- Update the trial protocol drawer + the activity recipe (when it's written for trial-style customer onboarding).

Severity: P2 — correctness. Trial protocol's universality assumption was wrong; without the conditional, future trials of generated-docs projects either silently break the project or get manually skipped (as happened here).

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_dfb5ece53785610c83ca6619 (loom/decisions, friction signal G). Trial: loom-b6o.

Related:
- loom-qp0 (audit-project generated-docs detection) — same root detection logic; consider implementing the helper once and using it from both skills.
close_reason: Merged in wave-1 close-out batch (commits 3591eb2, d2bd6c4, 85acb1b on main 2026-05-04). All P2 correctness + P3 ergonomics fixes from loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial. 34/34 bats tests green. New shared lib/docs-generated.sh detector used by audit-project + docs-scaffold. project-onboarder.md item-3 manually composed to combine j4r structural fix with cka MISS remediation. Follow-up beads filed: loom-yxn (P4 bd upstream defer-on-mixed-stage), loom-h4u (P4 historical drawer migration). Wave 2 (loom-8hg + loom-a29) and loom-wxo (3rd-customer trial) now unblocked.

title: audit-project: bd-hooks check uses wrong path (core.hooksPath, not .git/hooks/)
description:
The /audit-project skill checks for '.git/hooks/pre-commit' to determine whether bd hooks are installed. False positive: 'bd hooks install' actually writes to '.beads/hooks/' and sets 'git config core.hooksPath=.beads/hooks'. So the audit reports MISS even when hooks ARE installed correctly via the canonical bd path.

Discovered during loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial 2026-05-04. The parallel session ran 'bd hooks install' as one of the audit's recommended onboarding fixes, then verified — at which point it noticed bd's own install layout, and that the audit's check would have continued to report MISS even after install.

Required fix: replace the '.git/hooks/pre-commit existence' check with one of:
(a) Read 'git config core.hooksPath' and check '<that-dir>/pre-commit' exists.
(b) Check both locations: '.git/hooks/pre-commit' OR '.beads/hooks/pre-commit' (and any future bd hook dirs).
(c) Run 'bd doctor' (or whatever bd's self-check is) and parse its hook-install status.

Option (a) is the most general (works for any project setting core.hooksPath, not just bd). Option (c) is the most accurate (delegates to bd's own knowledge). Option (a) is recommended unless bd's self-check is stable + scripted-friendly.

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_598a609fc3e29b3c5937965f (loom/decisions). Trial: loom-b6o.

Severity: P2 — false positive on a check that drives onboarding behavior. Users who already have hooks correctly installed via canonical bd path will be told to install them again, possibly leading to redundant or conflicting setups.
close_reason: Merged in wave-1 close-out batch (commits 3591eb2, d2bd6c4, 85acb1b on main 2026-05-04). All P2 correctness + P3 ergonomics fixes from loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial. 34/34 bats tests green. New shared lib/docs-generated.sh detector used by audit-project + docs-scaffold. project-onboarder.md item-3 manually composed to combine j4r structural fix with cka MISS remediation. Follow-up beads filed: loom-yxn (P4 bd upstream defer-on-mixed-stage), loom-h4u (P4 historical drawer migration). Wave 2 (loom-8hg + loom-a29) and loom-wxo (3rd-customer trial) now unblocked.

title: audit-project: detect generated docs/ (gitignored or built by script) and redirect drift checks to source
description:
The /audit-project skill assumed docs/ was hand-written in tla-puzzles, but docs/ is gitignored (.gitignore:28) and fully generated by scripts/build-docs.sh (line 19: 'cp README.md "$DOCS/index.md"'). Result: skill reported [DOC FIX] cardinality drift in docs/index.md and suggested edits there; the parallel Claude session applied those edits, then discovered they will be overwritten on next build. README.md was the actual source-of-truth.

This is a correctness bug, not ergonomics — in any project that generates docs, the skill points the user at the artifact instead of the source. The fix-suggestion is wrong by design until detection lands.

Required behavior:
1. Detect generated docs/ via either signal:
   (a) docs/ matches a .gitignore entry
   (b) docs/index.md (or other docs/ files) referenced as cp/build target in any scripts/*.sh, Makefile, package.json scripts, etc.
2. On detection, either:
   (a) Skip the docs/ drift check entirely with [DOC SKIP][GENERATED] tag and a note pointing at the build script
   (b) Walk the build chain back to source files and report drift there (advanced — needs build-script parsing)
   (c) Hybrid: skip docs/ drift, but report drift in known root-level docs (README.md, etc.) — this is the related case for loom-ojn (MIRROR scope decision)
3. Minimum-viable fix: option (a) — just skip and warn. Don't report drift in artifact files.

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_598a609fc3e29b3c5937965f (loom/decisions). Discovered during loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial 2026-05-04, when the parallel session caught the generation chain only after applying fixes.

Severity: P2 not P3 because skill is *actively wrong* in this case, not just sub-optimal.

Related:
- loom-ojn (MIRROR tag scope) — overlaps because this project is exactly the case where 'README is source, docs/ mirrors README' makes README-drift checks the right thing to do.
- loom-8hg (--apply-trivial) — this bug would have caused --apply-trivial to silently corrupt the wrong file.
close_reason: Merged in wave-1 close-out batch (commits 3591eb2, d2bd6c4, 85acb1b on main 2026-05-04). All P2 correctness + P3 ergonomics fixes from loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial. 34/34 bats tests green. New shared lib/docs-generated.sh detector used by audit-project + docs-scaffold. project-onboarder.md item-3 manually composed to combine j4r structural fix with cka MISS remediation. Follow-up beads filed: loom-yxn (P4 bd upstream defer-on-mixed-stage), loom-h4u (P4 historical drawer migration). Wave 2 (loom-8hg + loom-a29) and loom-wxo (3rd-customer trial) now unblocked.

title: docs-scaffold: drop templates/diataxis/README.md from apply set (would overwrite project README)
description:
The bash recipe documented in templates/diataxis/README.md is:

```bash
cp -r templates/diataxis/. "$TARGET/"
```

This copies EVERY file under templates/diataxis/ — including the templates' own README.md, which lands at `<target>/README.md` and would **silently overwrite the project's own README.md** (data loss).

The skill SKILL.md M5/M6 spec doesn't explicitly drop this file from the apply set, so a strict implementation following the documented recipe will produce data loss for any target project that has a README.md at root.

HAW trial caught this only because HAW happens to lack a root README.md. A target project WITH a README would have lost it.

Fix:
- skills/docs-scaffold/SKILL.md M5: explicitly exclude templates/diataxis/README.md from the apply set ("the templates' own README is for loom maintainers, not the target project").
- templates/diataxis/README.md substitution-mechanism section: change the recipe to either (a) explicitly omit README.md, or (b) move README.md out of templates/diataxis/ entirely (e.g. templates/diataxis-README.md as a sibling) so cp -r doesn't pick it up.

Either fix works; (b) is more bulletproof since it makes the recipe correct as documented.

Source: loom-km8.6 HAW first-customer trial M5 phase. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_9bebc79fba4116c3a8b2489a (will supplement on close).
acceptance_criteria: docs-scaffold cannot land templates' own README.md into a target project; verified by running scaffold against a project that has its own README.md (it survives unchanged); templates/diataxis/README.md content/location updated so the documented bash recipe is correct as-written
close_reason: Moved templates/diataxis/README.md → templates/diataxis-README.md (out of the templates dir entirely). The documented cp -r recipe is now correct as-written and cannot overwrite a target's own README.md. Updated 4 inbound references (SKILL.md ×3, how-to ×2 — replace_all) and the README's own self-description. lib/tests/diataxis-template.test.sh still passes (9/9, mkdocs build --strict).

title: audit-project: WARN when basename-variant MemPalace wings exist (-/_ collision)
description:
Wing auto-detect picks the basename-derived slug, but a project's canonical wing may use a different separator (e.g. underscore vs hyphen) due to historical naming.

HAW trial: root `/home/frank/Working/hundred-acre-woods` → auto-detected wing `hundred-acre-woods` (3 drawers) → silently scoped drift checks to the smaller wing. The canonical wing has 13 drawers under `hundred_acre_woods`. The project-onboarder check 5 noticed 'two parallel wings present' but framed it PASS (informational), not WARN.

Silent-wrong is worse than loud-missing. The user has no signal that the docs-drift check (Check 5 in particular) is going to miss most of the prior decision history.

Fix: when audit-project resolves the wing AND mempalace_list_wings shows >1 wing whose slug is a basename-variant of the resolved one (`-` ↔ `_`, plural ↔ singular, snake_case ↔ kebab-case), emit a WARN with: 'did you mean --wing <other>? (N drawers) vs <auto> (M drawers)'. Surface this BEFORE running Step 3 docs-drift, so the user can rerun with the right --wing.

Source: loom-km8.6 HAW first-customer trial M1 audit phase. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_f2e3a5c273214cbf335a8918 §F2 (loom/decisions, 2026-05-04).
acceptance_criteria: audit-project Step 1 emits a WARN-level line when basename-variant wings exist with non-trivial drawer counts; the WARN names both wings and their drawer counts; the WARN appears before the project-onboarder dispatch and before Step 3
close_reason: Added Step 1b 'wing-variant warning' to audit-project SKILL.md: when --wing is auto-detected (not explicit), enumerate basename-variant siblings (-/_, plural/singular, snake/kebab) and emit a [WING WARN] BEFORE Step 2 if any sibling has more drawers. Shape: 'resolved <W> (M drawers) | variants <S>(N drawers) | suggested --wing <largest>'. Non-blocking; user owns the call. Combined report now has Pre-flight warnings section.

title: Agent tool with isolation:worktree may write edits to main repo paths instead of worktree
description:
## Symptom

The km8.4 worker subagent (dispatched via `Agent` tool with `isolation: "worktree"`) reported in its return summary that it had edited files at absolute paths under the main repo:

- `/home/frank/repos/loom/skills/audit-project/SKILL.md`
- `/home/frank/repos/loom/commands/audit-project.md`

(no worktree prefix in the agent's reported paths).

After the agent returned, the worktree at `/home/frank/repos/loom/.claude/worktrees/agent-a0e34a73/` showed only a staged `.beads/issues.jsonl` change — no actual edits to the audit-project files. Meanwhile, the **main repo working tree** showed both `commands/audit-project.md` and `skills/audit-project/SKILL.md` modified with the agent's content.

The agent's content was correct and well-formed; only the location was wrong.

## Evidence

- 2026-05-04 session shipping loom-km8.{2,3,4} in parallel
- Decision drawer: `drawer_loom_decisions_9bd804d6326ad49467f4e7fe` (loom/decisions, "LOOM-KM8 PHASE 2 — PARALLEL DISPATCH OF T2/T3/T4")
- KG: `Agent-tool-with-isolation-worktree → may_write_to → main-repo-paths-instead-of-worktree` (2026-05-04)
- km8.2 and km8.3 worker agents (dispatched in the same message, same `isolation: "worktree"` parameter, similar prompts) wrote to their **worktree** paths correctly. The bug was specific to km8.4's run, which suggests it's intermittent rather than systematic.

## Hypotheses to investigate

1. **Agent used cwd-relative paths.** The Edit tool resolves relative paths against the agent's pwd. If the agent's pwd was the main repo (not the worktree) and it called Edit with relative paths like `commands/audit-project.md`, the writes would land in main.
2. **Edit tool path resolution.** Even with absolute paths, something about how worktree paths resolve under `.claude/worktrees/` (which is inside the main repo's directory tree) could route writes to the main repo's working tree instead of the worktree's.
3. **Agent wrote absolute paths but stripped/rewrote them.** The agent's summary contains absolute paths without the worktree prefix — possibly the agent itself never used the worktree paths because its tools didn't surface that it was in a worktree.
4. **Per-tool isolation gap.** `isolation: "worktree"` may set up the git worktree but not change the agent's tool cwd or path-resolution context.

## Difference from working agents (km8.2 + km8.3)

- km8.2 worker created NEW files (no pre-existing main copy to clobber). Its summary explicitly named worktree paths (`/home/frank/repos/loom/.claude/worktrees/agent-ad662d4f/commands/docs-scaffold.md`).
- km8.3 worker EDITED an existing tracked file (`agents/project-onboarder.md`) — same shape as km8.4. Its summary named the worktree path. No leak to main.

So the difference isn't "new file vs. edited file" or "single file vs. multiple files." km8.3 and km8.4 are structurally similar; only one leaked to main.

## Repro plan

1. Dispatch a single agent with `isolation: "worktree"` and a prompt that says "edit `<absolute-path-to-tracked-file-in-main-repo>`" (using the main path explicitly).
2. Compare against: prompt that says "edit `<file>` in your worktree" (worktree-relative or worktree-absolute).
3. Compare against: prompt that names no path at all and lets the agent discover the file via Read.
4. In each case, observe where the Edit lands.

## Acceptance

- Document the actual path-resolution behavior for `Agent` + `isolation: "worktree"` + Edit tool.
- If the bug is in user-facing behavior, file a follow-up to either (a) make worker prompts always use worktree-prefixed absolute paths, or (b) have the harness sandbox the worker's filesystem to its worktree.
- Update the loom dispatching-parallel-agents how-to (if any) with the verified safe pattern.

## Out of scope

- Fixing the underlying tool/harness behavior (this is a Claude Code / Agent SDK issue, not loom-internal). Loom can document a workaround but can't patch the tool.
close_reason: research finding filed: drawer_loom_decisions_df73c725b47dd67832935e3a; CLAUDE.md updated with safe-pattern (relative paths in dispatch prompts, post-dispatch git diff --stat in both trees)

title: bd pre-commit hook re-export defeats loom-22h workaround when subagents work in worktrees
description:
loom-22h closed 2026-05-04 with a defensive workaround: `/issues.jsonl` in .gitignore (root-anchored) + CLAUDE.md 'Don't' note recommending main-path commits over worktree-mode commits. Today's session (loom-9z1.{8,9} parallel dispatch) demonstrated the workaround is insufficient when subagents work in worktrees.

**Evidence (2026-05-04):**

1. **loom-9z1.8 worker** — bd pre-commit hook staged stray `issues.jsonl` at worktree root on the first two commit attempts. Worker bypassed via `git -c core.hooksPath=/dev/null commit`. Final commit 9c80a9c clean. Worker's report explicitly noted loom-22h still active.

2. **loom-9z1.9 worker** — bd pre-commit hook staged stray `issues.jsonl`. Worker tried `git rm --cached issues.jsonl` + `git commit --amend`; the hook re-added the file on amend. Worker did NOT skip hooks (CLAUDE.md forbids `--no-verify` without explicit user approval). Commit c5fa4a2 shipped to branch with the stray file. Dispatcher had to clean at merge: `git merge --no-commit --no-ff` then `git rm --cached issues.jsonl && rm issues.jsonl` before the merge commit (6ee0e62 / linearized as 6f3e0cb).

**Why the existing workaround fails:**

- `.gitignore` doesn't help once the file is tracked. The hook re-stages on every commit, so even after `git rm --cached`, the next commit re-adds it.
- CLAUDE.md 'commit from main' advice is human-targeted prose. Subagents dispatched into worktrees don't reliably internalize it — they see the natural workflow as 'commit where I'm working.'
- The `core.hooksPath=/dev/null` bypass works but is undocumented. One worker discovered it independently; the other didn't and had to ship the broken commit for the dispatcher to fix.

**Root cause (unchanged from loom-22h):** bd's pre-commit hook (`bd hooks run pre-commit` invoked from `.beads/hooks/pre-commit`) exports `issues.jsonl` to the worktree root when commit happens inside a git worktree, instead of (or in addition to) the canonical `.beads/issues.jsonl`. The export message lies about the path it's writing to.

**Proposed fix shapes (to triage during work):**

- **A. Upstream fix in bd** — patch `bd hooks run pre-commit` to resolve the canonical export path via `git rev-parse --git-common-dir` (returns the main repo's .git directory even from a worktree) and write only to `<common-dir>/../.beads/issues.jsonl`. This is the right fix but requires a bd CLI change.
- **B. Loom-side wrapper hook** — replace bd's pre-commit installer with a wrapper that detects worktree mode (`git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree` + path check) and skips the export there entirely, deferring it to the main-repo commit. Cost: divergence from upstream bd hook contract.
- **C. Per-worktree git config disable** — install.sh / worktree-creation helper sets `core.hooksPath=/dev/null` (or a no-op directory) on the worktree's local git config so the hook never fires from worktrees. Cost: hides the hook entirely from worktrees, which may surprise users who do want hook behavior there.
- **D. Recipe-side defense** — bake the `core.hooksPath=/dev/null` bypass into bead-lifecycle-shell phase C (commit step) when `stage=worktree`, with a clear comment explaining the workaround. Cost: every recipe inherits a workaround for a bug that should be fixed at the source. Has the virtue of being immediate and not requiring upstream coordination.

**Default recommendation pending brainstorm:** A is the right long-term fix; D is the right immediate fix because it propagates via the shell to every recipe and unblocks today's dispatch pattern. B and C are intermediate options that may not be worth the complexity if D ships.

**Acceptance:**
- A subagent dispatched into a worktree can commit normally without the bd hook staging stray `issues.jsonl`. Verify by running the loom-9z1.{8,9}-style dispatch pattern after the fix.
- The dispatcher does not need to merge-clean stray `issues.jsonl` from worktree branches.
- Existing CLAUDE.md 'commit from main' note can either stay (belt-and-suspenders) or be removed (if the fix is robust enough to make the advice unnecessary).

**Out of scope:** changing the canonical export path; only the worktree-context re-export bug.

**Recipe shape:** bugfix-a-bead. Depends on: nothing structural — loom-22h is closed and superseded-in-spirit by this bead but not formally.
close_reason: Closed

title: Set up MkDocs Material + GitHub Pages deploy + Diataxis skeleton
description:
Goal: Foundation for the Diataxis docs site. Get the publishing pipeline working end-to-end against placeholder content; subsequent migration beads (A2-A5) fill the quadrants.

Files: mkdocs.yml, requirements.txt (build env), .github/workflows/docs.yml, docs/index.md (4-quadrant landing), docs/{tutorials,how-to,reference,explanation}/index.md (placeholder quadrant landings).

Steps:
1. Author mkdocs.yml with Material theme + mkdocs-include-markdown plugin (glob auto-discovery enabled). Enable search, dark mode, navigation tabs.
2. requirements.txt: mkdocs-material + mkdocs-include-markdown-plugin pinned to current versions.
3. GitHub Actions workflow: build on push to main, deploy to gh-pages branch.
4. Skeleton landing pages: root index.md orients readers by intent (new? → Tutorials / solving a problem? → How-to / looking up a fact? → Reference / want to understand why? → Explanation), each quadrant index.md is a stub TOC.
5. Configure repo Pages settings: source = gh-pages branch, custom domain optional.

Acceptance:
- mkdocs build succeeds locally.
- GH Actions deploys on push to main.
- Published page resolves; the 4-quadrant nav is navigable.
- Stub landing pages render without broken links.

Diataxis-discipline note (from R1 drawer at claim time): ensure index.md orientation copy follows the 4-quadrant intent pattern Daniele Procida recommends (NOT a hierarchical product-feature nav).

Recipe shape: feature-a-bead.
close_reason: MkDocs Material + GH Pages deploy + Diataxis 4-quadrant skeleton landed (commit 4f3cd06; merged 30a5b5c). All four quadrants land with at-least-one real page per R1 C3 (no empty stubs): Tutorials = 2 walkthroughs git-mv'd; How-to = install.md from README; Reference = session-startup auto-included via mkdocs-include-markdown demonstrating C1; Explanation = §1 mental-model lifted from manual.md. mkdocs build --strict exits 0. Unblocks loom-9z1.3-.6 (A2-A5 quadrant migrations), loom-9z1.8 (B1 doc-surface check), loom-9z1.9 (/audit-project --check=docs). manual.md retire deferred to A6 (loom-9z1.7).

title: Diataxis: principles, fail points, loom-specific accommodations (research)
description:
Goal: Establish a locked understanding of Diataxis (the documentation framework) BEFORE any restructure work begins. Every implementation/migration bead in this epic depends on this drawer.

Define the question:
- What does each Diataxis quadrant (Tutorials, How-to, Reference, Explanation) actually require — voice, audience, depth, what to keep OUT?
- Where do real-world Diataxis adoptions fail? (Common patterns to validate: tutorial creep into how-to; reference padding into explanation; four-quadrant nav becoming a navigation trap; primitive-as-reference duplication; getting-started tutorial bloat.)
- What loom-specific accommodations do we need? (Markdown-heavy infra repo where SKILLs are already reference-shaped; canonical-skills with mkdocs-include-markdown glob; a single-author meta-project with no external users yet but designed to be picked up.)

Steps:
1. Search palace + bd memories for prior Diataxis art (probably none).
2. Fetch authoritative external docs: diataxis.fr (canonical site by Daniele Procida); published critiques + case studies; any blog posts about Diataxis fail modes.
3. Synthesize.

Output: decision drawer in loom/decisions naming
(a) discipline rules per quadrant (voice / audience / what to include / what to keep OUT),
(b) documented fail patterns + how to avoid them,
(c) loom-specific accommodations (canonical-skills, glob auto-discovery, single-author dynamics).

Recipe shape: research-a-bead. Drawer is the deliverable.

Blocks: A1, D1 (which transitively blocks the rest of the epic).
close_reason: R1 research output filed in loom/decisions (drawer DIATAXIS DISCIPLINE — R1 RESEARCH OUTPUT). Synthesized 4-quadrant verbatim-Procida discipline rules + 10 fail-pattern catalog (F1-F10) + 6 loom accommodations (C1-C6). Unblocks A1 (loom-9z1.2), A2-A5, B1, C1, D1.

title: Diataxis docs restructure: MkDocs Material + canonical-skills + drift defenses
description:
Rebuild loom's docs as a Diataxis-shaped MkDocs Material site published via GitHub Pages. Skills/commands/agents/hooks remain canonical (single source of truth); docs site curates and links via mkdocs-include-markdown glob auto-discovery. Process integration (recipe phase B1 doc-surface check) + drift detection (/audit-project --check=docs) defend the alignment.

Phase 1 (this epic): loom's own docs.
Phase 2 (D1's plan + future child beads): extract pattern as template loom-managed projects can adopt via /audit-project.

Locked decisions captured in conversation 2026-05-03:
- Scope: Option 3 (loom first, then template-ize)
- Tooling: MkDocs Material + GitHub Pages
- Source-of-truth: option 1 (skills canonical, docs link via include-markdown)
- Process: B1 doc-surface check in bead-lifecycle-shell
- Drift detection: extend /audit-project with --check=docs (system + beads + MemPalace > docs precedence)
- Glob auto-discovery for primitive includes (no nav.yml maintenance)
- 10 beads under one epic

The full design will be filed as a decision drawer at R1's close + supplemented at D1's close. The child beads' descriptions will be enriched with detailed per-page content specs at claim time, sourced from R1's drawer.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Drop disable-model-invocation flag from activity recipe SKILL.mds + shell
description: The six activity recipe SKILL.mds (bugfix/feature/refactor/research/cleanup/docs-a-bead) and bead-lifecycle-shell carry 'disable-model-invocation: true' in frontmatter. This makes the Skill tool refuse to load them ('cannot be used with Skill tool due to disable-model-invocation'), which blocks the loom-1ab router from dispatching to recipes via Skill(<recipe>) — the canonical handoff path. The existing /bugfix-a-bead and /research-a-bead slash commands also say 'Invoke the X skill' and would hit the same wall (untested). FIX: remove 'disable-model-invocation: true' from frontmatter of all 7 SKILL.mds. Recipes are explicitly invoked via slash command (or via the router); the flag was belt-and-suspenders against accidental auto-fire. Descriptions are already narrow and workflow-mode off/light gating provides additional defense. ACCEPTANCE: 7 SKILL.mds edited; Skill(<any-recipe>) loads cleanly; existing /bugfix-a-bead and /research-a-bead slash commands work end-to-end. See loom/decisions drawer 0e40c06 (2026-05-03).
close_reason: Removed disable-model-invocation:true from 6 recipe SKILL.mds + bead-lifecycle-shell. Updated body text + manual.md. Unblocks loom-1ab router. commit c44d7cb.

title: loom: /wrap-up final step — deploy via install.sh when in loom checkout
description: When working on loom, the /wrap-up command should auto-detect the loom checkout (presence of install.sh + settings.snippet.json + skills/ + commands/ at pwd root) and run a deploy step as its final action. The deploy step needs to (a) mkdir any missing ~/.claude/skills/<new>/ parent dirs, (b) rm dangling symlinks owned by loom (target points into $LOOM_ROOT but file no longer exists), then (c) ./install.sh. This compensates for two install.sh gaps documented today in MemPalace drawer 'install.sh — TWO RENAME-DEPLOY GAPS FOUND' (loom/decisions, 2026-05-03). Until install.sh itself absorbs the fix (separate bead, P3), this addendum keeps loom self-deploying after every wrap-up. Edit only commands/wrap-up.md; the loom-detection check guards the step from firing in non-loom projects.
close_reason: Added section 6 to commands/wrap-up.md: detects loom checkout, mkdir's missing skill subdirs, prunes dangling loom-owned symlinks, runs ./install.sh. Section is no-op in non-loom projects. Cross-refs the install.sh gap drawer for lineage. Will collapse to plain ./install.sh once install.sh fix lands (separate bead).

title: v2: router — /working-a-bead becomes selector that dispatches by bead.type
description: commands/working-a-bead.md becomes a router. Logic: takes bead-id (optional --recipe=<name> override), runs bd show, scores against the 6 recipes by bead.type field + description heuristics. Routes to the matching recipe. On ambiguity (bead matches 2+ recipes plausibly): list ambiguous candidates with one-line 'because' for each and prompt user to pick or use --recipe=<name> override (Frank's locked decision 6, 2026-05-02). Default routing: type=bug → bugfix; type=feature → feature; type=epic → planning (TBD); type=task → check description keywords (refactor/cleanup/docs/research) with fallback to bugfix.
close_reason: Router shipped at commands/working-a-bead.md. Dispatches by bead.type with keyword scoring for tasks, ambiguity prompt on ties, --recipe override, epic guard. Skill(<recipe>) dispatch enabled by loom-7z1. End-to-end smoke test deferred to next session. commit da939e6.

title: v2: research-a-bead recipe

No code, no worktree (usually). Captures the discipline that was implicit in the deploy-day MemPalace search habit.

title: v2: feature-a-bead recipe
description: skills/feature-a-bead/SKILL.md. Activity-specific middle: brainstorm (superpowers/beadpowers) → writing-plans (multi-task) → TDD on NEW behavior (the contract) → integration test → bug-class equivalent for negative cases. Differs from bugfix: no symptom to reproduce; the RED test pins desired behavior, not failure. References the shared bead-lifecycle-shell for claim/isolate/verify/close/capture.

title: v2: refactor-a-bead recipe
description: skills/refactor-a-bead/SKILL.md. Activity-specific middle: identify scope → write characterization tests if missing (pin current behavior) → refactor → verify behavior preserved (all original tests pass) → close. Differs from bugfix: TDD inverted — tests come BEFORE the refactor and must KEEP passing. No new behavior; structural change only.

title: MCP long-tail tools (tunnels, hallways, tags, deletion, admin) — deferred
description:
Goal: everything in the existing mempalace_* tool surface NOT covered by loom-40ec.4.1-.4.4's MVP scope -- deferred because it wasn't exercised in the session that drove this epic's scoping, and the highest-value paths (search, drawer CRUD, KG, diary, status) matter more for an initial cutover. Covers: mempalace_create_tunnel/find_tunnels/follow_tunnels/traverse (cross-wing lineage), mempalace_tag_drawer (blocked upstream anyway per loom-yn38/loom-4w9g -- MemPalace's own tag_drawer has a known bug), mempalace_list_rooms/list_wings/list_hallways/list_tunnels, mempalace_check_duplicate, mempalace_delete_drawer/delete_by_source/delete_hallway/delete_tunnel (destructive -- needs careful test coverage before shipping), mempalace_sync/checkpoint/reconnect (concurrency/session-lifecycle tools whose Dolt-native equivalents may differ significantly from MemPalace's chroma-backed originals -- may need their own design pass, not a mechanical port), mempalace_get_aaak_spec/get_taxonomy (static reference data, low complexity), mempalace_memories_filed_away, mempalace_hook_settings.

Do NOT scope mempalace_mine into this list -- that tool's auto-mine pattern was the literal root cause of MemPalace's untenability (runaway auto-mine hook -> single-writer lockout, documented in the now-closed loom-p01j). The new architecture's write path is explicit add_drawer/kg_add calls (from drawer-author/kg-relationship-extractor subagents), not an automatic background miner. If an equivalent capability is wanted later, it needs its own design decision, not a mechanical port of the tool that broke MemPalace.

This bead is intentionally coarse -- split it further into per-tool-group children at claim time once .4.1-.4.4 have landed and the actual MCP server framework/conventions are established.

Files: TBD at claim time
AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE: design (needs its own scoping pass, deliberately deferred)

title: [DEFERRED] Generic managed-project docs<->source manifest drift gate
description:
AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE: design — needs a real managed project's manifest (endpoints/config/CLI) to design against; do not build speculatively.

Goal (DEFERRED — do not build until pulled): a reusable docs<->source drift gate that loom-MANAGED downstream projects inherit, generalizing loom-docs-catalogue/loom-docs-gen beyond loom's own skills/commands/agents/hooks structure. A managed project would declare 'doc surface X enumerates manifest Y' (reference table <-> API endpoints / config keys / CLI flags) and get a gated drift check, installed by docs-scaffold/loom-adopt and run by /audit-project --check=docs.

WHY DEFERRED: downstream projects lack loom's tidy globbable primitive structure; manifest extraction is project-specific. A generic abstraction now is speculative (YAGNI) — wait for a real managed project whose reference docs drift, then design against its ACTUAL manifest.
Lineage: generalizes loom-wjuo (loom-docs-catalogue) + loom-itph (loom-docs-gen) to managed projects; the loom-side dogfood is the Check-4-delegation sibling.
close_reason: Closed not-planned (was deferred). Speculative future capability — captured as a PARKED IDEA (drawer 183c7348 + bd memory parked-idea-generic-manifest-gate) rather than left as a zombie deferred bead. Refile fresh when a real managed project's reference docs actually drift.

Scan surface: /audit-project --check=docs Checks 1-5 (skills/audit-project/SKILL.md), the B1 doc-surface audit (loom-9z1.8, bead-lifecycle-shell), bd doctor conventions, and any 'nudge' hooks that detect-but-don't-enforce a CORRECTNESS invariant — carefully distinguishing INTENTIONAL nudge-not-block (loom-yb5, attended decisions) from accidental advisory-only on a correctness invariant.
Grounding: loom-wj26 child gate-don't-advise principle.
Acceptance: a findings drawer in loom/decisions listing each check + gated/advisory verdict + recommended action; follow-up beads filed for the gate-worthy ones.

title: shellcheck dev-prereq: constitution lint can't run when shellcheck absent (install + document + fail-loud verify)
description: shellcheck (the constitution lint: 'shellcheck hooks/*.sh lib/*.sh scripts/*') is NOT installed in this environment, so the lint gate silently couldn't run during the skxj+wzcn wrap-up (fell back to bash -n; both workers + central hit it). Parts: (1) DOCUMENT shellcheck as a required dev prerequisite (README dev-setup + a note in .claude/project-constitution.md prose) so the lint gate is reproducible. (2) VERIFY the lint path FAILS LOUD (non-zero, no false-green) when shellcheck is absent — loom_resolve_command runs the resolved command + propagates its exit code, so a missing-binary 'command not found' should surface non-zero already; confirm + add a guard/test if it can silently pass. (3) Install shellcheck (no-sudo static binary ok) + run the constitution lint across loom's shell; fix quick findings or file a follow-up if a large cleanup. Files: README.md, .claude/project-constitution.md, lib/tests/ (lint-fail-loud test if needed). Grounding: surfaced in the skxj+wzcn wrap-up; wave drawer 3cea427.
close_reason: shellcheck lint gate made real+passable: .shellcheckrc (disable SC1091 dynamic-source noise) + constitution canonical_commands.lint gated at --severity=warning; fixed all 11 warnings (2 inspection-flagged ones were harmless — dead-equivalent case patterns + intentional sentinel string, not bugs); documented shellcheck as a no-sudo dev prereq (README + constitution); fail-loud test (lib/tests/lint-fail-loud.test.sh 2/2 — non-zero when shellcheck absent). Gate now rc=0 clean, suite 98 ALL GREEN. Merged 91bb417.

title: Audit loom grep-based detectors for substring over-match (anchor literal-token matches)
description: Two substring-over-match detector false-positives surfaced the SAME DAY (2026-06-10): loom-2y6x (AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE substring would self-exclude the meta-bead → fixed to anchored '^[[:space:]]*AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE:') and loom-skxj (resolver-invariant RUNNER_RE matched bare 'pytest' inside the 'pytest-of-*' temp-dir glob → fixed to 'pytest([^-]|$)'). Same hazard: a grep-based detector matching a literal token that ALSO appears in non-invocation contexts (path globs, prose, meta-references). Audit the REMAINING loom grep-based detectors and anchor/contextualize any that over-match. Candidates: hooks/cwd-drift-guard.sh (refuses 'git merge'/'git push'/'bd close'/'bd update' — DOES IT over-match 'git merge-base' (used in the smoke battery!) or 'bd update-something'? HIGH-PRIORITY CHECK), hooks/constitution-enforce.sh (forbidden/bypass_patterns), scripts/loom-audit-resolve, hooks/bd-close-capture.sh (bead-ID regex), lib/tests/resolver-invariant.test.sh RUNNER_RE (other tokens: 'go test' vs 'go test-x', 'npm run', 'make test' — same -suffix looseness as pytest?). For each: confirm anchored to a real invocation/marker position OR fix; add a NEGATIVE test pinning the non-invocation context it must NOT fire on. Files: hooks/cwd-drift-guard.sh, hooks/constitution-enforce.sh, scripts/loom-audit-resolve, lib/tests/ (audit surfaces exact call-sites). RED: INVARIANT: no loom grep-based detector fires on a literal token appearing in a non-invocation context; each audited detector carries a negative test pinning the false-positive it must NOT fire on. Grounding: loom-2y6x + loom-skxj fixes; KG recurring_hazard; wave drawer 3cea427.
close_reason: Audited loom grep-based detectors for substring over-match — ALL already-safe: cwd-drift-guard anchors merge(\s|$) (git merge-base correctly NOT refused — the suspected bug was safe), constitution-enforce + bd-close-capture use shlex argv-token matching, loom-audit-resolve is not a detector. The 2y6x+skxj over-matches were the only two (both already fixed). No production change; added 6 mutation-verified regression-pin negative tests to cwd-drift-guard.test.sh (43/43). RUNNER_RE needed no further tightening. Merged 041e12b.

title: Wire OPTIONAL ast-grep into refactor-a-bead + cleanup-a-bead middles (detected-not-required; grep/sed fallback)
description:
Per loom-2pg research verdict (drawer below): adopt ast-grep as an OPTIONAL structural query/rewrite substrate for the refactor + cleanup recipe variable-middles, scoped to loom-MANAGED downstream polyglot projects (Go/Python/TS). NOT a hard dep; loom does NOT install it (machine-global Rust binary, user's choice); degrade gracefully.

Mechanism: recipes detect 'command -v ast-grep' (NEVER 'sg' — collides with /usr/bin/sg setgroups on Linux, confirmed live 2026-06-10). If present: recommend 'ast-grep run -p <pattern> -r <rewrite>' for AST-safe structural rewrite (refactor 'restructure' step) and structural usage-finding (cleanup 'orphan-reference hunt' step) — far safer than sed/grep (won't match in strings/comments, respects syntax). If absent: fall back to grep/sed (current behavior) + a one-line nudge that ast-grep would make it safer. NOT for loom's own bash/markdown code (marginal value).

Optional first step before wiring: a small empirical pilot (install ast-grep, run one real structural rewrite on a downstream repo) to confirm fit. Integration point: a loom-managed project's project-constitution.md could declare ast-grep as an available tool, and the recipes read that.

Files: skills/refactor-a-bead/SKILL.md, skills/cleanup-a-bead/SKILL.md
Grounding: loom-2pg verdict drawer (loom/decisions). Comparison: beats comby (no complex queries) + semgrep (security-focused, slow); AST-safe unlike sed.
AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE: design — needs a pilot/empirical confirm + recipe-middle wording design before autonomous build.

title: scripts/loom-fanout-detect should honor AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE markers
description:
Confirmed 2026-06-10: loom-fanout-detect is purely mechanical (Files:-disjoint + no dep-edge) and does NOT parse the AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE marker in bead descriptions. So it keeps proposing beads that are upstream/attended (n2b9), needs-decision (skxj), or design (wzcn/2pg) — central must hand-filter every wave (the documented 'manual curation fallback'). The detector should read each candidate's description and EXCLUDE any bead carrying a LEADING 'AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE:' directive line from proposed waves, so its output is directly dispatchable without re-curation. Keep the existing conservative no-Files:-line exclusion.

DESIGN LOCKED 2026-06-10 (attended, w/ user): match the ANCHORED line form (grep -iE '^[[:space:]]*AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE:'), mirroring the existing Files:/RED: matchers in the same script — NOT a bare substring grep. A substring match would self-exclude this very bead (2y6x mentions 'AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE' in prose) and any future bead discussing the marker. Use 2y6x itself as the negative test fixture. ALSO: document the (currently emergent/undocumented) AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE convention in CLAUDE.md alongside Files:/RED:.

Files: scripts/loom-fanout-detect, lib/tests/loom-fanout-detect.test.sh, CLAUDE.md
RED: INVARIANT: a ready bead whose description carries a LEADING 'AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE:' directive line is excluded from every wave loom-fanout-detect proposes; a bead that only MENTIONS the string mid-prose (e.g. loom-2y6x itself) is NOT excluded.
close_reason: loom-fanout-detect honors AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE via anchored-line match (^\s*AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE:), not substring (avoids self-exclusion footgun); 2y6x itself is the negative dogfood fixture. Convention documented in CLAUDE.md. Live: detector no longer proposes the n2b9/skxj false-positive. Merged. Suite ALL GREEN.

title: SessionStart pytest-tempdir prune (./tmp/pytest-of-* >24h) — NEEDS user decision on policy
description:
Split from loom-z3m.17 part-2 (lineage: liza f12, sid=d1a14835 — 'the pytests are eating up a HUGE amount of drive space, look at ./tmp/pytest-of-frank ... cleaned up regularly'). A SessionStart housekeeping hook that prunes ./tmp/pytest-of-* older than 24h, opt-out via LOOM_PYTEST_TEMPDIR_PRUNE_SKIP=1.

NEEDS-DECISION (why not landed unattended): this hook DELETES files on the user's machine — a destructive, machine-global behavior that should not ship autonomously overnight. Open policy questions for an attended session: (a) age threshold (24h?), (b) which dirs (only ./tmp/pytest-of-*? ~/tmp? cwd-relative?), (c) opt-out default, (d) alternative — audit-project drops 'pytest --basetemp=$(mktemp -d)' into scripts/test templates so tmp lands in auto-cleaned locations (PREVENTION over PRUNING, arguably cleaner). Decide prune-vs-prevent before implementing.

Files: hooks/pytest-tempdir-prune.sh (or extend an existing SessionStart hook), install.sh, lib/tests/
AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE: needs-decision — file-DELETING machine-global hook; user must choose prune-vs-prevent policy + age threshold + opt-out before implementing. Not autonomously dispatchable.
close_reason: BOTH prevent+prune: SessionStart hook hooks/pytest-tempdir-prune.sh prunes ./tmp/pytest-of-* >24h (project-scoped, -maxdepth 1, opt-out LOOM_PYTEST_TEMPDIR_PRUNE_SKIP=1, exit-0-always) + retention-config prevent doc (tmp_path_retention_count=1/policy=failed). Wired via settings.snippet.json (not install.sh). 13/13 test; suite 97-file ALL GREEN. Follow-up: tightened resolver-invariant detector (pytest-of-* glob false-positive). Merged b02ca8b.

title: Guard loom's PURELY-bd-dependent hooks with 'command -v bd || exit 0' (fail-open when bd absent)
description:
Split from loom-z3m.17 part-1 (lineage: liza f11, sid=3fe1ed50 — 'staleness sensor tries to call bd in the apartment session; bd isn't there. Should be guarded'). The offending staleness-sensor is the BD-PLUGIN's hook (non-loom — loom cannot edit it). The loom-side generalization: loom hooks that call bd should fail-open when bd is absent.

CAUTION (investigation finding 2026-06-10): a NAIVE top-of-file 'command -v bd || exit 0' sweep is UNSAFE. Several loom hooks call bd but ALSO do non-bd work — top-guarding them would disable that work when bd is absent:
  - pre-push-mkdocs-strict.sh / bd-preflight-docs-strict.sh — run mkdocs --strict (would be skipped)
  - edit-after-failure-guard.sh — the TDD RED-before-source guard (would be disabled)
  - cwd-drift-guard.sh — guards GIT merge/push too, not just bd (would be disabled)
ONLY purely-bd-dependent hooks are safe top-guard candidates: bd-prime-wrapper.sh, post-rewrite.sh, bd-worktree-preseed.sh, git-push-bd-sync.sh, bd-close-capture.sh, bd-remember-guest-guard.sh — and even these need per-hook confirmation (some fire only on bd PreToolUse matchers, so they never fire when bd is absent → guard is harmless-but-unnecessary). install.sh template should ship the guard for the purely-bd subset only.

Files: hooks/bd-prime-wrapper.sh, hooks/post-rewrite.sh, hooks/bd-worktree-preseed.sh, hooks/git-push-bd-sync.sh, install.sh, lib/tests/bd-hook-guard.test.sh
RED: INVARIANT: a purely-bd-dependent loom hook exits 0 (no-op) when bd is not on PATH, WITHOUT disabling any non-bd hook's protection.
close_reason: Fail-open 'command -v bd || exit 0' guard on 4 purely-bd hooks (bd-prime-wrapper/post-rewrite/bd-worktree-preseed/git-push-bd-sync) + guard-rail test proving non-bd hooks (cwd-drift/edit-after-failure) stay protected. install.sh symlinks → guard rides along. Merged 562eac9. Suite ALL GREEN.

title: loom-mine-history: pass salience prompt via stdin tempfile, not argv (ARG_MAX 'Argument list too long' on huge commits)
description:
Surfaced by the loom-rnxp e2e mine (2026-06-10): the salience call 'claude -p "$prompt"' passes the full prompt as a COMMAND-LINE ARGUMENT. For a very large commit body/diff the prompt exceeds the OS ARG_MAX, so exec fails: 'claude: Argument list too long' (lib/loom-mine-history.sh:253). That unit yields no draft. Observed 1/786 in the e2e mine — GRACEFUL (abort-loud tolerated the <25% fraction, run completed with 64 drafts), so P3 not P1.

FIX: write $prompt to a temp file and invoke 'claude -p < "$promptfile"' (prompt via stdin, no argv). NOTE the interaction with loom-t80c: this REPLACES the '</dev/null' stdin on the salience call — the dedicated prompt file becomes the isolated stdin, which STILL prevents the survivors-fd leak (stdin is the prompt file, not the loop's $survivors fd). The t80c regression test (loom-mine-history-stdin-isolation) must be updated to assert stdin is the prompt file (not the survivors stream) rather than asserting literal '</dev/null'. Verify: a unit whose prompt exceeds ARG_MAX is processed without 'Argument list too long', AND the survivors stream still does not leak.

Lineage: loom-t80c (the stdin fix this supersedes/interacts with), loom-rnxp (surfacing mine). Files: lib/loom-mine-history.sh, lib/tests/loom-mine-history-stdin-isolation.test.sh
RED: INVARIANT: a salience unit whose prompt exceeds ARG_MAX is still processed (no 'Argument list too long'); the survivors stream still does not leak into claude stdin.
close_reason: ARG_MAX fix: salience prompt via stdin tempfile (claude -p <promptfile); preserves t80c survivors-fd leak-guard. Merged b363314. Follow-up: fixed sibling loom-mine-history.test.sh (argv→stdin capture). Suite ALL GREEN.

title: Harden loom-mine-history vs transient API bursts: probe+backoff instead of hard abort at the 4-unit floor
description:
Evidence (loom-rnxp run, 2026-06-09 night): the full mine aborted 4/4 at the START of the LLM loop (abort-loud, ug4p) — yet minutes later the SAME newest units passed 11/11 manual + 10/10 capped real-engine + 8 drafts. So it was a TRANSIENT throttle/overload burst, not low salience and not a content/engine bug. The decisive variable: the failed run was BACKGROUNDED while central made concurrent main-session turns; the successful capped run ran FOREGROUND with central idle (matches the prior 'claude-p throttle under concurrent main-session load' diagnosis).

GAP: abort-loud's floor (_LMH_FAIL_MIN_PROCESSED=4, _LMH_FAIL_THRESHOLD_PCT=25) is correct for a SUSTAINED throttle but brittle at the START — a transient 4-unit burst in the first 4 units trips the gate and discards the whole harvest. Per the 529 playbook (.claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md Parts A-D, loom-417): before a hard abort, HEALTH-PROBE + EXPONENTIAL BACKOFF (30s->1m->2m->4m, capped) and resume only after a clean probe. The current per-unit retry (_LMH_LLM_RETRIES=3, backoff 1-2-4s) is too short to ride out a sustained burst.

Proposed (needs design): (a) on hitting the abort threshold, PAUSE+health-probe+backoff rather than abort-and-exit; resume the SAME loop after a clean probe. (b) optionally a 'warm-up grace' so a start-burst doesn't trip the gate before enough successes accumulate. (c) document the operational rule: run the mine with ZERO concurrent main-session load (foreground-blocking, or backgrounded with central fully silent).

Lineage: loom-ug4p (abort-loud), loom-417 (529 resilience playbook, doc-only), loom-rnxp (this run's evidence). Files: lib/loom-mine-history.sh, lib/tests/loom-mine-history-abort.test.sh
RED: INVARIANT: given a claude stub that FAILS the first 4 calls then succeeds, the salience pass rides out the burst (probe+backoff) and completes with drafts, rather than aborting non-zero at the 4-unit floor.
AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE: needs-design — backoff/probe policy + warm-up-grace choice
close_reason: probe+backoff+warm-up: salience loop rides out transient API bursts via pause+_lmh_health_probe+exp-backoff (_LMH_BACKOFF_SCHEDULE default 30/60/120/240, env-injectable) + warm-up grace (_LMH_WARMUP_UNITS=8, mid-loop only); forgives n_failed on clean probe; sustained outage STILL aborts non-zero (post-loop gate warm-up-independent → loom-ug4p false-green guard preserved). RED→GREEN 6/6 (new lib/tests/loom-mine-history-abort.test.sh); suite ALL GREEN. Merged 6c3fea0.

title: bd preflight: render the project's checklist from its constitution (general per-runtime fix, supersedes 2wms)
description:
Goal: bd preflight prints bd's DEFAULT Go-project PR checklist (go test -tags / golangci-lint / gofmt / vendorHash / version.go-vs-default.nix) for ANY project, regardless of runtime. Wrong for bash/python/ts/etc. Generalize: bd preflight (or a loom-side preflight wrapper) should read the project-constitution.md (language.runtime + canonical_commands.{test,lint,build,gen}) and render THAT project's real PR-readiness checklist.

SUPERSEDES loom-2wms (which only fixed loom's OWN preflight to shell-suite+mkdocs-strict). This is the general per-runtime fix Frank chose 2026-06-09 because his greenfield project (and every non-Go loom-managed project) hits the same wrong-template gap.

Steps: 1. Investigate where bd preflight sources its checklist (bd config templates / .beads/ / hardcoded in bd binary). 2. If bd supports a per-project template config -> loom sets it from the constitution (audit-project --check=preflight, or an install-time step). 3. If bd hardcodes it -> a loom-side `preflight` wrapper that reads project-constitution.md canonical_commands and prints the checklist; document; possibly file upstream to bd. 4. Loom dogfood = shell suite + mkdocs --strict (the old 2wms target) falls out as the loom-case of the general mechanism.

Acceptance: running preflight in a loom-managed project prints that project's actual gates (from its constitution), not bd's Go template. Loom's own case = shell suite + mkdocs --strict.

Files: (investigate first — bd config vs a new scripts/loom-preflight wrapper + hooks/ + audit-project). Relates-to: loom-6f8 (constitution epic — the per-runtime source of truth). Supersedes loom-2wms.
close_reason: scripts/loom-preflight renders the PR checklist from .claude/project-constitution.md canonical_commands+runtime (yq-free, reuses _loom_sr_canonical_command), superseding bd's hardcoded Go template. Supersedes loom-2wms (loom's bash case falls out of the general mechanism). 13/13 test, suite 94/94. Merge 86c0433. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_795e410605b45025086bfc91.

title: Upstream mempalace: add append param to update_drawer (kill drawer-rewrite capture-tax)
description:
Goal: Kill the drawer-rewrite capture-tax at its ROOT by adding an append capability to mempalace's update_drawer, so an agent sends only the DELTA instead of re-emitting the full ~3KB drawer body on every precipitation. The capture-tax is the AGENT-CONTEXT cost; only a server-side read-modify-write removes it (loom can't fix from its own side — MCP tools are not callable from bash, and the agent is the only update_drawer caller, so any loom-side helper still round-trips full content through the agent).

This is the REAL fix for loom-u9es; its loom-side sibling (u9es) is the INTERIM prose nudge. Decided 2026-06-09 brainstorm: mempalace is external/slow/uncertain -> file upstream; the loom interim covers the gap.

API form (decided): an `append: bool = False` param on tool_update_drawer(drawer_id, content, append, wing, room). When append=True: new_doc = old_doc + content. The server ALREADY fetches old_doc (mempalace/mcp_server.py:749/754), so the server change is ~3 lines + the MCP_TOOLS schema entry (mcp_server.py:1427). Propose the NEED + this minimal form in the issue; defer the final API to the maintainer (they may prefer a separate mempalace_append_to_drawer tool, or a richer section-patch that also flips STATE HEADER fields — note both as alternatives, do not insist).

Lane: issue+PR (full upstream-a-bead recipe). Target: github.com/MemPalace/mempalace (local clone ~/repos/mempalace @ 29bc868, PR #887 merged). RED: extend tests/test_mcp_server.py update_drawer cases with an append-mode case asserting result doc == old_doc + content. GREEN: ~3-line change in tool_update_drawer + schema entry. Draft issue+PR and pass the upstream-a-bead M6 review gate BEFORE any GitHub write. Privacy: code-only change, no private palace data — low redaction risk, but still run M6.

Files: ~/repos/mempalace/mempalace/mcp_server.py, ~/repos/mempalace/tests/test_mcp_server.py (upstream repo; no loom-side source change)
Relates-to: loom-u9es (interim sibling, loom-side). Precedent: loom-98x (sibling-plugin friction -> upstream-pending pattern). Origin: /explore dogfood loom-ld1q.5 F-FRICTION-1.
AUTOFAN-EXCLUDE: upstream+attended — mempalace append-param is an upstream-a-bead contribution (privacy redaction + human review gate); Files: live in the upstream mempalace repo, not loom. Not autonomously dispatchable.
close_reason: Filed upstream MemPalace/mempalace via upstream-a-bead --issue+pr: issue #1761 + PR #1762 (append:bool=False on tool_update_drawer — old_doc+sanitized(content) when True; default replace, back-compat). RED test_update_drawer_append → GREEN; full suite 889 passed, ruff clean; RED-before-GREEN lineage on branch loom-n2b9 off develop, pushed to fork. Watch-bead loom-7q45 tracks merge/rejection. Loom-side: NO code (inline-central — mempalace is an external clone where a dispatched worker's edit-guard would block). Real fix for the capture-tax; interim sibling loom-u9es already shipped.

title: Guard loom's own mkdocs.yml against drifting behind the diataxis template's markdown_extensions
description:
loom's own mkdocs.yml silently lagged its own diataxis template for ~2 weeks: the template gained pymdownx.emoji (loom-ad1, 2026-05-26, commit cda3730) but loom's own mkdocs.yml never synced it, so the live front page rendered grid-card icons as literal text (the sibling fix bead) UNDETECTED. loom doesn't dogfood its own template's mkdocs markdown_extensions.

For loom itself the test asserts loom/mkdocs.yml ⊇ template extensions.
